 `./internal/engine/engine.go`

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	}
46	
47	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
48	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
49	type TranslateResult struct {
50		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
51		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
52		Target         string   `json:"target"`
53		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
54		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
55		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
56	
57		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
58		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
59		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
60	
61		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
62		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
63		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
64		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
65		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
66	
67		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
68		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
69		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
70	
71		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
72		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
73		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
74		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
75		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
76		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
77	
78		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
79		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
80		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
81		// Transient: not serialized.
82		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
83	}
84	
85	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
86	type DictEntry struct {
87		Word      string    `json:"word"`
88		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
89		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
90		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
91	}
92	
93	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
94	type Meaning struct {
95		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
96		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
97		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
98		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
99	}
100	
101	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
102	type Definition struct {
103		Text    string `json:"definition"`
104		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
105	}
106	
107	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
108	type ChunkKind int
109	
110	const (
111		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
112		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
113		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
114		ChunkDone
115		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
116		ChunkError
117	)
118	
119	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
120	type Chunk struct {
121		Kind   ChunkKind
122		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
123		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
124		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
125	}
126	
127	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
128	//
129	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
130	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
131	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
132	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
133	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
134	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
135	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
136	type Engine interface {
137		Name() string
138		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
139		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
140		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
141		Supports(m Mode) bool
142	}
143	
144	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
145	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
146	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
147		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
148		if err != nil {
149			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
150		} else {
151			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
152		}
153		close(ch)
154		return ch
155	}
156	
157	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
158	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
159	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
160	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
161		for c := range ch {
162			switch c.Kind {
163			case ChunkToken:
164				if onToken != nil {
165					onToken(c.Text)
166				}
167			case ChunkDone:
168				return c.Result, nil
169			case ChunkError:
170				return nil, c.Err
171			}
172		}
173		return nil, ErrNoResult
174	}
175
```

 `./internal/engine/smartdict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
10	)
11	
12	// SmartDictConfig parameterizes the smart-dict engine.
13	type SmartDictConfig struct {
14		// CloseDistance is the English edit distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
15		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and returned as-is; a farther match (and
16		// any hard miss) triggers the LLM fallback. 0 disables the typo shortcut, so
17		// every non-exact lookup falls back.
18		CloseDistance int
19		// Preset is the LLM prompt style used for the fallback; "" => PresetDictionary
20		// (a gloss plus example sentences).
21		Preset string
22	}
23	
24	// SmartDictEngine composes a dictionary engine with an LLM: it answers from the
25	// dictionary when it can, and falls back to the LLM when the word is missing or the
26	// fuzzy match is too far off. It is a distinct, selectable engine — the plain
27	// dictionary engine is left untouched.
28	//
29	// This is the first engine to branch on a produced result's quality (the Chain
30	// only fails over on hard errors), so the fallback always stamps a Warning: the
31	// downgrade from an offline lookup to an LLM answer is never silent.
32	type SmartDictEngine struct {
33		dict Engine
34		llm  Engine
35		cfg  SmartDictConfig
36	}
37	
38	// NewSmartDict builds a smart-dict engine over a dictionary and an LLM backend.
39	func NewSmartDict(dict, llm Engine, cfg SmartDictConfig) *SmartDictEngine {
40		return &SmartDictEngine{dict: dict, llm: llm, cfg: cfg}
41	}
42	
43	// Name returns "smart-dict".
44	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Name() string { return "smart-dict" }
45	
46	// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups.
47	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
48	
49	// Detect is not meaningful for a dictionary engine.
50	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
51		return "", nil
52	}
53	
54	// Available reports true when either backend can serve (a dictionary hit, or the
55	// LLM fallback).
56	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
57		return e.dict.Available(ctx) || e.llm.Available(ctx)
58	}
59	
60	// Translate looks the word up in the dictionary and, on a miss or a too-weak fuzzy
61	// match, falls back to the LLM (streaming preserved).
62	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
63		dictCh, err := e.dict.Translate(ctx, req)
64		if err != nil {
65			// A setup failure (e.g. empty input) — nothing to fall back to.
66			return nil, err
67		}
68	
69		out := make(chan Chunk, 32)
70		go func() {
71			defer close(out)
72			// The dictionary engines are single-shot (no token stream), so drain fully
73			// before deciding whether the answer is good enough.
74			res, derr := Drain(dictCh, nil)
75			if e.dictAnswered(res, derr) {
76				debug.Logf("smart-dict: %q answered from dictionary", strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
77				out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
78				return
79			}
80			debug.Logf("smart-dict: %q → LLM fallback (no usable dictionary entry)", strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
81			e.fallback(ctx, req, derr, out)
82		}()
83		return out, nil
84	}
85	
86	// dictAnswered reports whether the dictionary produced a result good enough to
87	// return without asking the LLM: an exact entry, or a fuzzy suggestion close enough
88	// to be a likely typo (within CloseDistance).
89	func (e *SmartDictEngine) dictAnswered(res *TranslateResult, derr error) bool {
90		if derr != nil || res == nil {
91			return false // hard miss (ErrNoDictEntry) or transport error → fall back
92		}
93		if res.Dictionary != nil {
94			return true // exact hit
95		}
96		// A close typo (distance known and within threshold) stays as "did you mean".
97		// Unknown distance (0, e.g. Chinese prefix matches) counts as too far → fall back.
98		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 && res.SuggestDistance != 0 && res.SuggestDistance <= e.cfg.CloseDistance {
99			return true
100		}
101		return false // suggestions too far, "not installed" notes, or empty → fall back
102	}
103	
104	// fallback runs the LLM in translate mode and pipes its chunks to out, stamping a
105	// warning on the terminal result so the downgrade is visible.
106	func (e *SmartDictEngine) fallback(ctx context.Context, req Request, derr error, out chan<- Chunk) {
107		word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
108	
109		r := req
110		r.Mode = ModeTranslate
111		r.Preset = e.cfg.Preset
112		if r.Preset == "" {
113			r.Preset = PresetDictionary
114		}
115		r.Target = smartTarget(req.Text, req.Target)
116	
117		llmCh, err := e.llm.Translate(ctx, r)
118		if err != nil {
119			out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("smart-dict: no dictionary entry for %q and LLM fallback failed: %w", word, err)}
120			return
121		}
122		for ch := range llmCh {
123			if ch.Kind == ChunkDone && ch.Result != nil {
124				ch.Result.Warnings = append(ch.Result.Warnings,
125					fmt.Sprintf("no dictionary entry for %q — defined via %s (LLM)", word, ch.Result.Engine))
126			}
127			out <- ch
128		}
129	}
130	
131	// smartTarget picks the LLM fallback's target language, mirroring the bilingual
132	// dictionary (Chinese ↔ English): Chinese input → English; English/other input →
133	// the caller's target, or Chinese when that target is English/unset.
134	func smartTarget(text, target string) string {
135		if lang.IsChinese(text) {
136			return "en"
137		}
138		t := strings.TrimSpace(target)
139		if t == "" || t == "auto" || strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(t), "en") {
140			return "zh"
141		}
142		return t
143	}
144
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/smartauto.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
10	)
11	
12	// SmartAutoEngine is the "smart" default: it treats a single word/term as a
13	// dictionary lookup (delegating to the smart-dict engine, which itself falls back
14	// to the LLM on a miss) and a phrase or sentence as an LLM translation. It is
15	// bidirectional-friendly — the caller sets Pair/PairHome/PairAway and the routed
16	// Target, and both branches honor them.
17	//
18	// Because dictionary word lookups are direction-agnostic (script routes them to
19	// CC-CEDICT vs ECDICT) and phrase translation uses the pair-aware LLM prompt, a
20	// single word like "test" is answered from the dictionary instead of risking an
21	// LLM echo, and a full sentence is translated into the other pair language.
22	type SmartAutoEngine struct {
23		smart Engine // dictionary + LLM fallback (serves ModeDict)
24		llm   Engine // LLM translate, possibly a fallback chain (serves ModeTranslate)
25	}
26	
27	// NewSmartAuto builds a smart-auto engine over a smart-dict engine (word lookups)
28	// and an LLM/translate engine (phrases).
29	func NewSmartAuto(smart, llm Engine) *SmartAutoEngine {
30		return &SmartAutoEngine{smart: smart, llm: llm}
31	}
32	
33	// Name returns "smart-auto".
34	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Name() string { return "smart-auto" }
35	
36	// Supports translate mode (the main-path request mode); it dispatches to a
37	// dictionary lookup internally when the input looks like a single word.
38	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
39	
40	// Detect is not meaningful here (the LLM/dict sub-engines own detection).
41	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
42		return "", nil
43	}
44	
45	// Available reports true when either branch can serve.
46	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
47		return e.smart.Available(ctx) || e.llm.Available(ctx)
48	}
49	
50	// Translate routes a single word/term to the dictionary (smart-dict) and a phrase
51	// to the LLM. Streaming is preserved by returning the chosen sub-engine's channel.
52	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
53		if isLookup(req.Text) {
54			debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → dictionary lookup (smart-dict), target=%s", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target)
55			r := req
56			r.Mode = ModeDict
57			return e.smart.Translate(ctx, r)
58		}
59		debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → LLM translate, target=%s pair=%v", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target, req.Pair)
60		r := req
61		r.Mode = ModeTranslate
62		return e.llm.Translate(ctx, r)
63	}
64	
65	// isLookup reports whether text should be treated as a dictionary lookup (a
66	// single word or short term) rather than a phrase to translate. Latin/other: one
67	// token of letters (plus '-' / apostrophe), up to 32 runes. CJK: a short run of
68	// Han without spaces (a word or idiom, up to 4 characters). Anything with
69	// internal whitespace is a phrase.
70	func isLookup(text string) bool {
71		t := strings.TrimSpace(text)
72		if t == "" {
73			return false
74		}
75		if strings.ContainsAny(t, " \t\n\r") {
76			return false // multiple tokens → phrase
77		}
78		if lang.IsChinese(t) {
79			return len([]rune(t)) <= 4
80		}
81		for _, r := range t {
82			if !unicode.IsLetter(r) && r != '-' && r != '\'' {
83				return false // digits/punctuation → not a plain word
84			}
85		}
86		return len([]rune(t)) <= 32
87	}
88	
89	// truncateText shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
90	func truncateText(s string) string {
91		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
92		r := []rune(s)
93		if len(r) > 40 {
94			return string(r[:40]) + "…"
95		}
96		return s
97	}
98
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/dict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"os"
10		"sort"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/agnivade/levenshtein"
16	)
17	
18	// suggestLimit caps the number of ranked "did you mean" candidates on a miss.
19	const suggestLimit = 7
20	
21	// DictConfig configures the dictionary engine.
22	type DictConfig struct {
23		Endpoint string // base, e.g. https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries
24		Lang     string // e.g. "en"
25		Fuzzy    bool
26		Wordlist string // path to a newline word list; "" => /usr/share/dict/words
27		Timeout  time.Duration
28	}
29	
30	// DictEngine looks up word definitions via the Free Dictionary API, with an
31	// exact match first and a local fuzzy (nearest-headword) fallback on 404.
32	type DictEngine struct {
33		cfg  DictConfig
34		wl   *wordIndex
35		http *http.Client
36	}
37	
38	// NewDict builds a dictionary engine.
39	func NewDict(cfg DictConfig) *DictEngine {
40		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
41			cfg.Endpoint = "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries"
42		}
43		if cfg.Lang == "" {
44			cfg.Lang = "en"
45		}
46		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
47			cfg.Timeout = 5 * time.Second
48		}
49		path := cfg.Wordlist
50		if path == "" {
51			path = "/usr/share/dict/words"
52		}
53		return &DictEngine{cfg: cfg, wl: &wordIndex{path: path}, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
54	}
55	
56	// Name returns "dictionary".
57	func (e *DictEngine) Name() string { return "dictionary" }
58	
59	// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups only.
60	func (e *DictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
61	
62	// Detect is not meaningful for the dictionary engine.
63	func (e *DictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) { return "", nil }
64	
65	// Available probes the API with a common word.
66	func (e *DictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
67		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
68		defer cancel()
69		_, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, "test")
70		return err == nil && status == http.StatusOK
71	}
72	
73	// Translate performs a dictionary lookup (exact, then fuzzy on 404).
74	func (e *DictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
75		word := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
76		if word == "" {
77			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
78		}
79	
80		entry, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, word)
81		if err == nil && status == http.StatusOK && entry != nil {
82			return single(e.result(entry, req), nil), nil
83		}
84		if err != nil {
85			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err)), nil
86		}
87	
88		// 404: return a ranked "did you mean" list (the frontend chooses one). Gated
89		// to English because the bundled wordlist (/usr/share/dict/words) is English.
90		if status == http.StatusNotFound && e.cfg.Fuzzy && strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(e.cfg.Lang), "en") {
91			if cands, best := e.wl.nearestN(word, 2, suggestLimit); len(cands) > 0 {
92				return single(&TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: e.Name(), Suggestions: cands, SuggestDistance: best}, nil), nil
93			}
94		}
95		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word)), nil
96	}
97	
98	func (e *DictEngine) result(entry *DictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
99		return &TranslateResult{
100			Translation: entry.gloss(),
101			Target:      req.Target,
102			Engine:      e.Name(),
103			Dictionary:  entry,
104		}
105	}
106	
107	// --- Free Dictionary API wire types ---
108	
109	type apiEntry struct {
110		Word       string        `json:"word"`
111		Phonetic   string        `json:"phonetic"`
112		Phonetics  []apiPhonetic `json:"phonetics"`
113		Meanings   []apiMeaning  `json:"meanings"`
114		SourceURLs []string      `json:"sourceUrls"`
115	}
116	type apiPhonetic struct {
117		Text string `json:"text"`
118	}
119	type apiMeaning struct {
120		PartOfSpeech string          `json:"partOfSpeech"`
121		Definitions  []apiDefinition `json:"definitions"`
122		Synonyms     []string        `json:"synonyms"`
123		Antonyms     []string        `json:"antonyms"`
124	}
125	type apiDefinition struct {
126		Definition string `json:"definition"`
127		Example    string `json:"example"`
128	}
129	
130	// fetch requests one word; returns (entry, httpStatus, err). A 404 yields
131	// (nil, 404, nil) so the caller can trigger the fuzzy fallback.
132	func (e *DictEngine) fetch(ctx context.Context, word string) (*DictEntry, int, error) {
133		u := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s", strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.Endpoint, "/"), e.cfg.Lang, urlPathEscape(word))
134		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, 0, err
137		}
138		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
139		if err != nil {
140			return nil, 0, err
141		}
142		defer resp.Body.Close()
143		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {
144			_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
145			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
146		}
147		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
148			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
149			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))
150		}
151		var entries []apiEntry
152		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&entries); err != nil {
153			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("decode: %w", err)
154		}
155		if len(entries) == 0 {
156			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
157		}
158		return mergeEntries(entries), http.StatusOK, nil
159	}
160	
161	// mergeEntries folds the API's per-entry results into one DictEntry.
162	func mergeEntries(entries []apiEntry) *DictEntry {
163		out := &DictEntry{Word: entries[0].Word}
164		for _, en := range entries {
165			if out.Phonetic == "" && en.Phonetic != "" {
166				out.Phonetic = en.Phonetic
167			}
168			for _, p := range en.Phonetics {
169				if out.Phonetic == "" && p.Text != "" {
170					out.Phonetic = p.Text
171				}
172			}
173			for _, m := range en.Meanings {
174				meaning := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: m.PartOfSpeech, Synonyms: m.Synonyms, Antonyms: m.Antonyms}
175				for _, d := range m.Definitions {
176					meaning.Definitions = append(meaning.Definitions, Definition{Text: d.Definition, Example: d.Example})
177				}
178				out.Meanings = append(out.Meanings, meaning)
179			}
180			if out.SourceURL == "" && len(en.SourceURLs) > 0 {
181				out.SourceURL = en.SourceURLs[0]
182			}
183		}
184		return out
185	}
186	
187	// gloss returns a short one-line definition for history/plain display.
188	func (d *DictEntry) gloss() string {
189		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
190			for _, def := range m.Definitions {
191				if def.Text != "" {
192					return def.Text
193				}
194			}
195		}
196		return d.Word
197	}
198	
199	func urlPathEscape(s string) string {
200		// dictionary words are simple; escape spaces defensively.
201		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, " ", "%20")
202	}
203	
204	// --- local fuzzy word index ---
205	
206	type wordIndex struct {
207		path  string
208		once  sync.Once
209		words []string
210	}
211	
212	func (wi *wordIndex) load() {
213		wi.once.Do(func() {
214			b, err := os.ReadFile(wi.path)
215			if err != nil {
216				return // no wordlist: fuzzy silently disabled
217			}
218			for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
219				w := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(line))
220				if w != "" {
221					wi.words = append(wi.words, w)
222				}
223			}
224		})
225	}
226	
227	// nearestN returns up to n headwords within maxDist edits of word, closest first
228	// (ties broken alphabetically), excluding word itself. The second return is the
229	// best (smallest) edit distance among the results, or 0 when there are none.
230	func (wi *wordIndex) nearestN(word string, maxDist, n int) ([]string, int) {
231		wi.load()
232		type cand struct {
233			w string
234			d int
235		}
236		var cs []cand
237		for _, w := range wi.words {
238			if w == word || abs(len(w)-len(word)) > maxDist {
239				continue
240			}
241			if d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(word, w); d <= maxDist {
242				cs = append(cs, cand{w, d})
243			}
244		}
245		sort.Slice(cs, func(i, j int) bool {
246			if cs[i].d != cs[j].d {
247				return cs[i].d < cs[j].d
248			}
249			return cs[i].w < cs[j].w
250		})
251		if len(cs) > n {
252			cs = cs[:n]
253		}
254		out := make([]string, len(cs))
255		for i, c := range cs {
256			out[i] = c.w
257		}
258		best := 0
259		if len(cs) > 0 {
260			best = cs[0].d // slice is sorted by distance ascending
261		}
262		return out, best
263	}
264	
265	func abs(n int) int {
266		if n < 0 {
267			return -n
268		}
269		return n
270	}
271
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Command translate is a fast CLI/TUI translation tool.
2	package main
3	
4	import (
5		"context"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"os/signal"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd"
11	)
12	
13	func main() {
14		// Cancel in-flight work (including LLM requests) on Ctrl-C / SIGTERM.
15		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
16		defer stop()
17	
18		if err := cmd.Execute(ctx); err != nil {
19			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "translate:", err)
20			os.Exit(1)
21		}
22	}
```

```text
1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strings"
14	
15		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
16		"golang.org/x/term"
17	
18		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
19	
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
26		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
27		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
28	)
29	
30	var (
31		flagTo           string
32		flagFrom         string
33		flagModel        string
34		flagProvider     string
35		flagEngine       string
36		flagTier         string
37		flagPreset       string
38		flagInstructions string
39		flagPair         bool
40		flagPairWith     string
41		flagJSON         bool
42		flagNoHistory    bool
43		flagDebug        bool
44	)
45	
46	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
47	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
48		root := &cobra.Command{
49			Use:   "translate [text...]",
50			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
51			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
52				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
53				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
54				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
55			Version:       buildVersion(),
56			SilenceUsage:  true,
57			SilenceErrors: true,
58			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
59			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
60			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
61			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
62			RunE: runRoot,
63		}
64		f := root.PersistentFlags()
65		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
66		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
67		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
68		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
69		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
70		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
71		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
72		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
73		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
74		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
75		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
76		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
77		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
78	
79		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
80		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd())
81		return root
82	}
83	
84	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
85	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
86		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
87	}
88	
89	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
90	func overrides() config.Overrides {
91		return config.Overrides{
92			Source:       flagFrom,
93			Target:       flagTo,
94			Engine:       flagEngine,
95			Provider:     flagProvider,
96			Model:        flagModel,
97			Tier:         flagTier,
98			Preset:       flagPreset,
99			Instructions: flagInstructions,
100			Pair:         flagPair,
101			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
102			Debug:        flagDebug,
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
107	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
108	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
109	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
110		if len(args) > 0 {
111			return config.ModeCLI
112		}
113		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
114			return config.ModeCLI
115		}
116		return config.ModeTUI
117	}
118	
119	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
120		ctx := cmd.Context()
121	
122		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
123		if err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126		if created {
127			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
128		}
129	
130		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
131		if res.Debug {
132			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
133			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
134			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
135				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
136			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
137				debug.Enable(w)
138			}
139		}
140		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
141		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
142		pairWith := ""
143		if res.Pair {
144			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
145			pairWith = pwm.Code
146		}
147		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s",
148			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith)
149		if res.Pair && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
150			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
151		}
152	
153		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
154			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
155		}
156		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
157		if err != nil {
158			return err
159		}
160	
161		st := openStore(cfg)
162		if st != nil {
163			defer st.Close()
164		}
165	
166		switch {
167		case len(args) > 0:
168			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
169			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
170			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
171			if err != nil {
172				return err
173			}
174			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
175			return nil
176	
177		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
178			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
179			if err != nil {
180				return err
181			}
182			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
183			if text == "" {
184				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
185			}
186			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
187			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
188			if err != nil {
189				return err
190			}
191			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
192			return nil
193	
194		default:
195			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
196		}
197	}
198	
199	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
200	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
201		if pair && away != "" {
202			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
203			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
204			return t
205		}
206		return home
207	}
208	
209	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
210	func clip(s string, n int) string {
211		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
212		r := []rune(s)
213		if len(r) > n {
214			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
215		}
216		return s
217	}
218	
219	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
220	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
221	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
222		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
223			return nil
224		}
225		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
226			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
227		if err != nil {
228			return nil
229		}
230		return f
231	}
232	
233	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
234	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
235	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
236		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
237			return
238		}
239		stt, err := state.Load()
240		if err != nil || stt == nil {
241			return
242		}
243		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
244			res.Source = stt.Source
245			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
246		}
247		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
248			res.Target = stt.Target
249			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
250		}
251	}
252	
253	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
254	// suppressed with --no-history.
255	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
256		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
257			return nil
258		}
259		path := cfg.History.Path
260		if path == "" {
261			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
262		}
263		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
264		if err != nil {
265			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
266			return nil
267		}
268		return st
269	}
270	
271	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
272	//
273	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
274	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
275	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
276	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
277		if st != nil {
278			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
279		}
280		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
281			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
282		}
283	}
284	
285	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
286	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
287		src := source
288		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
289			src = res.DetectedSource
290		}
291		return store.Record{
292			SourceLang:   src,
293			TargetLang:   target,
294			Engine:       res.Engine,
295			Model:        res.Model,
296			Input:        input,
297			Output:       res.Translation,
298			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
299			Notes:        res.Notes,
300		}
301	}
302	
303	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
304	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
305		mode := "fixed"
306		if source == "auto" {
307			mode = "auto"
308		}
309		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
310			Source:     source,
311			Target:     target,
312			SourceMode: mode,
313			Engine:     engineName,
314		})
315	}
316	
317	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
318	// pair on exit.
319	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
320		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
321		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
322		modelProvider := ""
323		if res.Provider != nil {
324			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
325			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
326		}
327		p := tui.Params{
328			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
329			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
330			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
331			Store:         st,
332			Source:        source,
333			Target:        target,
334			Pair:          res.Pair,
335			PairWith:      pairWith,
336			Model:         res.Model,
337			Preset:        res.Preset,
338			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
339			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
340			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
341		}
342		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
343		if err != nil {
344			return err
345		}
346		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
347			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
348				s, t := fm.Pair()
349				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
350			}
351		}
352		return nil
353	}
354	
355	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
356	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
357	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
358		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
359		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
360		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
361			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
362		}
363		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
364			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
365		}
366		return sm.Code, tm.Code
367	}
368	
369	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
370	//
371	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
372	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
373	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
374	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
375		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
376		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON
377	
378		req := engine.Request{
379			Text:     text,
380			Source:   source,
381			Target:   target,
382			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
383			Stream:   stream,
384			Preset:   preset,
385			Extra:    instructions,
386			Pair:     pair,
387			PairHome: pairHome,
388			PairAway: pairAway,
389		}
390		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
391		if err != nil {
392			return nil, err
393		}
394	
395		var onTok func(string)
396		if stream {
397			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
398		}
399		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
400		if err != nil {
401			if stream {
402				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
403			}
404			return nil, err
405		}
406	
407		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
408		// failed and why, and how to switch.
409		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
410			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
411		}
412		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
413			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
414		}
415	
416		switch {
417		case flagJSON:
418			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
419			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
420			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
421				return nil, err
422			}
423		case stream:
424			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
425		default:
426			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
427		}
428		return res, nil
429	}
430
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/define.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		return nil
64	}
65	
66	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
67	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
68	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
69	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
70		cfg := res.Cfg
71		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
72			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
73		}
74		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
75			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
76		}
77		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
78	}
79	
80	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
81	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
82		d := res.Dictionary
83		if d == nil {
84			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
85				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
86			}
87			return res.Translation + "\n"
88		}
89		var b strings.Builder
90		head := d.Word
91		if d.Phonetic != "" {
92			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
93		}
94		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
95		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
96			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
97			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
98				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
99					break
100				}
101				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
102				if def.Example != "" {
103					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
104				}
105			}
106		}
107		return b.String()
108	}
109
```

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
11	)
12	
13	// LocalDictConfig configures the offline bilingual dictionary engine.
14	type LocalDictConfig struct {
15		Dir          string
16		CedictURL    string
17		EcdictURL    string
18		AutoDownload bool        // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first Chinese lookup
19		Fuzzy        bool        // emit ranked suggestions on a miss
20		APIFallback  *DictEngine // nil unless configured; used for English when ECDICT is absent
21		Timeout      time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// LocalDictEngine looks up definitions offline: Chinese → CC-CEDICT (zh→en),
25	// English → ECDICT (en→zh). It satisfies the Engine interface and maps results
26	// into the same DictEntry/Suggestions shape the TUI already renders.
27	type LocalDictEngine struct {
28		cfg LocalDictConfig
29		ce  *cedictIndex
30		ec  *ecdictDB
31		wl  *wordIndex // /usr/share/dict/words, for English "did you mean"
32	}
33	
34	// NewLocalDict builds a local bilingual dictionary engine.
35	func NewLocalDict(cfg LocalDictConfig) *LocalDictEngine {
36		return &LocalDictEngine{
37			cfg: cfg,
38			ce:  newCedictIndex(CedictPath(cfg.Dir)),
39			ec:  newEcdictDB(EcdictDBPath(cfg.Dir)),
40			wl:  &wordIndex{path: "/usr/share/dict/words"},
41		}
42	}
43	
44	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Name() string         { return "dictionary" }
45	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
46	
47	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
48		return "", nil
49	}
50	
51	// Available reports true if at least one data source (or the API fallback) is usable.
52	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
53		if fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) || e.ec.available() {
54			return true
55		}
56		if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
57			return e.cfg.APIFallback.Available(ctx)
58		}
59		return false
60	}
61	
62	// Translate routes by script: Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT.
63	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
64		word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
65		if word == "" {
66			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
67		}
68		if lang.IsChinese(word) {
69			return e.lookupZh(ctx, word, req), nil
70		}
71		return e.lookupEn(ctx, word, req), nil
72	}
73	
74	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupZh(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
75		if !fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) {
76			if e.cfg.AutoDownload {
77				if err := DownloadCedict(ctx, e.cfg.CedictURL, CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir), nil); err != nil {
78					return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
79				}
80			} else {
81				return single(notInstalled(req, "CC-CEDICT not installed — run `translate dict update cedict`"), nil)
82			}
83		}
84		if entries := e.ce.lookup(word); len(entries) > 0 {
85			return single(cedictResult(word, entries, req), nil)
86		}
87		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
88			if sugg := e.ce.prefixSuggest(word, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
89				// CC-CEDICT prefix matches carry no edit distance → 0 (unknown).
90				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, 0), nil)
91			}
92		}
93		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
94	}
95	
96	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupEn(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
97		if !e.ec.available() {
98			// No ECDICT yet: fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (English defs) if enabled.
99			if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
100				ch, err := e.cfg.APIFallback.Translate(ctx, req)
101				if err == nil {
102					return ch
103				}
104			}
105			return single(notInstalled(req, "ECDICT not installed — run `translate dict update ecdict`"), nil)
106		}
107		en, err := e.ec.lookup(ctx, word)
108		if err != nil {
109			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
110		}
111		if en != nil {
112			return single(ecdictResult(en, req), nil)
113		}
114		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
115			if sugg, best := e.wl.nearestN(strings.ToLower(word), 2, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
116				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, best), nil)
117			}
118		}
119		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
120	}
121	
122	// --- result mapping into the existing DictEntry / Suggestions shape ---
123	
124	func cedictResult(word string, entries []*cedictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
125		d := &DictEntry{Word: word}
126		if len(entries) > 0 {
127			d.Phonetic = entries[0].Pinyin
128		}
129		for _, en := range entries {
130			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: en.Pinyin}
131			for _, def := range en.Defs {
132				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: def})
133			}
134			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
135		}
136		gloss := ""
137		if len(entries) > 0 && len(entries[0].Defs) > 0 {
138			gloss = entries[0].Defs[0]
139		}
140		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
141	}
142	
143	func ecdictResult(en *ecdictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
144		d := &DictEntry{Word: en.Word, Phonetic: en.Phonetic}
145		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
146			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "中文"}
147			for _, line := range zh {
148				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
149			}
150			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
151		}
152		if de := splitEcdict(en.Definition); len(de) > 0 {
153			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "definition"}
154			for _, line := range de {
155				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
156			}
157			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
158		}
159		gloss := ""
160		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
161			gloss = zh[0]
162		}
163		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
164	}
165	
166	func suggestResult(sugg []string, req Request, bestDist int) *TranslateResult {
167		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Suggestions: sugg, SuggestDistance: bestDist}
168	}
169	
170	func notInstalled(req Request, note string) *TranslateResult {
171		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Notes: note}
172	}
173	
174	func fileExists(p string) bool {
175		_, err := os.Stat(p)
176		return err == nil
177	}
178
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/cedict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"os"
6		"regexp"
7		"sort"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	)
11	
12	// cedictLine matches: Traditional Simplified [pin1 yin1] /def1/def2/
13	var cedictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`^(\S+)\s+(\S+)\s+\[([^\]]*)\]\s+/(.*)/\s*$`)
14	
15	type cedictEntry struct {
16		Trad, Simp, Pinyin string
17		Defs               []string
18	}
19	
20	// cedictIndex is an in-memory CC-CEDICT (Chinese → English), keyed on both the
21	// traditional and simplified forms. Loaded lazily on first lookup.
22	type cedictIndex struct {
23		path  string
24		once  sync.Once
25		err   error
26		byKey map[string][]*cedictEntry
27		keys  []string // sorted, for prefix suggestions
28	}
29	
30	func newCedictIndex(path string) *cedictIndex { return &cedictIndex{path: path} }
31	
32	func (ci *cedictIndex) load() error {
33		ci.once.Do(func() {
34			f, err := os.Open(ci.path)
35			if err != nil {
36				ci.err = err
37				return
38			}
39			defer f.Close()
40			ci.byKey = map[string][]*cedictEntry{}
41			sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
42			sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
43			for sc.Scan() {
44				line := sc.Text()
45				if strings.HasPrefix(line, "#") {
46					continue
47				}
48				m := cedictLine.FindStringSubmatch(line)
49				if m == nil {
50					continue
51				}
52				e := &cedictEntry{Trad: m[1], Simp: m[2], Pinyin: m[3]}
53				for _, d := range strings.Split(m[4], "/") {
54					if d = strings.TrimSpace(d); d != "" {
55						e.Defs = append(e.Defs, d)
56					}
57				}
58				ci.byKey[e.Simp] = append(ci.byKey[e.Simp], e)
59				if e.Trad != e.Simp {
60					ci.byKey[e.Trad] = append(ci.byKey[e.Trad], e)
61				}
62			}
63			ci.err = sc.Err()
64			ci.keys = make([]string, 0, len(ci.byKey))
65			for k := range ci.byKey {
66				ci.keys = append(ci.keys, k)
67			}
68			sort.Strings(ci.keys)
69		})
70		return ci.err
71	}
72	
73	func (ci *cedictIndex) lookup(word string) []*cedictEntry {
74		if ci.load() != nil {
75			return nil
76		}
77		return ci.byKey[word]
78	}
79	
80	// prefixSuggest returns up to n headwords beginning with word (excluding it).
81	// Chinese uses prefix, not edit distance (CJK edit distance is noisy).
82	func (ci *cedictIndex) prefixSuggest(word string, n int) []string {
83		if ci.load() != nil {
84			return nil
85		}
86		i := sort.SearchStrings(ci.keys, word)
87		var out []string
88		for ; i < len(ci.keys) && len(out) < n; i++ {
89			k := ci.keys[i]
90			if !strings.HasPrefix(k, word) {
91				break
92			}
93			if k != word {
94				out = append(out, k)
95			}
96		}
97		return out
98	}
99
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/ecdict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"sync"
9	)
10	
11	type ecdictEntry struct {
12		Word, Phonetic, Translation, Definition string
13	}
14	
15	// ecdictDB queries the locally-built ECDICT SQLite (English → Chinese). Point
16	// lookups are on-disk (near-zero steady-state RAM). Opened lazily.
17	type ecdictDB struct {
18		path string
19		once sync.Once
20		err  error
21		db   *sql.DB
22	}
23	
24	func newEcdictDB(path string) *ecdictDB { return &ecdictDB{path: path} }
25	
26	func (e *ecdictDB) available() bool {
27		_, err := os.Stat(e.path)
28		return err == nil
29	}
30	
31	func (e *ecdictDB) open() error {
32		e.once.Do(func() {
33			if _, err := os.Stat(e.path); err != nil {
34				e.err = err
35				return
36			}
37			db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+e.path+"?_pragma=query_only(true)")
38			if err != nil {
39				e.err = err
40				return
41			}
42			e.db = db
43		})
44		return e.err
45	}
46	
47	// lookup returns the exact (case-insensitive) entry, or nil if not found.
48	func (e *ecdictDB) lookup(ctx context.Context, word string) (*ecdictEntry, error) {
49		if err := e.open(); err != nil {
50			return nil, err
51		}
52		row := e.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
53			`SELECT word, phonetic, translation, definition FROM entries WHERE word_lc = ? LIMIT 1`,
54			strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(word)))
55		var en ecdictEntry
56		switch err := row.Scan(&en.Word, &en.Phonetic, &en.Translation, &en.Definition); err {
57		case nil:
58			return &en, nil
59		case sql.ErrNoRows:
60			return nil, nil
61		default:
62			return nil, err
63		}
64	}
65	
66	// splitEcdict splits ECDICT translation/definition fields — their senses are
67	// joined by a literal backslash-n, not a real newline.
68	func splitEcdict(s string) []string {
69		var out []string
70		for _, p := range strings.Split(s, `\n`) {
71			if p = strings.TrimSpace(p); p != "" {
72				out = append(out, p)
73			}
74		}
75		return out
76	}
77
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/dictdata.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"compress/gzip"
5		"context"
6		"database/sql"
7		"encoding/csv"
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strconv"
14		"strings"
15	
16		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
17	
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
19	)
20	
21	// DictDir returns the directory holding local dictionary data.
22	func DictDir(dir string) string {
23		if dir != "" {
24			return dir
25		}
26		return filepath.Join(xdgpath.DataDir(), "dict")
27	}
28	
29	// CedictPath / EcdictDBPath are the on-disk locations of the two data sources.
30	func CedictPath(dir string) string   { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "cedict_ts.u8") }
31	func EcdictDBPath(dir string) string { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "ecdict.db") }
32	
33	const ecdictSchema = `CREATE TABLE entries(
34	  word TEXT, word_lc TEXT, phonetic TEXT, translation TEXT,
35	  definition TEXT, pos TEXT, frq INTEGER, exchange TEXT);`
36	
37	// DownloadCedict fetches the gzipped CC-CEDICT, decompresses it, and writes the
38	// plain text file atomically (~9.4 MB).
39	func DownloadCedict(ctx context.Context, url, dst string, prog func(string)) error {
40		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o700); err != nil {
41			return err
42		}
43		if prog != nil {
44			prog("downloading CC-CEDICT…")
45		}
46		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
51		if err != nil {
52			return err
53		}
54		defer resp.Body.Close()
55		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
56			return fmt.Errorf("cedict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
57		}
58		gz, err := gzip.NewReader(resp.Body)
59		if err != nil {
60			return err
61		}
62		defer gz.Close()
63	
64		tmp := dst + ".tmp"
65		f, err := os.Create(tmp)
66		if err != nil {
67			return err
68		}
69		if _, err := io.Copy(f, gz); err != nil {
70			f.Close()
71			os.Remove(tmp)
72			return err
73		}
74		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
75			os.Remove(tmp)
76			return err
77		}
78		return os.Rename(tmp, dst)
79	}
80	
81	// BuildEcdictDB downloads the ECDICT CSV (~63 MB, MIT) and builds a queryable
82	// SQLite database (~80-100 MB) from it, atomically. Streams the CSV so it never
83	// holds the whole file in memory.
84	func BuildEcdictDB(ctx context.Context, csvURL, dbPath string, prog func(string)) (err error) {
85		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0o700); err != nil {
86			return err
87		}
88		if prog != nil {
89			prog("downloading ECDICT CSV (~63 MB)…")
90		}
91		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, csvURL, nil)
92		if err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer resp.Body.Close()
100		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
101			return fmt.Errorf("ecdict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
102		}
103	
104		tmp := dbPath + ".tmp"
105		_ = os.Remove(tmp)
106		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+tmp+"?_pragma=journal_mode(off)&_pragma=synchronous(off)")
107		if err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110		// On any error, close and remove the partial db.
111		defer func() {
112			if err != nil {
113				db.Close()
114				os.Remove(tmp)
115			}
116		}()
117	
118		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, ecdictSchema); err != nil {
119			return err
120		}
121		if prog != nil {
122			prog("importing entries…")
123		}
124		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
125		if err != nil {
126			return err
127		}
128		stmt, err := tx.PrepareContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO entries(word,word_lc,phonetic,translation,definition,pos,frq,exchange) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
129		if err != nil {
130			return err
131		}
132	
133		r := csv.NewReader(resp.Body)
134		r.FieldsPerRecord = -1 // tolerate ragged rows
135		r.LazyQuotes = true
136		if _, err = r.Read(); err != nil { // header row
137			return err
138		}
139		n := 0
140		for {
141			if err = ctx.Err(); err != nil {
142				return err
143			}
144			rec, rerr := r.Read()
145			if rerr == io.EOF {
146				break
147			}
148			if rerr != nil || len(rec) < 4 {
149				continue
150			}
151			frq := 0
152			if len(rec) > 9 {
153				frq, _ = strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(rec[9]))
154			}
155			pos, exchange := "", ""
156			if len(rec) > 4 {
157				pos = rec[4]
158			}
159			if len(rec) > 10 {
160				exchange = rec[10]
161			}
162			if _, err = stmt.ExecContext(ctx, rec[0], strings.ToLower(rec[0]), rec[1], rec[3], rec[2], pos, frq, exchange); err != nil {
163				return err
164			}
165			n++
166		}
167		if err = tx.Commit(); err != nil {
168			return err
169		}
170		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, `CREATE INDEX idx_word_lc ON entries(word_lc)`); err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		if err = db.Close(); err != nil {
174			return err
175		}
176		if prog != nil {
177			prog(fmt.Sprintf("built %d entries", n))
178		}
179		return os.Rename(tmp, dbPath)
180	}
181
```

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	}
46	
47	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
48	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
49	type TranslateResult struct {
50		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
51		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
52		Target         string   `json:"target"`
53		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
54		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
55		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
56	
57		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
58		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
59		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
60	
61		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
62		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
63		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
64		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
65		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
66	
67		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
68		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
69		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
70	
71		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
72		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
73		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
74		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
75		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
76		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
77	
78		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
79		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
80		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
81		// Transient: not serialized.
82		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
83	}
84	
85	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
86	type DictEntry struct {
87		Word      string    `json:"word"`
88		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
89		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
90		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
91	}
92	
93	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
94	type Meaning struct {
95		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
96		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
97		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
98		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
99	}
100	
101	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
102	type Definition struct {
103		Text    string `json:"definition"`
104		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
105	}
106	
107	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
108	type ChunkKind int
109	
110	const (
111		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
112		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
113		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
114		ChunkDone
115		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
116		ChunkError
117	)
118	
119	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
120	type Chunk struct {
121		Kind   ChunkKind
122		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
123		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
124		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
125	}
126	
127	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
128	//
129	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
130	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
131	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
132	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
133	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
134	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
135	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
136	type Engine interface {
137		Name() string
138		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
139		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
140		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
141		Supports(m Mode) bool
142	}
143	
144	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
145	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
146	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
147		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
148		if err != nil {
149			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
150		} else {
151			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
152		}
153		close(ch)
154		return ch
155	}
156	
157	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
158	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
159	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
160	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
161		for c := range ch {
162			switch c.Kind {
163			case ChunkToken:
164				if onToken != nil {
165					onToken(c.Text)
166				}
167			case ChunkDone:
168				return c.Result, nil
169			case ChunkError:
170				return nil, c.Err
171			}
172		}
173		return nil, ErrNoResult
174	}
175
```

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"compress/gzip"
5		"context"
6		"database/sql"
7		"encoding/csv"
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strconv"
14		"strings"
15	
16		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
17	
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
19	)
20	
21	// DictDir returns the directory holding local dictionary data.
22	func DictDir(dir string) string {
23		if dir != "" {
24			return dir
25		}
26		return filepath.Join(xdgpath.DataDir(), "dict")
27	}
28	
29	// CedictPath / EcdictDBPath are the on-disk locations of the two data sources.
30	func CedictPath(dir string) string   { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "cedict_ts.u8") }
31	func EcdictDBPath(dir string) string { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "ecdict.db") }
32	
33	const ecdictSchema = `CREATE TABLE entries(
34	  word TEXT, word_lc TEXT, phonetic TEXT, translation TEXT,
35	  definition TEXT, pos TEXT, frq INTEGER, exchange TEXT);`
36	
37	// DownloadCedict fetches the gzipped CC-CEDICT, decompresses it, and writes the
38	// plain text file atomically (~9.4 MB).
39	func DownloadCedict(ctx context.Context, url, dst string, prog func(string)) error {
40		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o700); err != nil {
41			return err
42		}
43		if prog != nil {
44			prog("downloading CC-CEDICT…")
45		}
46		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
51		if err != nil {
52			return err
53		}
54		defer resp.Body.Close()
55		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
56			return fmt.Errorf("cedict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
57		}
58		gz, err := gzip.NewReader(resp.Body)
59		if err != nil {
60			return err
61		}
62		defer gz.Close()
63	
64		tmp := dst + ".tmp"
65		f, err := os.Create(tmp)
66		if err != nil {
67			return err
68		}
69		if _, err := io.Copy(f, gz); err != nil {
70			f.Close()
71			os.Remove(tmp)
72			return err
73		}
74		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
75			os.Remove(tmp)
76			return err
77		}
78		return os.Rename(tmp, dst)
79	}
80	
81	// BuildEcdictDB downloads the ECDICT CSV (~63 MB, MIT) and builds a queryable
82	// SQLite database (~80-100 MB) from it, atomically. Streams the CSV so it never
83	// holds the whole file in memory.
84	func BuildEcdictDB(ctx context.Context, csvURL, dbPath string, prog func(string)) (err error) {
85		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0o700); err != nil {
86			return err
87		}
88		if prog != nil {
89			prog("downloading ECDICT CSV (~63 MB)…")
90		}
91		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, csvURL, nil)
92		if err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer resp.Body.Close()
100		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
101			return fmt.Errorf("ecdict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
102		}
103	
104		tmp := dbPath + ".tmp"
105		_ = os.Remove(tmp)
106		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+tmp+"?_pragma=journal_mode(off)&_pragma=synchronous(off)")
107		if err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110		// On any error, close and remove the partial db.
111		defer func() {
112			if err != nil {
113				db.Close()
114				os.Remove(tmp)
115			}
116		}()
117	
118		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, ecdictSchema); err != nil {
119			return err
120		}
121		if prog != nil {
122			prog("importing entries…")
123		}
124		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
125		if err != nil {
126			return err
127		}
128		stmt, err := tx.PrepareContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO entries(word,word_lc,phonetic,translation,definition,pos,frq,exchange) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
129		if err != nil {
130			return err
131		}
132	
133		r := csv.NewReader(resp.Body)
134		r.FieldsPerRecord = -1 // tolerate ragged rows
135		r.LazyQuotes = true
136		if _, err = r.Read(); err != nil { // header row
137			return err
138		}
139		n := 0
140		for {
141			if err = ctx.Err(); err != nil {
142				return err
143			}
144			rec, rerr := r.Read()
145			if rerr == io.EOF {
146				break
147			}
148			if rerr != nil || len(rec) < 4 {
149				continue
150			}
151			frq := 0
152			if len(rec) > 9 {
153				frq, _ = strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(rec[9]))
154			}
155			pos, exchange := "", ""
156			if len(rec) > 4 {
157				pos = rec[4]
158			}
159			if len(rec) > 10 {
160				exchange = rec[10]
161			}
162			if _, err = stmt.ExecContext(ctx, rec[0], strings.ToLower(rec[0]), rec[1], rec[3], rec[2], pos, frq, exchange); err != nil {
163				return err
164			}
165			n++
166		}
167		if err = tx.Commit(); err != nil {
168			return err
169		}
170		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, `CREATE INDEX idx_word_lc ON entries(word_lc)`); err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		if err = db.Close(); err != nil {
174			return err
175		}
176		if prog != nil {
177			prog(fmt.Sprintf("built %d entries", n))
178		}
179		return os.Rename(tmp, dbPath)
180	}
181
```
</details></tool-use>
