<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/dict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"os"
10		"sort"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/agnivade/levenshtein"
16	)
17	
18	// suggestLimit caps the number of ranked "did you mean" candidates on a miss.
19	const suggestLimit = 7
20	
21	// DictConfig configures the dictionary engine.
22	type DictConfig struct {
23		Endpoint string // base, e.g. https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries
24		Lang     string // e.g. "en"
25		Fuzzy    bool
26		Wordlist string // path to a newline word list; "" => /usr/share/dict/words
27		Timeout  time.Duration
28	}
29	
30	// DictEngine looks up word definitions via the Free Dictionary API, with an
31	// exact match first and a local fuzzy (nearest-headword) fallback on 404.
32	type DictEngine struct {
33		cfg  DictConfig
34		wl   *wordIndex
35		http *http.Client
36	}
37	
38	// NewDict builds a dictionary engine.
39	func NewDict(cfg DictConfig) *DictEngine {
40		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
41			cfg.Endpoint = "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries"
42		}
43		if cfg.Lang == "" {
44			cfg.Lang = "en"
45		}
46		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
47			cfg.Timeout = 5 * time.Second
48		}
49		path := cfg.Wordlist
50		if path == "" {
51			path = "/usr/share/dict/words"
52		}
53		return &DictEngine{cfg: cfg, wl: &wordIndex{path: path}, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
54	}
55	
56	// Name returns "dictionary".
57	func (e *DictEngine) Name() string { return "dictionary" }
58	
59	// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups only.
60	func (e *DictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
61	
62	// Detect is not meaningful for the dictionary engine.
63	func (e *DictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) { return "", nil }
64	
65	// Available probes the API with a common word.
66	func (e *DictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
67		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
68		defer cancel()
69		_, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, "test")
70		return err == nil && status == http.StatusOK
71	}
72	
73	// Translate performs a dictionary lookup (exact, then fuzzy on 404).
74	func (e *DictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
75		word := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
76		if word == "" {
77			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
78		}
79	
80		entry, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, word)
81		if err == nil && status == http.StatusOK && entry != nil {
82			return single(e.result(entry, req), nil), nil
83		}
84		if err != nil {
85			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err)), nil
86		}
87	
88		// 404: return a ranked "did you mean" list (the frontend chooses one). Gated
89		// to English because the bundled wordlist (/usr/share/dict/words) is English.
90		if status == http.StatusNotFound && e.cfg.Fuzzy && strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(e.cfg.Lang), "en") {
91			if cands, best := e.wl.nearestN(word, 2, suggestLimit); len(cands) > 0 {
92				return single(&TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: e.Name(), Suggestions: cands, SuggestDistance: best}, nil), nil
93			}
94		}
95		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word)), nil
96	}
97	
98	func (e *DictEngine) result(entry *DictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
99		return &TranslateResult{
100			Translation: entry.gloss(),
101			Target:      req.Target,
102			Engine:      e.Name(),
103			Dictionary:  entry,
104		}
105	}
106	
107	// --- Free Dictionary API wire types ---
108	
109	type apiEntry struct {
110		Word       string        `json:"word"`
111		Phonetic   string        `json:"phonetic"`
112		Phonetics  []apiPhonetic `json:"phonetics"`
113		Meanings   []apiMeaning  `json:"meanings"`
114		SourceURLs []string      `json:"sourceUrls"`
115	}
116	type apiPhonetic struct {
117		Text string `json:"text"`
118	}
119	type apiMeaning struct {
120		PartOfSpeech string          `json:"partOfSpeech"`
121		Definitions  []apiDefinition `json:"definitions"`
122		Synonyms     []string        `json:"synonyms"`
123		Antonyms     []string        `json:"antonyms"`
124	}
125	type apiDefinition struct {
126		Definition string `json:"definition"`
127		Example    string `json:"example"`
128	}
129	
130	// fetch requests one word; returns (entry, httpStatus, err). A 404 yields
131	// (nil, 404, nil) so the caller can trigger the fuzzy fallback.
132	func (e *DictEngine) fetch(ctx context.Context, word string) (*DictEntry, int, error) {
133		u := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s", strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.Endpoint, "/"), e.cfg.Lang, urlPathEscape(word))
134		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, 0, err
137		}
138		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
139		if err != nil {
140			return nil, 0, err
141		}
142		defer resp.Body.Close()
143		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {
144			_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
145			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
146		}
147		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
148			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
149			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))
150		}
151		var entries []apiEntry
152		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&entries); err != nil {
153			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("decode: %w", err)
154		}
155		if len(entries) == 0 {
156			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
157		}
158		return mergeEntries(entries), http.StatusOK, nil
159	}
160	
161	// mergeEntries folds the API's per-entry results into one DictEntry.
162	func mergeEntries(entries []apiEntry) *DictEntry {
163		out := &DictEntry{Word: entries[0].Word}
164		for _, en := range entries {
165			if out.Phonetic == "" && en.Phonetic != "" {
166				out.Phonetic = en.Phonetic
167			}
168			for _, p := range en.Phonetics {
169				if out.Phonetic == "" && p.Text != "" {
170					out.Phonetic = p.Text
171				}
172			}
173			for _, m := range en.Meanings {
174				meaning := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: m.PartOfSpeech, Synonyms: m.Synonyms, Antonyms: m.Antonyms}
175				for _, d := range m.Definitions {
176					meaning.Definitions = append(meaning.Definitions, Definition{Text: d.Definition, Example: d.Example})
177				}
178				out.Meanings = append(out.Meanings, meaning)
179			}
180			if out.SourceURL == "" && len(en.SourceURLs) > 0 {
181				out.SourceURL = en.SourceURLs[0]
182			}
183		}
184		return out
185	}
186	
187	// gloss returns a short one-line definition for history/plain display.
188	func (d *DictEntry) gloss() string {
189		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
190			for _, def := range m.Definitions {
191				if def.Text != "" {
192					return def.Text
193				}
194			}
195		}
196		return d.Word
197	}
198	
199	func urlPathEscape(s string) string {
200		// dictionary words are simple; escape spaces defensively.
201		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, " ", "%20")
202	}
203	
204	// --- local fuzzy word index ---
205	
206	type wordIndex struct {
207		path  string
208		once  sync.Once
209		words []string
210	}
211	
212	func (wi *wordIndex) load() {
213		wi.once.Do(func() {
214			b, err := os.ReadFile(wi.path)
215			if err != nil {
216				return // no wordlist: fuzzy silently disabled
217			}
218			for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
219				w := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(line))
220				if w != "" {
221					wi.words = append(wi.words, w)
222				}
223			}
224		})
225	}
226	
227	// nearestN returns up to n headwords within maxDist edits of word, closest first
228	// (ties broken alphabetically), excluding word itself. The second return is the
229	// best (smallest) edit distance among the results, or 0 when there are none.
230	func (wi *wordIndex) nearestN(word string, maxDist, n int) ([]string, int) {
231		wi.load()
232		type cand struct {
233			w string
234			d int
235		}
236		var cs []cand
237		for _, w := range wi.words {
238			if w == word || abs(len(w)-len(word)) > maxDist {
239				continue
240			}
241			if d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(word, w); d <= maxDist {
242				cs = append(cs, cand{w, d})
243			}
244		}
245		sort.Slice(cs, func(i, j int) bool {
246			if cs[i].d != cs[j].d {
247				return cs[i].d < cs[j].d
248			}
249			return cs[i].w < cs[j].w
250		})
251		if len(cs) > n {
252			cs = cs[:n]
253		}
254		out := make([]string, len(cs))
255		for i, c := range cs {
256			out[i] = c.w
257		}
258		best := 0
259		if len(cs) > 0 {
260			best = cs[0].d // slice is sorted by distance ascending
261		}
262		return out, best
263	}
264	
265	func abs(n int) int {
266		if n < 0 {
267			return -n
268		}
269		return n
270	}
271
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Package config defines translate's on-disk configuration (config.toml) and the
2	// resolution of effective settings (flags > config > env > defaults).
3	package config
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10	
11		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
14	)
15	
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
22		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
23		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
24		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
25		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
26		History   History    `toml:"history"`
27	}
28	
29	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
30	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
31	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
32	type Overlay struct {
33		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
34		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
35		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
36		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
37		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
38		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
39		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
40		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
41		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
42		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
43		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
44		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
45		Debug         *bool   `toml:"debug,omitempty"`
46	}
47	
48	// General holds behavior settings.
49	type General struct {
50		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
51		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
52		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
53		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
54		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`
55		LiveTranslate     bool   `toml:"live_translate"`
56		DebounceMs        int    `toml:"debounce_ms"`
57		Engine            string `toml:"engine"`                 // auto | smartauto | llm | google | <provider>
58		Tier              string `toml:"tier"`                   // default | fast | max
59		Preset            string `toml:"preset"`                 // concise | contextual | dictionary
60		Instructions      string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"` // extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)
61		AlternativesCount int    `toml:"alternatives_count"`
62		Stream            bool   `toml:"stream"`
63		Color             string `toml:"color"` // auto | always | never
64		Debug             bool   `toml:"debug"` // verbose decision logging (also --debug / TRANSLATE_DEBUG)
65	}
66	
67	// Chain is the ordered fallback list of provider/engine names.
68	type Chain struct {
69		Order []string `toml:"order"`
70	}
71	
72	// Provider is one OpenAI-compatible LLM backend.
73	type Provider struct {
74		Name      string `toml:"name"`

76		BaseURL   string `toml:"base_url"`
77		Model     string `toml:"model"`

81	}

88		UserAgent string   `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
89		TimeoutMs int      `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
90	}
91	
92	// Dict configures the dictionary lookup engine.
93	type Dict struct {
94		Enabled  bool   `toml:"enabled"`
95		Source   string `toml:"source"` // local (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT) | api (dictionaryapi.dev)
96		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
97		Lang     string `toml:"lang,omitempty"`
98		Fuzzy    bool   `toml:"fuzzy"`

100	
101		// Local bilingual dictionary (source=local).
102		Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => <XDG data>/dict
103		CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
104		EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`
105		AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"` // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first zh lookup
106		APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`  // fall back to dictionaryapi.dev on an English miss
107	}
108	
109	// History configures translation history storage.
110	type History struct {
111		Enabled bool   `toml:"enabled"`
112		Backend string `toml:"backend"` // jsonl | sqlite
113		Path    string `toml:"path,omitempty"`
114	}
115	
116	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back
117	// to the LLM when it misses or the fuzzy match is too far off (see engine.SmartDict).
118	type SmartDict struct {
119		// CloseDistance is the English edit-distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
120		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
121		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.
122		CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`
123		Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"` // LLM prompt style for the fallback
124		DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`   // `translate define` uses smart-dict when a provider is available
125	}
126	
127	// Recommended copilot-proxy model ids per tier. Claude models are served via the
128	// Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages); the LLM engine routes them there

132		ModelFast    = "claude-haiku-4-5" // snappy — the out-of-box default tier
133		ModelMax     = "claude-opus-4-8"  // highest quality
134	)

188				Endpoint:     "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries",

192				EcdictURL:    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv",
193				AutoDownload: false, // explicit `dict update` is the blessed path
194				APIFallback:  true,  // English lookups still work before ECDICT is installed
195			},
196			SmartDict: SmartDict{

198				Preset:        "dictionary", // gloss + example sentences
199				DefineDefault: true,         // `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up

207	
208	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
209	func Path() string {
210		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
211			return p
212		}
213		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
214	}
215	
216	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
217	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
218	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
219		p := Path()
220		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
221		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
222			cfg = Default()
223			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
224				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
225			}
226			return cfg, true, nil
227		}
228		if err != nil {
229			return nil, false, err
230		}
231		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
232		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
233			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
234		}
235		return cfg, false, nil
236	}
237	
238	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
239	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
240		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
241			return err
242		}
243		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
244		if err != nil {
245			return err
246		}
247		p := Path()
248		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
249		if err != nil {
250			return err
251		}
252		tmpName := tmp.Name()
253		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
254		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
255			tmp.Close()
256			return err
257		}
258		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
259			return err
260		}
261		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
262	}
263	
264	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
265	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
266		for i := range c.Providers {
267			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
268				return &c.Providers[i]
269			}
270		}
271		return nil
272	}
273	
274	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
275	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
276	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
277		switch tier {
278		case "fast":
279			if p.ModelFast != "" {
280				return p.ModelFast
281			}
282		case "max":
283			if p.ModelMax != "" {
284				return p.ModelMax
285			}
286		}
287		return p.Model
288	}
```

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
21	}
22	
23	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
24	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
25	type Mode int
26	
27	const (
28		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
29		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
30	)
31	
32	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
33	type Resolved struct {
34		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
35		Target       string
36		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
37		Provider     *Provider
38		Model        string
39		Tier         string
40		Preset       string
41		Instructions string
42		Pair         bool
43		PairWith     string
44		Stream       bool
45		Color        string
46		Debug        bool
47	
48		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
49		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
50		LiveTranslate bool
51		DebounceMs    int
52	
53		Cfg *Config
54	}
55	
56	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
57	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
58	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
59	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
60	
61	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
62	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
63		switch mode {
64		case ModeCLI:
65			return c.CLI
66		case ModeTUI:
67			return c.TUI
68		}
69		return nil
70	}
71	
72	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
73	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
74	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
75		if ov == nil {
76			return
77		}
78		if ov.Engine != nil {
79			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
80		}
81		if ov.Tier != nil {
82			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
83		}
84		if ov.Preset != nil {
85			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
86		}
87		if ov.Instructions != nil {
88			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
89		}
90		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
91			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
92		}
93		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
94			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
95		}
96		if ov.Pair != nil {
97			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
98		}
99		if ov.PairWith != nil {
100			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
101		}
102		if ov.Stream != nil {
103			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
104		}
105		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
106			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
107		}
108		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
109			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
110		}
111		if ov.Debug != nil {
112			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
113		}
114	}
115	
116	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
117	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
118	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
119		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
120		// the on-disk config is untouched).
121		g := c.General
122		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
123		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
124	
125		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
126			if flag != "" {
127				return flag
128			}
129			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
130				return v
131			}
132			return cfgVal
133		}
134	
135		r := Resolved{
136			Cfg:           c,
137			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
138			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
139			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
140			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
141			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
142			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
143			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
144			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
145			Color:         g.Color,
146			Stream:        g.Stream,
147			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
148			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
149			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
150		}
151	
152		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
153		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
154		if provName == "" {
155			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
156				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
157			} else {
158				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
159			}
160		}
161		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
162	
163		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
164		// provider's tier model.
165		cfgModel := ""
166		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
167			cfgModel = *ov.Model
168		}
169		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
170		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
171			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
172		}
173		return r
174	}
175	
176	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
177	// else the first configured provider, else "".
178	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
179		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
180			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
181				return name
182			}
183		}
184		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
185			return c.Providers[0].Name
186		}
187		return ""
188	}
189
```

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
10	)
11	
12	// buildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
13	// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
14	// engine="smartauto" builds the smart default (dictionary for words, LLM for
15	// phrases, bidirectional); a named provider builds a single LLM engine.
16	func buildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
17		cfg := res.Cfg
18	
19		switch res.Engine {
20		case "auto":
21			return buildAutoChain(res)
22		case "smartauto":
23			// Needs an LLM provider for the word-miss fallback and phrase translation;
24			// without one, behave like the plain auto chain (google/dict).
25			if res.Provider == nil {
26				return buildAutoChain(res)
27			}
28			return smartAutoFromConfig(res), nil
29		case "google":
30			return googleFromConfig(cfg), nil
31		case "llm":
32			// "llm" means "the resolved provider" (already chosen by Resolve).
33			if res.Provider != nil {
34				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
35			}
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
37		default:
38			// A named provider (e.g. "copilot", "ollama").
39			if res.Provider != nil {
40				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
41			}
42			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
43		}
44	}
45	
46	// buildAutoChain builds the "auto" fallback Chain over the providers/google named
47	// in chain.order. (google/dict engines otherwise join only via smart-auto or the
48	// TUI ^e cycle.)
49	func buildAutoChain(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
50		cfg := res.Cfg
51		var engines []engine.Engine
52		for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
53			switch {
54			case cfg.ProviderByName(name) != nil:
55				p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
56				engines = append(engines, llmFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
57			case name == "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled:
58				engines = append(engines, googleFromConfig(cfg))
59			}
60			// "dict" is wired in via smart-auto / the TUI engine cycle.
61		}
62		if len(engines) == 0 {
63			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
64		}
65		if len(engines) == 1 {
66			return engines[0], nil
67		}
68		return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
69	}
70	
71	// llmFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
72	func llmFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
73		if model == "" {
74			model = p.Model
75		}
76		return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
77			Name:      p.Name,
78			BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
79			Model:     model,
80			APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
81		})
82	}
83	
84	// googleFromConfig builds the free Google engine from config.
85	func googleFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) *engine.GoogleEngine {
86		return engine.NewGoogle(engine.GoogleConfig{
87			Endpoint:  cfg.Google.Endpoint,
88			ExtraDT:   cfg.Google.ExtraDT,
89			UserAgent: cfg.Google.UserAgent,
90			Timeout:   time.Duration(cfg.Google.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
91		})
92	}
93	
94	// dictFromConfig builds the dictionary engine from config: the offline bilingual
95	// engine (source=local, default) or the dictionaryapi.dev engine (source=api).
96	func dictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
97		if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" {
98			return engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
99				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
100				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
101				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
102				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
103			})
104		}
105		var apiFb *engine.DictEngine
106		if cfg.Dict.APIFallback {
107			apiFb = engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
108				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
109				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
110				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
111				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
112			})
113		}
114		return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{
115			Dir:          cfg.Dict.Dir,
116			CedictURL:    cfg.Dict.CedictURL,
117			EcdictURL:    cfg.Dict.EcdictURL,
118			AutoDownload: cfg.Dict.AutoDownload,
119			Fuzzy:        cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
120			APIFallback:  apiFb,
121		})
122	}
123	
124	// smartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
125	// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
126	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
127	func smartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
128		cfg := res.Cfg
129		return engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
130			CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
131			Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
132		})
133	}
134	
135	// smartAutoFromConfig builds the smart-auto default: a single word/term is a
136	// dictionary lookup (smart-dict, with an LLM fallback), and a phrase is an LLM
137	// translation via the auto chain (so phrases keep provider→google resilience).
138	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
139	func smartAutoFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
140		llm, err := buildAutoChain(res)
141		if err != nil {
142			// No chain engines (unlikely once a provider exists): fall back to the bare
143			// resolved provider so phrases can still translate.
144			llm = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
145		}
146		return engine.NewSmartAuto(smartDictFromConfig(res), llm)
147	}
148	
149	// buildEngineSet builds the list of engines the TUI can cycle through with ^e:
150	// the resolved primary (translate), plus Google (fast, keyless), the dictionary
151	// (word lookup), and — when an LLM provider is available — the smart dictionary
152	// (dictionary with an LLM fallback), so the user can switch on the fly.
153	func buildEngineSet(res config.Resolved, primary engine.Engine) []tui.NamedEngine {
154		cfg := res.Cfg
155		primaryName := primary.Name() // "auto", "smart-auto", "copilot", "google", …
156		if primaryName == "" {
157			primaryName = "auto"
158		}
159		set := []tui.NamedEngine{{Name: primaryName, Engine: primary, Mode: engine.ModeTranslate}}
160		if primaryName != "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled {
161			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "google", Engine: googleFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeTranslate})
162		}
163		if cfg.Dict.Enabled {
164			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "dictionary", Engine: dictFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
165			if res.Provider != nil {
166				set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "smart-dict", Engine: smartDictFromConfig(res), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
167			}
168		}
169		return set
170	}
171
```

```text
1	package engine

16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
17	)
18	
19	// LLMConfig parameterizes an OpenAI-compatible backend (copilot-proxy, Ollama,
20	// OpenRouter, LiteLLM, or a generic base_url+key endpoint).
21	type LLMConfig struct {
22		Name      string        // "copilot", "ollama", "openrouter", ...
23		BaseURL   string        // e.g. "http://localhost:4141/v1"
24		Model     string        // e.g. "claude-sonnet-5"
25		APIKeyEnv string        // env var holding the key; "" => no Authorization header
26		Timeout   time.Duration // per-request timeout (0 => 60s)
27	}
28	
29	// LLMEngine talks to any OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint.
30	type LLMEngine struct {
31		cfg  LLMConfig
32		key  string
33		http *http.Client
34	}
35	
36	// NewLLM builds an LLM engine, resolving the API key once from the environment.
37	func NewLLM(cfg LLMConfig) *LLMEngine {
38		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
39			cfg.Timeout = 60 * time.Second
40		}
41		key := ""
42		if cfg.APIKeyEnv != "" {
43			key = strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv))
44		}
45		return &LLMEngine{cfg: cfg, key: key, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
46	}
47	
48	// Name returns the provider name (e.g. "copilot").
49	func (e *LLMEngine) Name() string { return e.cfg.Name }
50	
51	// Supports reports that LLM engines translate. (Dictionary lookups route to the
52	// dedicated dictionary engine in v1, so the chain never sends dict mode here.)
53	func (e *LLMEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
54	
55	// auth adds the bearer token only when a key is configured. copilot-proxy needs
56	// no Authorization header, so an empty APIKeyEnv yields no header at all.
57	func (e *LLMEngine) auth(r *http.Request) {
58		if e.key != "" {
59			r.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+e.key)
60		}
61	}
62	
63	// endpoint joins the base URL with a path segment.
64	func (e *LLMEngine) endpoint(path string) string {
65		return strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.BaseURL, "/") + path
66	}
67	
68	// Available is a cheap health probe: GET {BaseURL}/models with a short timeout.
69	func (e *LLMEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
70		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 800*time.Millisecond)
71		defer cancel()
72		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
73		if err != nil {
74			return false
75		}
76		e.auth(req)
77		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
78		if err != nil {
79			return false
80		}
81		defer resp.Body.Close()
82		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
83		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
84	}
85	
86	// Detect is a no-op for the LLM engine in this slice; offline detection is added
87	// with the free-API slice. Callers fall back to lang.Detect when this returns "".
88	func (e *LLMEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
89		return "", nil
90	}
91	
92	// --- OpenAI chat wire types ---
93	
94	type chatMessage struct {
95		Role    string `json:"role"`
96		Content string `json:"content"`
97	}
98	
99	type chatRequest struct {
100		Model    string        `json:"model"`
101		Messages []chatMessage `json:"messages"`
102		Stream   bool          `json:"stream"`
103	}
104	
105	type chatResponse struct {
106		Choices []struct {
107			Message      chatMessage `json:"message"`
108			FinishReason string      `json:"finish_reason"`
109		} `json:"choices"`
110		Error *struct {
111			Message string `json:"message"`
112		} `json:"error"`
113	}
114	
115	// streamDelta is one OpenAI SSE chunk (stream:true). FinishReason is non-nil
116	// only on the terminal chunk ("stop" on a clean finish, "length" on a cap hit).
117	type streamDelta struct {
118		Choices []struct {
119			Delta struct {
120				Content string `json:"content"`
121			} `json:"delta"`
122			FinishReason *string `json:"finish_reason"`
123		} `json:"choices"`
124	}
125	
126	// --- Anthropic Messages API wire types (/v1/messages) ---
127	
128	const anthropicMaxTokens = 4096
129	
130	type anthropicRequest struct {
131		Model     string             `json:"model"`
132		MaxTokens int                `json:"max_tokens"`
133		System    string             `json:"system,omitempty"`
134		Messages  []anthropicMessage `json:"messages"`
135		Stream    bool               `json:"stream"`
136	}
137	
138	type anthropicMessage struct {
139		Role    string `json:"role"`
140		Content string `json:"content"`
141	}
142	
143	type anthropicResponse struct {
144		Content []struct {
145			Type string `json:"type"`
146			Text string `json:"text"`
147		} `json:"content"`
148		StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
149		Error      *struct {
150			Message string `json:"message"`
151		} `json:"error"`
152	}
153	
154	type anthropicStreamEvent struct {
155		Type  string `json:"type"`
156		Delta struct {
157			Type string `json:"type"`
158			Text string `json:"text"`
159			// StopReason is carried on the terminal "message_delta" event
160			// ("end_turn"/"stop_sequence" on a clean finish, "max_tokens" on a cap
161			// hit); empty on "text_delta" events.
162			StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
163		} `json:"delta"`
164		Error *struct {
165			Message string `json:"message"`
166		} `json:"error"`
167	}
168	
169	// isAnthropicModel reports whether a model id must use the Anthropic Messages API
170	// (/v1/messages) rather than /chat/completions. copilot-proxy serves Claude
171	// models ONLY via the messages endpoint — they return HTTP 400
172	// "model_not_supported" on /chat/completions despite being listed in /v1/models.
173	func isAnthropicModel(model string) bool {
174		return strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(model), "claude")
175	}
176	
177	// Translate performs a translation, streaming tokens when req.Stream is set.
178	// It always returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal chunk, and
179	// dispatches to the Anthropic Messages API for Claude models.
180	func (e *LLMEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
181		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
182			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
183		}
184		model := NormalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model)
185		// Apply a per-request model override only when it targets this provider, so a
186		// copilot model id never reaches an Ollama fallback (which would 404).
187		if req.Model != "" && (req.ModelProvider == "" || req.ModelProvider == e.cfg.Name) {
188			model = NormalizeModelID(req.Model)
189		}
190		if isAnthropicModel(model) {
191			return e.translateAnthropic(ctx, req, model)
192		}
193		return e.translateOpenAI(ctx, req, model)
194	}
195	
196	// ModelLister is implemented by engines that can enumerate their models.
197	type ModelLister interface {
198		Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error)
199	}
200	
201	type modelsResponse struct {
202		Data []struct {
203			ID                 string   `json:"id"`
204			SupportedEndpoints []string `json:"supported_endpoints"`
205		} `json:"data"`
206	}
207	
208	// Models fetches the provider's model ids, keeping only those usable through the
209	// transports this engine speaks: OpenAI /chat/completions, or (for claude-*) the
210	// Anthropic /v1/messages endpoint. Ids needing only /responses are dropped.
211	func (e *LLMEngine) Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
212		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 4*time.Second)
213		defer cancel()
214		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
215		if err != nil {
216			return nil, err
217		}
218		e.auth(req)
219		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
220		if err != nil {
221			return nil, err
222		}
223		defer resp.Body.Close()
224		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
225			return nil, e.httpError(resp)
226		}
227		var mr modelsResponse
228		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&mr); err != nil {
229			return nil, err
230		}
231		var out []string
232		for _, m := range mr.Data {
233			if usableModel(m.ID, m.SupportedEndpoints) {
234				out = append(out, m.ID)
235			}
236		}
237		sort.Strings(out)
238		return out, nil
239	}
240	
241	// usableModel reports whether a model id can be driven by this engine.
242	func usableModel(id string, endpoints []string) bool {
243		if isAnthropicModel(id) {
244			return true // routed via /v1/messages
245		}
246		for _, ep := range endpoints {
247			if ep == "/chat/completions" {
248				return true
249			}
250		}
251		// No endpoint metadata (e.g. Ollama) => assume chat-usable.
252		return len(endpoints) == 0
253	}
254	
255	// finalize builds the terminal result from the accumulated translation text.
256	func (e *LLMEngine) finalize(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
257		res := &TranslateResult{
258			Translation: strings.TrimSpace(full),
259			Target:      req.Target,
260			Engine:      e.cfg.Name,
261			Model:       model,
262		}
263		// The plain-text prompt returns only the translation, so fill the detected
264		// source offline when the caller asked to auto-detect.
265		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
266			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
267		}
268		return res
269	}
270	
271	// markTruncated flags a result whose stream ended before the model finished, so
272	// the caller keeps the partial text but never treats it as a complete answer.
273	func markTruncated(res *TranslateResult) {
274		res.Truncated = true
275		res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings,
276			"output was cut off before completion (stream truncated) — press Enter to retry")
277	}
278	
279	// translateOpenAI uses the OpenAI /chat/completions endpoint.
280	func (e *LLMEngine) translateOpenAI(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
281		system, user := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
282		body := chatRequest{
283			Model: model,
284			Messages: []chatMessage{
285				{Role: "system", Content: system},
286				{Role: "user", Content: user},
287			},
288			Stream: req.Stream,
289		}
290		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
291		if err != nil {
292			return nil, err
293		}
294		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/chat/completions"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
295		if err != nil {
296			return nil, err
297		}
298		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
299		if req.Stream {
300			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
301		}
302		e.auth(httpReq)
303	
304		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32) // buffered so a fast stream doesn't block on renders
305		go func() {
306			defer close(ch)
307			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
308			if err != nil {
309				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
310				return
311			}
312			defer resp.Body.Close()
313			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
314				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
315				return
316			}
317	
318			var full strings.Builder
319			complete := true
320			if req.Stream {
321				var err error
322				complete, err = e.readSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
323				if err != nil {
324					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
325					return
326				}
327			} else {
328				var cr chatResponse
329				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&cr); err != nil {
330					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
331					return
332				}
333				if cr.Error != nil {
334					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, cr.Error.Message)}
335					return
336				}
337				if len(cr.Choices) == 0 {
338					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: empty response", e.cfg.Name)}
339					return
340				}
341				full.WriteString(cr.Choices[0].Message.Content)
342				complete = cr.Choices[0].FinishReason != "length" // "length" => hit the cap
343			}
344			res := e.finalize(full.String(), model, req)
345			if !complete {
346				markTruncated(res)
347			}
348			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
349		}()
350		return ch, nil
351	}
352	
353	// translateAnthropic uses the Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages).
354	func (e *LLMEngine) translateAnthropic(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
355		system, user := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
356		body := anthropicRequest{
357			Model:     model,
358			MaxTokens: anthropicMaxTokens,
359			System:    system,
360			Messages:  []anthropicMessage{{Role: "user", Content: user}},
361			Stream:    req.Stream,
362		}
363		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
364		if err != nil {
365			return nil, err
366		}
367		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/messages"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
368		if err != nil {
369			return nil, err
370		}
371		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
372		httpReq.Header.Set("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
373		if req.Stream {
374			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
375		}
376		e.anthropicAuth(httpReq)
377	
378		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32)
379		go func() {
380			defer close(ch)
381			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
382			if err != nil {
383				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
384				return
385			}
386			defer resp.Body.Close()
387			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
388				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
389				return
390			}
391	
392			var full strings.Builder
393			complete := true
394			if req.Stream {
395				var err error
396				complete, err = e.readAnthropicSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
397				if err != nil {
398					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
399					return
400				}
401			} else {
402				var ar anthropicResponse
403				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&ar); err != nil {
404					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
405					return
406				}
407				if ar.Error != nil {
408					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ar.Error.Message)}
409					return
410				}
411				for _, c := range ar.Content {
412					if c.Type == "text" {
413						full.WriteString(c.Text)
414					}
415				}
416				complete = ar.StopReason != "max_tokens" // "max_tokens" => hit the cap
417			}
418			res := e.finalize(full.String(), model, req)
419			if !complete {
420				markTruncated(res)
421			}
422			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
423		}()
424		return ch, nil
425	}
426	
427	// anthropicAuth sets the Anthropic auth header (x-api-key, not Bearer). The
428	// copilot-proxy needs none, so an empty key yields no header.
429	func (e *LLMEngine) anthropicAuth(r *http.Request) {
430		if e.key != "" {
431			r.Header.Set("x-api-key", e.key)
432		}
433	}
434	
435	// readSSE parses an OpenAI-style event stream, emitting a ChunkToken per content
436	// delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports complete=true only
437	// when the stream ended on a terminal marker ([DONE] or finish_reason=="stop");
438	// a stream that closes with no marker, or on finish_reason=="length", is treated
439	// as truncated so the caller can flag it rather than silently accept partial text.
440	func (e *LLMEngine) readSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
441		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
442		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20) // tolerate long SSE lines
443		sawDone := false
444		finish := ""
445		for sc.Scan() {
446			line := sc.Text()
447			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
448				continue
449			}
450			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
451			if payload == "[DONE]" {
452				sawDone = true
453				break
454			}
455			var d streamDelta
456			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &d) != nil || len(d.Choices) == 0 {
457				continue
458			}
459			if fr := d.Choices[0].FinishReason; fr != nil && *fr != "" {
460				finish = *fr
461			}
462			tok := d.Choices[0].Delta.Content
463			if tok == "" {
464				continue
465			}
466			full.WriteString(tok)
467			select {
468			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: tok}:
469			case <-ctx.Done():
470				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
471			}
472		}
473		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
474			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
475		}
476		complete := finish != "length" && (sawDone || finish == "stop")
477		return complete, nil
478	}
479	
480	// readAnthropicSSE parses an Anthropic Messages event stream, emitting a
481	// ChunkToken per text_delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports
482	// complete=true only when a terminal marker arrived (a message_stop event, or a
483	// message_delta with a natural stop_reason); a stream that closes with neither,
484	// or on stop_reason=="max_tokens", is treated as truncated.
485	func (e *LLMEngine) readAnthropicSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
486		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
487		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
488		sawStop := false
489		stopReason := ""
490		for sc.Scan() {
491			line := sc.Text()
492			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
493				continue // ignore "event:" and blank lines
494			}
495			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
496			if payload == "" {
497				continue
498			}
499			var ev anthropicStreamEvent
500			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &ev) != nil {
501				continue
502			}
503			if ev.Error != nil {
504				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ev.Error.Message)
505			}
506			switch ev.Type {
507			case "message_stop":
508				sawStop = true
509				continue
510			case "message_delta":
511				if ev.Delta.StopReason != "" {
512					stopReason = ev.Delta.StopReason
513				}
514				continue
515			}
516			if ev.Type != "content_block_delta" || ev.Delta.Type != "text_delta" || ev.Delta.Text == "" {
517				continue
518			}
519			full.WriteString(ev.Delta.Text)
520			select {
521			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: ev.Delta.Text}:
522			case <-ctx.Done():
523				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
524			}
525		}
526		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
527			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
528		}
529		complete := stopReason != "max_tokens" &&
530			(sawStop || stopReason == "end_turn" || stopReason == "stop_sequence")
531		return complete, nil
532	}
533	
534	// httpError reads an error body (best effort) into a descriptive error.
535	func (e *LLMEngine) httpError(resp *http.Response) error {
536		b, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 2<<10))
537		msg := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
538		if msg == "" {
539			msg = resp.Status
540		}
541		return fmt.Errorf("%s: http %d: %s", e.cfg.Name, resp.StatusCode, msg)
542	}
543
```

 `./internal/engine/google.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"net/url"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
14	)
15	
16	// GoogleConfig configures the free, unofficial Google translate_a/single endpoint.
17	type GoogleConfig struct {
18		Endpoint  string
19		ExtraDT   []string // extra dt slots, e.g. "bd" (bilingual dict), "at" (alternates)
20		UserAgent string
21		Timeout   time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// GoogleEngine is a keyless translation engine using Google's web endpoint.
25	// It is unofficial and best-effort: it can rate-limit (HTTP 429) or change shape
26	// without notice, so it lives at the end of the fallback chain.
27	type GoogleEngine struct {
28		cfg  GoogleConfig
29		http *http.Client
30	}
31	
32	// NewGoogle builds a Google engine with sensible defaults.
33	func NewGoogle(cfg GoogleConfig) *GoogleEngine {
34		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
35			cfg.Endpoint = "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single"
36		}
37		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
38			cfg.Timeout = 4 * time.Second
39		}
40		if cfg.UserAgent == "" {
41			cfg.UserAgent = "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli"
42		}
43		return &GoogleEngine{cfg: cfg, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
44	}
45	
46	// Name returns "google".
47	func (e *GoogleEngine) Name() string { return "google" }
48	
49	// Supports reports that Google handles translation only (not dictionary).
50	func (e *GoogleEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
51	
52	func (e *GoogleEngine) buildURL(req Request) string {
53		sl := req.Source
54		if sl == "" {
55			sl = "auto"
56		}
57		q := url.Values{}
58		q.Set("client", "gtx")
59		q.Set("sl", sl)
60		q.Set("tl", req.Target)
61		q.Set("dt", "t")
62		for _, dt := range e.cfg.ExtraDT {
63			q.Add("dt", dt)
64		}
65		q.Set("q", req.Text)
66		return e.cfg.Endpoint + "?" + q.Encode()
67	}
68	
69	// Translate performs one keyless translation (non-streaming).
70	func (e *GoogleEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
71		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
72			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
73		}
74		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.buildURL(req), nil)
75		if err != nil {
76			return nil, err
77		}
78		httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
79	
80		resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
81		if err != nil {
82			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: %w", err)), nil
83		}
84		defer resp.Body.Close()
85		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
86			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
87			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))), nil
88		}
89	
90		var data []json.RawMessage
91		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&data); err != nil {
92			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: decode: %w", err)), nil
93		}
94		res, err := parseGoogle(data, req)
95		if err != nil {
96			return single(nil, err), nil
97		}
98		res.Engine = e.Name()
99		return single(res, nil), nil
100	}
101	
102	// parseGoogle defensively walks the nested-array response. Positions: data[0] is
103	// an array of [translated, original, ...] segments (long text splits into many);
104	// data[2] is the detected source language.
105	func parseGoogle(data []json.RawMessage, req Request) (*TranslateResult, error) {
106		if len(data) < 1 {
107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: empty payload")
108		}
109		var segs []json.RawMessage
110		if err := json.Unmarshal(data[0], &segs); err != nil {
111			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: bad segments: %w", err)
112		}
113		var b strings.Builder
114		for _, s := range segs {
115			var parts []json.RawMessage
116			if json.Unmarshal(s, &parts) != nil || len(parts) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			var txt string
120			if json.Unmarshal(parts[0], &txt) == nil {
121				b.WriteString(txt)
122			}
123		}
124		res := &TranslateResult{Translation: strings.TrimSpace(b.String()), Target: req.Target}
125		if res.Translation == "" {
126			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: no translation in response")
127		}
128		if len(data) > 2 {
129			var det string
130			if json.Unmarshal(data[2], &det) == nil {
131				res.DetectedSource = det
132			}
133		}
134		return res, nil
135	}
136	
137	// Detect uses the offline detector (cheap, no network round-trip).
138	func (e *GoogleEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
139		return lang.Detect(text), nil
140	}
141	
142	// Available probes the endpoint with a tiny translation request.
143	func (e *GoogleEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
144		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
145		defer cancel()
146		u := e.buildURL(Request{Text: "a", Source: "auto", Target: "en"})
147		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
148		if err != nil {
149			return false
150		}
151		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
152		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
153		if err != nil {
154			return false
155		}
156		defer resp.Body.Close()
157		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
158		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
159	}
```

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
11	)
12	
13	// LocalDictConfig configures the offline bilingual dictionary engine.
14	type LocalDictConfig struct {
15		Dir          string
16		CedictURL    string
17		EcdictURL    string
18		AutoDownload bool        // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first Chinese lookup
19		Fuzzy        bool        // emit ranked suggestions on a miss
20		APIFallback  *DictEngine // nil unless configured; used for English when ECDICT is absent
21		Timeout      time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// LocalDictEngine looks up definitions offline: Chinese → CC-CEDICT (zh→en),
25	// English → ECDICT (en→zh). It satisfies the Engine interface and maps results
26	// into the same DictEntry/Suggestions shape the TUI already renders.
27	type LocalDictEngine struct {
28		cfg LocalDictConfig
29		ce  *cedictIndex
30		ec  *ecdictDB
31		wl  *wordIndex // /usr/share/dict/words, for English "did you mean"
32	}
33	
34	// NewLocalDict builds a local bilingual dictionary engine.
35	func NewLocalDict(cfg LocalDictConfig) *LocalDictEngine {
36		return &LocalDictEngine{
37			cfg: cfg,
38			ce:  newCedictIndex(CedictPath(cfg.Dir)),
39			ec:  newEcdictDB(EcdictDBPath(cfg.Dir)),
40			wl:  &wordIndex{path: "/usr/share/dict/words"},
41		}
42	}
43	
44	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Name() string         { return "dictionary" }
45	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
46	
47	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
48		return "", nil
49	}
50	
51	// Available reports true if at least one data source (or the API fallback) is usable.
52	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
53		if fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) || e.ec.available() {
54			return true
55		}
56		if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
57			return e.cfg.APIFallback.Available(ctx)
58		}
59		return false
60	}
61	
62	// Translate routes by script: Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT.
63	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
64		word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
65		if word == "" {
66			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
67		}
68		if lang.IsChinese(word) {
69			return e.lookupZh(ctx, word, req), nil
70		}
71		return e.lookupEn(ctx, word, req), nil
72	}
73	
74	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupZh(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
75		if !fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) {
76			if e.cfg.AutoDownload {
77				if err := DownloadCedict(ctx, e.cfg.CedictURL, CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir), nil); err != nil {
78					return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
79				}
80			} else {
81				return single(notInstalled(req, "CC-CEDICT not installed — run `translate dict update cedict`"), nil)
82			}
83		}
84		if entries := e.ce.lookup(word); len(entries) > 0 {
85			return single(cedictResult(word, entries, req), nil)
86		}
87		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
88			if sugg := e.ce.prefixSuggest(word, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
89				// CC-CEDICT prefix matches carry no edit distance → 0 (unknown).
90				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, 0), nil)
91			}
92		}
93		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
94	}
95	
96	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupEn(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
97		if !e.ec.available() {
98			// No ECDICT yet: fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (English defs) if enabled.
99			if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
100				ch, err := e.cfg.APIFallback.Translate(ctx, req)
101				if err == nil {
102					return ch
103				}
104			}
105			return single(notInstalled(req, "ECDICT not installed — run `translate dict update ecdict`"), nil)
106		}
107		en, err := e.ec.lookup(ctx, word)
108		if err != nil {
109			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
110		}
111		if en != nil {
112			return single(ecdictResult(en, req), nil)
113		}
114		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
115			if sugg, best := e.wl.nearestN(strings.ToLower(word), 2, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
116				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, best), nil)
117			}
118		}
119		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
120	}
121	
122	// --- result mapping into the existing DictEntry / Suggestions shape ---
123	
124	func cedictResult(word string, entries []*cedictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
125		d := &DictEntry{Word: word}
126		if len(entries) > 0 {
127			d.Phonetic = entries[0].Pinyin
128		}
129		for _, en := range entries {
130			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: en.Pinyin}
131			for _, def := range en.Defs {
132				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: def})
133			}
134			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
135		}
136		gloss := ""
137		if len(entries) > 0 && len(entries[0].Defs) > 0 {
138			gloss = entries[0].Defs[0]
139		}
140		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
141	}
142	
143	func ecdictResult(en *ecdictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
144		d := &DictEntry{Word: en.Word, Phonetic: en.Phonetic}
145		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
146			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "中文"}
147			for _, line := range zh {
148				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
149			}
150			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
151		}
152		if de := splitEcdict(en.Definition); len(de) > 0 {
153			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "definition"}
154			for _, line := range de {
155				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
156			}
157			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
158		}
159		gloss := ""
160		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
161			gloss = zh[0]
162		}
163		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
164	}
165	
166	func suggestResult(sugg []string, req Request, bestDist int) *TranslateResult {
167		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Suggestions: sugg, SuggestDistance: bestDist}
168	}
169	
170	func notInstalled(req Request, note string) *TranslateResult {
171		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Notes: note}
172	}
173	
174	func fileExists(p string) bool {
175		_, err := os.Stat(p)
176		return err == nil
177	}
178
```

 `./internal/tui/model.go`

```text
1	// Package tui implements the interactive Bubble Tea (v2, charm.land/*) front-end.
2	// It imports the shared engine layer but the engine layer never imports it.
3	package tui
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"time"
8	
9		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
10		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/list"
11		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
12		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textarea"
13		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
14		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
18	)
19	
20	// NamedEngine is one selectable engine in the TUI's ^e cycle.
21	type NamedEngine struct {
22		Name   string // "auto", "google", "dictionary", …
23		Engine engine.Engine
24		Mode   engine.Mode
25	}
26	
27	// Params configures a TUI session.
28	type Params struct {
29		Engines       []NamedEngine      // selectable engines; index 0 is the default
30		ModelSource   engine.ModelLister // fetches the model list for the model picker (may be nil)
31		ModelProvider string             // provider name the model override applies to
32		Store         store.Store        // nil when history is disabled
33		Source        string
34		Target        string
35		Pair          bool   // bidirectional pair mode
36		PairWith      string // the "away" language (resolved code)
37		Model         string // display model id for the footer (initial)
38		Preset        string // LLM prompt style
39		Instructions  string // extra system-prompt guidance
40		Live          bool   // live-debounce default state
41		DebounceMs    int
42	}
43	
44	type status int
45	
46	const (
47		statusIdle status = iota
48		statusTyping
49		statusTranslating
50		statusDone
51		statusError
52	)
53	
54	// focusPane selects which pane keyboard navigation drives. Global bindings
55	// (Enter, ^y, ^e, …) work regardless; only ordinary/scroll keys are routed.
56	type focusPane int
57	
58	const (
59		focusInput  focusPane = iota // typing + cursor navigation in the textarea (default)
60		focusOutput                  // keyboard scrolling of the result viewport
61	)
62	
63	// inputH is the fixed height (rows) of the input textarea box; shared by the
64	// layout and the click-to-focus hit test.
65	const inputH = 4
66	
67	// Model is the Bubble Tea model. All methods use value receivers and return the
68	// modified copy (the v2 convention) to avoid "state didn't stick" bugs.
69	type Model struct {
70		p    Params
71		base context.Context
72	
73		ta          textarea.Model
74		vp          viewport.Model
75		hist        list.Model
76		langList    list.Model
77		modelList   list.Model
78		suggestList list.Model
79		presetList  list.Model
80		sp          spinner.Model
81		keys        keyMap
82		st          styles
83	
84		width, height int
85		ready         bool
86		overlay       overlayKind
87		focus         focusPane // input (default) vs output; drives scroll/typing routing
88		resultH       int       // result viewport height (kept fixed to avoid layout jumps)
89	
90		source   string
91		target   string
92		pair     bool
93		pairWith string
94		preset   string
95		live     bool
96		engIdx   int // index into p.Engines (the active engine)
97	
98		// model picker state (session-cached)
99		cachedModels  []string
100		modelOverride string
101		modelsLoading bool
102		modelsErr     error
103	
104		// curEngine/curModel reflect the engine that actually served the last
105		// result (may differ from the selected engine when the auto chain falls back).
106		curEngine string
107		curModel  string
108	
109		status status
110		result *engine.TranslateResult
111		err    error
112		flash  string // transient footer notice (e.g. "copied ✓")
113	
114		// request lifecycle: one monotonic seq drives debounce-collapse, cancel, and
115		// stale-stream-drop. cancel/stream/streamBuf belong to the in-flight request.
116		// streamBuf is a plain string (NOT strings.Builder): the model is copied by
117		// value on every Update, and copying a used Builder panics.
118		seq         int
119		inflight    int
120		cancel      context.CancelFunc
121		stream      <-chan engine.Chunk
122		streamBuf   string
123		debounce    time.Duration
124		lastInput   string     // input text that produced the in-flight/last request
125		lastDoneKey cacheKey   // key of the last COMPLETED result (skip identical re-runs)
126		pendingKey  cacheKey   // cache key of the in-flight request
127		cache       cacheStore // session result cache (live cache-hit = instant, no API call)
128	
129		// Truncation auto-retry: a truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is
130		// re-fired once automatically, since a fresh request almost always completes.
131		// autoRetryKey is the pendingKey we've already retried once (so we retry at
132		// most once per input); retrying drives the "retrying" placeholder label.
133		autoRetryKey cacheKey
134		retrying     bool
135	}
136	
137	// active returns the currently selected engine.
138	func (m Model) active() NamedEngine {
139		if len(m.p.Engines) == 0 {
140			return NamedEngine{}
141		}
142		return m.p.Engines[m.engIdx%len(m.p.Engines)]
143	}
144	
145	// New builds a TUI model.
146	func New(ctx context.Context, p Params) Model {
147		ta := textarea.New()
148		ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
149		ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
150		ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
151		// Enter triggers translation, so move newline insertion to Alt+Enter.
152		ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"))
153		ta.Focus()
154	
155		debounce := time.Duration(p.DebounceMs) * time.Millisecond
156		if debounce <= 0 {
157			debounce = 400 * time.Millisecond
158		}
159	
160		hist := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
161		hist.Title = "History (↵ recall · esc close)"
162		hist.SetShowHelp(false)
163	
164		langList := list.New(langItems(), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
165		langList.Title = "Target language (↵ select · esc close)"
166		langList.SetShowHelp(false)
167	
168		modelList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
169		modelList.Title = "Model (↵ select · esc close)"
170		modelList.SetShowHelp(false)
171	
172		suggestList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
173		suggestList.Title = "Did you mean? (↵ look up · esc close)"
174		suggestList.SetShowHelp(false)
175	
176		preset := p.Preset
177		if preset == "" {
178			preset = engine.PresetConcise
179		}
180		presetList := list.New(presetItems(preset), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
181		presetList.Title = "Style (↵ select · esc close)"
182		presetList.SetShowHelp(false)
183	
184		sp := spinner.New()
185		sp.Spinner = spinner.MiniDot
186	
187		// Soft-wrap long paragraph lines to the viewport width; without this a single
188		// long (unwrapped) translation line is clipped and its tail is lost on screen.
189		vp := viewport.New()
190		vp.SoftWrap = true
191	
192		return Model{
193			p:           p,
194			base:        ctx,
195			ta:          ta,
196			vp:          vp,
197			hist:        hist,
198			langList:    langList,
199			modelList:   modelList,
200			suggestList: suggestList,
201			presetList:  presetList,
202			sp:          sp,
203			keys:        defaultKeys(),
204			st:          newStyles(),
205			source:      p.Source,
206			target:      p.Target,
207			pair:        p.Pair,
208			pairWith:    p.PairWith,
209			preset:      preset,
210			live:        p.Live,
211			status:      statusIdle,
212			debounce:    debounce,
213			cache:       cacheStore{},
214		}
215	}
216	
217	// Pair returns the current source and target language codes (read by the caller
218	// after the program exits to persist the last pair).
219	func (m Model) Pair() (source, target string) { return m.source, m.target }
220	
221	// Init requests the initial window size and starts the cursor blink + spinner.
222	func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
223		return tea.Batch(textarea.Blink, m.sp.Tick, tea.RequestWindowSize)
224	}
225
```

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		return nil
64	}
65	
66	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
67	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
68	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
69	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
70		cfg := res.Cfg
71		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
72			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
73		}
74		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
75			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
76		}
77		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
78	}
79	
80	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
81	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
82		d := res.Dictionary
83		if d == nil {
84			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
85				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
86			}
87			return res.Translation + "\n"
88		}
89		var b strings.Builder
90		head := d.Word
91		if d.Phonetic != "" {
92			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
93		}
94		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
95		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
96			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
97			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
98				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
99					break
100				}
101				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
102				if def.Example != "" {
103					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
104				}
105			}
106		}
107		return b.String()
108	}
109
```

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
8	)
9	
10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- ALWAYS translate into the target language. A single word, name, technical term, or
31	  loanword written in a DIFFERENT language must still be translated — never echo it back.
32	  Only when the text is ALREADY in the target language should you return it (lightly
33	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
34	
35	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
36	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
37	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
38	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
39	
40	Rules:
41	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
42	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
43	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
44	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
45	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
46	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
47	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
48	
49	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
50	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
51	that also gives brief usage examples.
52	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
53	
54	Rules:
55	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
56	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
57	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
58	- Format each example on two lines:
59	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
60	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
61	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
62	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
63	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
64	
65	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
66	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
67		switch preset {
68		case PresetContextual:
69			return translateSystemPromptContextual
70		case PresetDictionary:
71			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
72		default:
73			return translateSystemPromptConcise
74		}
75	}
76	
77	// pairDirective is appended to the system prompt in bidirectional pair mode. It
78	// makes the model own language detection (more robust than a pre-computed target
79	// for short/ambiguous input) and forbids echoing the input — the exact failure
80	// this fixes ("test" → "test" instead of "測試").
81	const pairDirective = `Bidirectional mode: the text is written in ONE of %s or %s. Detect which of the two it is, and translate it into the OTHER one. ALWAYS translate — never return the text unchanged, even for a single word, a proper name, a technical term, or a loanword.`
82	
83	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
84	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
85	Target language: %s
86	
87	Text:
88	%s`
89	
90	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
91	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
92	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
93	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
94		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
95		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
96			src = "auto"
97		} else {
98			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
99		}
100		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
101		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
102		// In pair mode, let the model detect the input language and route to the other
103		// side, and never echo — this is appended to (not a replacement of) the preset
104		// so the chosen output format (concise/contextual/dictionary) is preserved.
105		if req.Pair && req.PairHome != "" && req.PairAway != "" {
106			system += "\n\n" + fmt.Sprintf(pairDirective, lang.Name(req.PairHome), lang.Name(req.PairAway))
107		}
108		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
109			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
110		}
111		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
112	}
113
```

```text
1	// Package lang resolves language names/codes with typo tolerance and provides
2	// offline language detection. It is shared by the CLI and the TUI.
3	package lang
4	
5	import (
6		"sort"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/agnivade/levenshtein"
10	)
11	
12	// Lang is one known language: its ISO-639-1 code, English name, and aliases
13	// (native names, ISO-639-2 codes, common misspellings).
14	type Lang struct {
15		Code    string
16		Name    string
17		Aliases []string
18	}
19	
20	// Match is the result of resolving a query to a language.
21	type Match struct {
22		Code  string
23		Name  string
24		Score float64 // 1.0 == exact; lower for fuzzy
25		Exact bool
26	}
27	
28	// table is a pragmatic set of common languages. Fuzzy matching handles typos
29	// on the Latin names, so aliases mainly carry native scripts and ISO-639-2.
30	var table = []Lang{
31		{"en", "english", []string{"eng"}},
32		{"zh", "chinese", []string{"mandarin", "zho", "chi", "中文", "汉语", "漢語", "普通话"}},
33		{"zh-TW", "chinese (traditional)", []string{"traditional chinese", "chinese traditional", "zh-hant", "zht", "繁體中文", "繁体中文", "繁中", "taiwanese mandarin", "taiwan"}},
34		{"zh-CN", "chinese (simplified)", []string{"simplified chinese", "chinese simplified", "zh-hans", "zhs", "简体中文", "简中", "mainland chinese"}},
35		{"es", "spanish", []string{"spa", "español", "castellano"}},
36		{"fr", "french", []string{"fra", "fre", "français"}},
37		{"de", "german", []string{"deu", "ger", "deutsch"}},
38		{"ja", "japanese", []string{"jpn", "日本語"}},
39		{"ko", "korean", []string{"kor", "한국어", "조선말"}},
40		{"pt", "portuguese", []string{"por", "português"}},
41		{"it", "italian", []string{"ita", "italiano"}},
```
