67	
68	// candidate is one searchable token (code, name, or alias) pointing at a Lang.
69	type candidate struct {
70		token string
71		lang  *Lang
72		// weight scales the fuzzy score: exact code/name matches are preferred.
73		primary bool
74	}
75	
76	var candidates = buildCandidates()
77	
78	func buildCandidates() []candidate {
79		var cs []candidate
80		for i := range table {
81			l := &table[i]
82			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Code), lang: l, primary: true})
83			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Name), lang: l, primary: true})
84			for _, a := range l.Aliases {
85				cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(a), lang: l})
86			}
87		}
88		return cs
89	}
90	
91	// Resolve maps a free-form query to a language, tolerating typos. It returns the
92	// best match plus ranked alternatives (for TUI disambiguation). "auto" and ""
93	// pass through as an auto-detect sentinel (Code "auto", Exact true).
94	func Resolve(q string) (Match, []Match) {
95		norm := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(q))
96		if norm == "" || norm == "auto" {
97			return Match{Code: "auto", Name: "auto-detect", Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
98		}
99	
100		// Exact match on any code / name / alias.
101		for _, c := range candidates {
102			if c.token == norm {
103				return Match{Code: c.lang.Code, Name: c.lang.Name, Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
104			}
105		}
106	
107		// Fuzzy: rank candidates by normalized Levenshtein similarity.
108		type scored struct {
109			lang  *Lang
110			score float64
111		}
112		best := map[string]scored{} // dedupe by language code, keep best score
113		for _, c := range candidates {
114			d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(norm, c.token)
115			maxLen := max(len(norm), len(c.token))
116			if maxLen == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			sim := 1 - float64(d)/float64(maxLen)
120			if !c.primary {
121				sim -= 0.05 // slight preference for code/name over alias
122			}
123			if cur, ok := best[c.lang.Code]; !ok || sim > cur.score {
124				best[c.lang.Code] = scored{lang: c.lang, score: sim}
125			}
126		}
127	
128		ranked := make([]Match, 0, len(best))
129		for _, s := range best {
130			ranked = append(ranked, Match{Code: s.lang.Code, Name: s.lang.Name, Score: s.score})
131		}
132		sort.Slice(ranked, func(i, j int) bool { return ranked[i].Score > ranked[j].Score })
133	
134		if len(ranked) == 0 || ranked[0].Score < 0.5 {
135			// No confident match; echo the query back as an opaque code so callers
136			// can still attempt it (e.g. a code we don't have in the table).
137			return Match{Code: norm, Name: norm, Score: 0}, ranked
138		}
139		top := 3
140		if len(ranked) < top {
141			top = len(ranked)
142		}
143		return ranked[0], ranked[1:top]
144	}
145	
146	// List returns all known languages (for the target-language picker).
147	func List() []Lang {
148		out := make([]Lang, len(table))
149		copy(out, table)
150		return out
151	}
152	
153	// Name returns the English name for a code, or the code itself if unknown.
154	func Name(code string) string {
155		if strings.EqualFold(code, "auto") || code == "" {
156			return "auto-detect"
157		}
158		for i := range table {
159			if strings.EqualFold(table[i].Code, code) {
160				return table[i].Name
161			}
162		}
163		return code
164	}
165
```
</details></tool-use>

16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`

29	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
30	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
31	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
32	type Overlay struct {

34		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`

36		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`

116	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back

118	type SmartDict struct {

208	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
209	func Path() string {
210		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
211			return p
212		}
213		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
214	}
215	
216	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
217	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
218	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
219		p := Path()
220		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
221		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
222			cfg = Default()
223			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
224				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
225			}
226			return cfg, true, nil
227		}
228		if err != nil {
229			return nil, false, err
230		}
231		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
232		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
233			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
234		}
235		return cfg, false, nil
236	}
237	
238	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
239	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
240		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
241			return err
242		}
243		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
244		if err != nil {
245			return err
246		}
247		p := Path()
248		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
249		if err != nil {
250			return err
251		}
252		tmpName := tmp.Name()
253		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
254		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
255			tmp.Close()
256			return err
257		}
258		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
259			return err
260		}
261		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
262	}
263	
264	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
265	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
266		for i := range c.Providers {
267			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
268				return &c.Providers[i]
269			}
270		}
271		return nil
272	}
273	
274	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
275	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
276	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
277		switch tier {
278		case "fast":
279			if p.ModelFast != "" {
280				return p.ModelFast
281			}
282		case "max":
283			if p.ModelMax != "" {
284				return p.ModelMax
285			}
286		}
287		return p.Model
288	}

 `./internal/store/store.go`

```text
1	// Package store persists translation history. v1 uses an append-only JSONL file
2	// with in-memory fuzzy search behind the Store interface; a sqlite+FTS5 backend
3	// can drop in later without changing callers.
4	package store
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// Record is one history entry (also the JSONL line shape).
12	type Record struct {
13		ID           string    `json:"id"`
14		TS           time.Time `json:"ts"`
15		SourceLang   string    `json:"source_lang"`
16		TargetLang   string    `json:"target_lang"`
17		Engine       string    `json:"engine"`
18		Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
19		Input        string    `json:"input"`
20		Output       string    `json:"output"`
21		Alternatives []string  `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
22		Notes        string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
23		Favorite     bool      `json:"favorite,omitempty"`
24	}
25	
26	// Store is the history persistence interface.
27	type Store interface {
28		// Add assigns an ID and timestamp, persists the record, and returns it.
29		Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error)
30		// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
31		Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error)
32		// Search returns records fuzzy-matching query (over input+output), best first.
33		Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error)
34		// Close releases any resources.
35		Close() error
36	}
37
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/store/jsonl.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/rand"
6		"encoding/hex"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strconv"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/sahilm/fuzzy"
16	)
17	
18	// jsonlStore is an append-only JSONL-backed Store with in-memory fuzzy search.
19	type jsonlStore struct {
20		path string
21		mu   sync.Mutex
22	}
23	
24	// OpenJSONL opens (creating parent dirs for) a JSONL history file.
25	func OpenJSONL(path string) (Store, error) {
26		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o700); err != nil {
27			return nil, err
28		}
29		return &jsonlStore{path: path}, nil
30	}
31	
32	func (s *jsonlStore) Close() error { return nil }
33	
34	// Add stamps the record with an id + timestamp and appends it as one JSON line.
35	func (s *jsonlStore) Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error) {
36		if r.ID == "" {
37			r.ID = newID()
38		}
39		if r.TS.IsZero() {
40			r.TS = time.Now()
41		}
42		line, err := json.Marshal(r)
43		if err != nil {
44			return r, err
45		}
46	
47		s.mu.Lock()
48		defer s.mu.Unlock()
49		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o600)
50		if err != nil {
51			return r, err
52		}
53		defer f.Close()
54		if _, err := f.Write(append(line, '\n')); err != nil {
55			return r, err
56		}
57		return r, nil
58	}
59	
60	// readAll loads every record in chronological (file) order.
61	func (s *jsonlStore) readAll() ([]Record, error) {
62		s.mu.Lock()
63		defer s.mu.Unlock()
64		b, err := os.ReadFile(s.path)
65		if err != nil {
66			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
67				return nil, nil
68			}
69			return nil, err
70		}
71		var out []Record
72		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
73			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
74			if line == "" {
75				continue
76			}
77			var r Record
78			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &r) == nil {
79				out = append(out, r)
80			}
81		}
82		return out, nil
83	}
84	
85	// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
86	func (s *jsonlStore) Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
87		all, err := s.readAll()
88		if err != nil {
89			return nil, err
90		}
91		reverse(all)
92		if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
93			all = all[:limit]
94		}
95		return all, nil
96	}
97	
98	// Search fuzzy-ranks records by "input ⏎ output", best match first.
99	func (s *jsonlStore) Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
100		all, err := s.readAll()
101		if err != nil {
102			return nil, err
103		}
104		q := strings.TrimSpace(query)
105		if q == "" {
106			reverse(all)
107			if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
108				all = all[:limit]
109			}
110			return all, nil
111		}
112		hay := make([]string, len(all))
113		for i, r := range all {
114			hay[i] = r.Input + " " + r.Output
115		}
116		matches := fuzzy.Find(q, hay)
117		out := make([]Record, 0, len(matches))
118		for _, m := range matches {
119			out = append(out, all[m.Index])
120			if limit > 0 && len(out) >= limit {
121				break
122			}
123		}
124		return out, nil
125	}
126	
127	func reverse(rs []Record) {
128		for i, j := 0, len(rs)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
129			rs[i], rs[j] = rs[j], rs[i]
130		}
131	}
132	
133	// newID is a sortable-enough unique id: base36 nanos + 4 random bytes.
134	func newID() string {
135		var b [4]byte
136		_, _ = rand.Read(b[:])
137		return strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 36) + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(b[:])
138	}
139
```
</details></tool-use>

5	// cacheKey identifies a translation result for the session cache. It is
6	// comparable, so it can be a map key. Keying on the *selected* engine name
7	// (e.g. "auto") means a chain fallback still caches under the user-visible engine.
8	type cacheKey struct {
9		preset, engineName, model, source, target, text string
10	}
11	
12	// cacheKeyFor builds the key for the given input text under the current settings.
13	func (m Model) cacheKeyFor(text string) cacheKey {
14		return cacheKey{
15			preset:     m.preset,
16			engineName: m.active().Name,
17			model:      m.modelOverride,
18			source:     m.source,
19			target:     m.target,
20			text:       text,
21		}
22	}
23	
24	// cacheStore is the session result cache. Session-scoped and unbounded (small);
25	// LLM output is nondeterministic so it is not persisted across sessions.
26	type cacheStore map[cacheKey]*engine.TranslateResult

<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

`sed -n '1,120p' /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/README.md`

```text
# translate

A fast CLI/TUI translation tool for the terminal — one-shot from the shell or an
interactive live-translating TUI. Backed by an auto fallback chain over your
local LLM providers (copilot-proxy → Ollama), a free web API (Google), and a
dictionary, with typo-tolerant language resolution and translation history.

## Install

```sh
go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest   # into $GOBIN (or ~/go/bin)
```

## Build & run

```sh
go build -o translate .      # Go 1.25+ (developed on 1.26)
./translate "hola mundo" --to en          # one-shot
echo "bonjour" | ./translate --to en      # pipe
./translate                               # interactive TUI
```

First run writes a default config to `~/.config/translate/config.toml`; run
`./translate init` for a guided setup that probes which providers are up.

## Usage

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `translate <text>` / `… \| translate` | One-shot translation (streams to a TTY; plain text when piped) |
| `translate` (no args, TTY) | Interactive TUI |
| `translate --to <lang> --from <lang>` | Language override; both are fuzzy (`chinees` → `zh`) |
| `translate --json` | Emit the full structured result |
| `translate define <word>` | Dictionary lookup (bilingual: zh↔en local, or English API) |
| `translate history` / `history search <q>` | Recent history / fuzzy search (`--tsv`, `--json`) |
| `translate init` | Interactive config wizard (probes providers) |
| `translate config path\|show` · `lang resolve <q>` | Introspection helpers |

Flags: `--engine smartauto|auto|<provider>|google`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--tier default|fast|max`, `--preset concise|contextual|dictionary`, `--instructions`, `--pair`/`--pair-with`, `--no-history`, `--debug`.
Env overrides: `TRANSLATE_TARGET`, `TRANSLATE_SOURCE`, `TRANSLATE_ENGINE`, `TRANSLATE_PROVIDER`, `TRANSLATE_MODEL`, `TRANSLATE_CONFIG`, `TRANSLATE_DEBUG`.
Precedence: **flag > env > `[cli]`/`[tui]` > `[general]` > default**.

### Per-front-end defaults (`[cli]` / `[tui]`)

The CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI share `[general]`, but
either can override any subset of it via an optional `[cli]` or `[tui]` table — e.g.
a snappy concise CLI and a richer, higher-tier TUI:

```toml
[general]
preset = "contextual"
tier   = "fast"

[cli]                 # one-shot / piped translation
preset = "concise"

[tui]                 # interactive front-end
tier          = "max"
live_translate = true
```

Only keys you list are overridden; the rest inherit `[general]`. Flags and env vars
still win over both.

## TUI keys

`↵` translate · live-debounce auto-translates ~700ms after you stop typing (off by default) ·
`^y` copy · `^l` toggle live · `^e` cycle engine · `^t` target language · `^p` model ·
`^o` style · `^g` toggle pair mode · `^u` clear · `⇥` switch input/output focus · `^r` history · `⌥↵` newline · `^c`/`esc` quit.

`⇥` (Tab) moves keyboard focus between the input box and the result box; the focused
box gets an accent border. With the result focused, `↑/↓ j/k PgUp/PgDn g/G space` scroll
it; typing any character snaps focus back to the input. A mouse click focuses the box
it lands in.

## Engines & fallback

The `auto` engine tries `chain.order` in turn, failing over **before the first
token** (so a down provider switches cleanly; a mid-stream error surfaces rather
than restarting):

- **copilot-proxy** — OpenAI-compatible at `http://localhost:4141/v1`, no API key.
  Default tier is **fast** (`claude-haiku-4-5`); `--tier default` → `claude-sonnet-5`,
  `--tier max` → `claude-opus-4-8`. Claude models are served only via the proxy's
  Anthropic Messages endpoint (`/v1/messages`) — the engine routes `claude-*` ids
  there automatically (they 400 on `/chat/completions`).
- **Ollama** — `http://localhost:11434/v1` (offline; `llama3.2:3b`).
- **Google** — free, keyless; also reports the detected source language.
- **openrouter** — configured but off the default chain; needs `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.

> ⚠️ Using a GitHub Copilot subscription to back non-GitHub tools (copilot-proxy)
> may violate Copilot's Terms of Service. Reorder `chain.order` (drop `copilot`,
> lead with `ollama`/`google`) in the config if that matters to you.

Config lives at `~/.config/translate/config.toml`, history at
`~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl`, last-pair state at
`~/.local/state/translate/state.json` (XDG-honored on macOS and Linux).

### Smart auto (`smartauto`)

The recommended default (choose it in `translate init`). It routes by input shape:
a **single word/term** is looked up in the dictionary (with the same LLM fallback as
`smart-dict` on a miss), while a **phrase or sentence** is translated by the LLM. Set
`engine = "smartauto"` in `[general]` (or `--engine smartauto`). It composes with
pair mode, so a single word and a full sentence both translate in the right direction.

### Pair mode (bidirectional)

With `pair = true` + `pair_with`, the tool detects which of the two languages your
input is in and translates it into the **other** — one box, both directions
(`你好 → hi`, `test → 測試`). For a CJK/non-CJK pair (e.g. `zh-TW`⇄`en`) direction is
decided by script, so even short input routes correctly; the LLM translate prompt is
also told to detect-and-route and to **never echo** the input. `pair_with` must differ
from the target (`translate init` enforces this; a degenerate config warns).

### Debugging

`--debug` (or `TRANSLATE_DEBUG=1`, or `[general] debug = true`) logs the intermediate
decisions — resolved settings, pair routing, word-vs-phrase classification, dictionary
hit/miss, and chain fallback. The one-shot CLI logs to **stderr**; the TUI logs to
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
8		lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
12	)
13	
14	// View renders the two-box layout (input, result) plus a status/help footer.
15	// AltScreen + mouse are set declaratively (the v2 way).
16	func (m Model) View() tea.View {
17		if !m.ready {
18			return altView("initializing…")
19		}
20		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
21			return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, m.overlayBody(), m.overlayFooter()))
22		}
23	
24		inStyle := m.st.input
25		if m.ta.Focused() {
26			inStyle = m.st.inputHi
27		}
28		input := inStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Render(m.ta.View())
29	
30		// The result box is kept at a fixed height (viewport height) so the layout
31		// never jumps between the "translating…" placeholder and streamed output.
32		resStyle := m.st.result
33		if m.focus == focusOutput {
34			resStyle = m.st.resultHi
35		}
36		result := resStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Height(m.resultH).Render(m.vp.View())
37		footer := m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.statusLine())
38	
39		return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, input, result, footer))
40	}
41	
42	// overlayBody renders the active picker's list (or its loading/empty state).
43	func (m Model) overlayBody() string {
44		switch m.overlay {
45		case overlayHistory:
46			return m.hist.View()
47		case overlayLang:
48			return m.langList.View()
49		case overlayModel:
50			switch {
51			case m.modelsLoading:
52				return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("loading models…")
53			case m.modelsErr != nil:
54				return m.st.warn.Render("⚠ " + m.modelsErr.Error())
55			case len(m.cachedModels) == 0:
56				return m.st.warn.Render("no models available (is the provider up?)")
57			default:
58				return m.modelList.View()
59			}
60		case overlaySuggest:
61			return m.suggestList.View()
62		case overlayPreset:
63			return m.presetList.View()
64		}
65		return ""
66	}
67	
68	func (m Model) overlayFooter() string {
69		hint := "↵ select  esc close  ^c quit"
70		switch m.overlay {
71		case overlayHistory:
72			hint = "↵ recall  esc close  ^c quit"
73		case overlaySuggest:
74			hint = "↵ look up  esc close  ^c quit"
75		}
76		return m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.st.dim.Render(hint))
77	}
78	
79	// altView wraps content in a full-screen (alt-screen) tea.View with mouse on.
80	func altView(content string) tea.View {
81		v := tea.NewView(content)
82		v.AltScreen = true
83		v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
84		return v
85	}
86	
87	// statusLine renders the footer with the current transient state.
88	func (m Model) statusLine() string { return m.footerContent(false) }
89	
90	// footerHeight measures how many rows the footer occupies at the current width.
91	// It reserves the widest transient state ("translating") so the layout never
92	// jumps when that segment appears mid-flight.
93	func (m Model) footerHeight() int {
94		if m.width <= 0 {
95			return 1
96		}
97		return lg.Height(m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.footerContent(true)))
98	}
99	
100	// footerContent builds the footer string. forceState reserves the transient
101	// "translating" segment (for height measurement); otherwise the actual state is
102	// shown. lipgloss word-wraps it to the width, so narrow terminals get 2+ rows.
103	func (m Model) footerContent(forceState bool) string {
104		live := m.st.liveOff.Render("live○")
105		if m.live {
106			live = m.st.liveOn.Render("live●")
107		}
108	
109		var state string
110		if forceState {
111			state = m.st.dim.Render("⠿ translating")
112		} else if m.flash != "" {
113			state = m.st.liveOn.Render(m.flash)
114		} else {
115			switch m.status {
116			case statusTranslating:
117				state = m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("translating")
118			case statusError:
119				state = m.st.errText.Render("error")
120			}
121		}
122	
123		// Dictionary mode: no source→target pair (English-only lookups per script).
124		if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
125			left := strings.Join([]string{
126				m.st.label.Render("dictionary (zh↔en)"),
127				live,
128			}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
129			if state != "" {
130				left += "  " + state
131			}
132			return left + "  " + m.st.dim.Render("↵ define  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
133		}
134	
135		pair := fmt.Sprintf("%s→%s", lang.Name(m.source), lang.Name(m.target))
136		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
137			pair = fmt.Sprintf("pair %s⇄%s", m.target, m.pairWith) // compact codes
138		}
139	
140		// Engine segment: the selected engine name, plus the model that actually
141		// served the last result (once known).
142		engineSeg := m.active().Name
143		if m.curEngine != "" && m.curEngine != m.active().Name {
144			engineSeg = m.curEngine // chain fell back to a specific engine
145		}
146		if m.curModel != "" {
147			engineSeg = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", engineSeg, m.curModel)
148		}
149	
150		left := strings.Join([]string{
151			m.st.label.Render(pair),
152			m.st.dim.Render(engineSeg),
153			m.st.dim.Render("style:" + m.preset),
154			live,
155		}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
156		if state != "" {
157			left += "  " + state
158		}
159	
160		help := m.st.dim.Render("↵ translate  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^t lang  ^p model  ^o style  ^g pair  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
161		return left + "  " + help
162	}
163	
164	// renderResult formats a completed translation, a dictionary entry, or a ranked
165	// "did you mean" list when a dictionary lookup missed.
166	func (m Model) renderResult(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
167		if res.Dictionary != nil {
168			return m.renderDictionary(res)
169		}
170		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
171			return m.renderSuggestions(res)
172		}
173		var b strings.Builder
174		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(res.Translation))
175	
176		if res.DetectedSource != "" && (m.source == "" || m.source == "auto") {
177			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("detected: "+lang.Name(res.DetectedSource)))
178		}
179		for _, a := range res.Alternatives {
180			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("~ "+a))
181		}
182		if res.Notes != "" {
183			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+res.Notes))
184		}
185		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
186			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("⚠ "+w))
187		}
188		// A truncation warning already tells the user to press Enter to retry; the
189		// engine-switch hint only fits chain-fallback warnings, so skip it there.
190		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && !res.Truncated {
191			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("(^e switch engine · check the model/provider)"))
192		}
193		return b.String()
194	}
195	
196	// renderDictionary formats a dictionary lookup for the result pane.
197	func (m Model) renderDictionary(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
198		d := res.Dictionary
199		var b strings.Builder
200		head := d.Word
201		if d.Phonetic != "" {
202			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
203		}
204		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(head))
205		for _, mn := range d.Meanings {
206			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render(mn.PartOfSpeech))
207			for i, def := range mn.Definitions {
208				if i >= 3 {
209					break
210				}
211				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("• "+def.Text))
212			}
213		}
214		return b.String()
215	}
216	
217	// renderSuggestions renders the ranked "did you mean" list for a dictionary miss.
218	func (m Model) renderSuggestions(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
219		var b strings.Builder
220		b.WriteString(m.st.dim.Render("no exact match — did you mean:"))
221		for i, w := range res.Suggestions {
222			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d.", i+1)) + " " + m.st.alt.Render(w))
223		}
224		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("↵ choose"))
225		return b.String()
226	}
227
```
</details></tool-use>

1	// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
2	//
3	// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
4	// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
5	// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
6	// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
7	// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
8	package xdgpath
9	
10	import (
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13	)
14	
15	// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
16	const app = "translate"
17	
18	// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
19	func base(env, def string) string {
20		if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
21			return v
22		}
23		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
24		if err != nil {
25			home = "."
26		}
27		return filepath.Join(home, def)
28	}
29	
30	// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
31	func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }
32	
33	// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
34	func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }
35	
36	// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
37	func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }
38	
39	// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
40	func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }
41	
42	// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
43	func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }
44	
45	// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
46	func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }
47	
48	// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
49	func EnsureDirs() error {
50		for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
51			if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
52				return err
53			}
54		}
55		return nil
56	}

While I wait, here's what the research surfaced for **free TTS options** relevant to a dictionary/translate tool:

**Offline (no network, no API key):**
- **macOS `say`** — built into this machine (darwin), zero setup, has Mandarin (Ting-Ting) + many voices. Pipe to `afplay` not even needed.
- **espeak-ng** — tiny, phoneme-level, robotic but instant; good for IPA.
- **Piper** — neural offline, better quality, needs per-voice model downloads.

**Online (free, no key, high quality):**
- **Google Translate TTS** endpoint (what `gTTS` wraps) — free, multilingual, naturally matches a translate tool's languages; can be rate-limited.
- **edge-tts** — Microsoft Edge neural voices, free, high quality.

Let me wait for the codebase exploration to complete before finalizing the plan.

I have a comprehensive picture of the architecture. Here is my report.

## Architecture Map: `translate` CLI/TUI

### 1. Language & framework
**Go** (module `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, Go 1.26.4 in `go.mod`, README says 1.25+). Key dependencies:
- **CLI framework:** `github.com/spf13/cobra` (+ `pflag`)
- **TUI:** Charm's **Bubble Tea v2** ecosystem under the new `charm.land/*` import paths — `charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `charm.land/bubbles/v2` (textarea, viewport, list, spinner, key), `charm.land/lipgloss/v2`, `charm.land/huh/v2` (forms/wizard)
- **Support libs:** `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go SQLite for ECDICT), `atotto/clipboard`, `whatlanggo` + `sahilm/fuzzy` + `agnivade/levenshtein` (language detect / fuzzy match), `pelletier/go-toml/v2` (config)

### 2. Main entry points & key files
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/main.go` — thin `main()`; sets up a cancellable `signal.NotifyContext` and calls `cmd.Execute(ctx)`.
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/root.go` — the heart of dispatch. `runRoot` decides between three modes: one-shot CLI (args), piped stdin (non-TTY), or interactive TUI (TTY, no args). Contains `oneShot`, `runTUI`, engine building, history recording.
- **TUI entry:** `runTUI` in `cmd/root.go` builds `tui.Params` and runs `tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p))`. The model lives in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/model.go`.
- **CLI subcommands** (all in `cmd/`): `define.go` (dictionary lookup), `config.go`, `lang.go`, `history.go`, `init.go` (huh wizard), `dictupdate.go` (`dict update cedict|ecdict`), `version.go`, `build.go`. Registered in `NewRootCmd` via `root.AddCommand(...)`.

### 3. Directory / module structure
```
main.go                     entry
cmd/                        cobra command tree (CLI front-end)
internal/
  config/    config.go, resolve.go   TOML config + precedence resolution (flag>env>[cli]/[tui]>[general])
  engine/    the shared backend abstraction (see below)
  lang/      detect.go, lang.go      fuzzy language resolution, script detection, pair routing
  tui/       Bubble Tea front-end (model/update/view/keys/styles/pickers/msgs/cache/history)
  store/     store.go, jsonl.go      history persistence (JSONL; sqlite backend option)
  state/     state.go                last-pair state (state.json)
  xdgpath/   paths.go                XDG config/data/state/cache dirs
  debug/     debug.go                gated decision logging
```
The **engine layer is the core abstraction** and is UI-agnostic: `internal/engine/engine.go` documents that it "imports zero TUI code" — both the CLI and TUI drive identical `engine.Engine` values and diverge only at presentation.

### 4. TUI library
Bubble Tea v2 (value-receiver model convention). `Model` (in `internal/tui/model.go`) composes a `textarea`, result `viewport`, several `list.Model` pickers (history, language, model, suggestions, preset), and a spinner. Files: `model.go` (state), `update.go` (Update loop / key handling / clipboard), `view.go` (rendering incl. `renderDictionary`/`renderResult`/`renderSuggestions`), `keys.go` (keymap), `styles.go`, `pickers.go`, `msgs.go`, `cache.go`, `history.go`. Keybindings are Ctrl-chords defined in `defaultKeys()` (`^y` copy, `^e` engine, `^t` lang, `^p` model, `^o` style, `^g` pair, etc.).

### 5. CLI framework mechanics
**Cobra.** `NewRootCmd()` (cmd/root.go:47) builds the root with `Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs` so bare text (`translate "hola"`) falls through to translation while real subcommands still route. Persistent flags (`--to/-t`, `--from/-f`, `--engine`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--tier`, `--preset`, `--pair`, `--json`, `--debug`, etc.) are collected into `config.Overrides` by `overrides()` and merged by `cfg.Resolve(...)`. `Execute(ctx)` runs `ExecuteContext`.

### 6. Build / run
From the **Justfile** (`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/Justfile`):
- `just build` → `go build -o translate .`
- `just run *ARGS` (e.g. `just run "hola mundo" --to en`), `just tui`, `just define WORD`, `just init`
- `just check` = `fmt` + `vet` + `build`; `just test` = `go test ./...`; `just install` copies to `~/.dotfiles/bin/translate`
- README: `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest`, or `go build -o translate .` then `./translate ...`. First run writes `~/.config/translate/config.toml`.

---

### Directly relevant context for a TTS/pronunciation feature (describing what exists, not proposing changes)

The engine already carries **pronunciation data** you'd feed to TTS:
- `engine.DictEntry` (in `internal/engine/engine.go`) has a `Phonetic string` field (IPA-style). It's populated three ways:
  - Free Dictionary API path (`internal/engine/dict.go`, `mergeEntries`) — IPA text from `phonetics[].text`.
  - CC-CEDICT (Chinese) path (`internal/engine/localdict.go`, `cedictResult`) — sets `Phonetic` to the entry's **Pinyin**; also uses Pinyin as the `PartOfSpeech` label per meaning.
  - ECDICT (English→Chinese) path (`ecdictResult`) — sets `Phonetic` from the ECDICT `phonetic` column.
- Dictionary lookups flow through `engine.Request{Mode: engine.ModeDict}` and return a `TranslateResult` whose `Dictionary *DictEntry` holds `Word` + `Phonetic` + `Meanings`.

Where it surfaces / where an action would hook in:
- **CLI:** `renderDict` in `cmd/define.go` prints `Word` + `Phonetic` on the head line.
- **TUI:** `renderDictionary` in `internal/tui/view.go` (lines ~197-205) renders the head with phonetic; the TUI already has a precedent for a "produce an artifact from the current result" keybinding — the **`^y` copy** action in `internal/tui/update.go` (~line 216, `keys.Copy` → `clipboard.WriteAll(m.copyText())`, `copyText()` at update.go:440).

External-process pattern: the codebase currently **shells out to nothing** — no `os/exec` usage anywhere (only `atotto/clipboard` is used for the copy feature). So there is no existing pattern for invoking a system TTS binary (`say`, `espeak`, etc.); audio playback would be new territory.

Config extensibility: config is TOML via `internal/config/config.go` with per-feature structs (`Dict`, `SmartDict`, `Google`, etc.) and a `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay mechanism (`Overlay` struct) — a natural place a TTS feature's settings would slot in.
</output>
```
</details></tool-use>

57			SilenceErrors: true,
58			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
59			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
60			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
61			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
62			RunE: runRoot,
63		}
64		f := root.PersistentFlags()
65		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
66		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
67		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
68		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
69		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
70		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
71		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
72		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
73		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
74		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
75		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
76		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
77		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
78	
79		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
80		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd())
81		return root
82	}
83	
84	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
85	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
86		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
87	}
88	
89	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
90	func overrides() config.Overrides {
91		return config.Overrides{
92			Source:       flagFrom,
93			Target:       flagTo,
94			Engine:       flagEngine,
95			Provider:     flagProvider,
96			Model:        flagModel,
97			Tier:         flagTier,
98			Preset:       flagPreset,
99			Instructions: flagInstructions,
100			Pair:         flagPair,
101			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
102			Debug:        flagDebug,
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
107	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
108	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
109	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
110		if len(args) > 0 {
111			return config.ModeCLI
112		}
113		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
114			return config.ModeCLI
115		}
116		return config.ModeTUI
117	}
118	
119	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
120		ctx := cmd.Context()
121	
122		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
123		if err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126		if created {
127			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
128		}
129	
130		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
131		if res.Debug {
132			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
133			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
134			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
135				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
136			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
137				debug.Enable(w)
138			}
139		}
140		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
141		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
142		pairWith := ""
143		if res.Pair {
144			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
145			pairWith = pwm.Code
146		}
147		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s",
148			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith)
149		if res.Pair && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
150			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
151		}
152	
153		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
154			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
155		}
156		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
157		if err != nil {
158			return err
159		}
160	
161		st := openStore(cfg)
162		if st != nil {
163			defer st.Close()
164		}
165	
166		switch {
167		case len(args) > 0:
168			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
169			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
170			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
171			if err != nil {
172				return err
173			}
174			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
175			return nil
176	
177		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
178			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
179			if err != nil {
180				return err
181			}
182			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
183			if text == "" {
184				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
185			}
186			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
187			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
188			if err != nil {
189				return err
190			}
191			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
192			return nil
193	
194		default:
195			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
196		}
197	}
198	
199	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
200	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
201		if pair && away != "" {
202			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
203			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
204			return t
205		}
206		return home
207	}
208	
209	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
210	func clip(s string, n int) string {
211		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
212		r := []rune(s)
213		if len(r) > n {
214			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
215		}
216		return s
217	}
218	
219	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
220	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
221	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
222		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
223			return nil
224		}
225		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
226			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
227		if err != nil {
228			return nil
229		}
230		return f
231	}
232	
233	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
234	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
235	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
236		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
237			return
238		}
239		stt, err := state.Load()
240		if err != nil || stt == nil {
241			return
242		}
243		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
244			res.Source = stt.Source
245			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
246		}
247		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
248			res.Target = stt.Target
249			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
250		}
251	}
252	
253	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
254	// suppressed with --no-history.
255	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
256		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
257			return nil
258		}
259		path := cfg.History.Path
260		if path == "" {
261			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
262		}
263		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
264		if err != nil {
265			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
266			return nil
267		}
268		return st
269	}
270	
271	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
272	//
273	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
274	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
275	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
276	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
277		if st != nil {
278			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
279		}
280		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
281			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
282		}
283	}
284	
285	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
286	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
287		src := source
288		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
289			src = res.DetectedSource
290		}
291		return store.Record{
292			SourceLang:   src,
293			TargetLang:   target,
294			Engine:       res.Engine,
295			Model:        res.Model,
296			Input:        input,
297			Output:       res.Translation,
298			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
299			Notes:        res.Notes,
300		}
301	}
302	
303	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
304	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
305		mode := "fixed"
306		if source == "auto" {
307			mode = "auto"
308		}
309		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
310			Source:     source,
311			Target:     target,
312			SourceMode: mode,
313			Engine:     engineName,
314		})
315	}
316	
317	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
318	// pair on exit.
319	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
320		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
321		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
322		modelProvider := ""
323		if res.Provider != nil {
324			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
325			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
326		}
327		p := tui.Params{
328			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
329			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
330			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
331			Store:         st,
332			Source:        source,
333			Target:        target,
334			Pair:          res.Pair,
335			PairWith:      pairWith,
336			Model:         res.Model,
337			Preset:        res.Preset,
338			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
339			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
340			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
341		}
342		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
343		if err != nil {
344			return err
345		}
346		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
347			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
348				s, t := fm.Pair()
349				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
350			}
351		}
352		return nil
353	}
354	
355	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
356	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
357	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
358		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
359		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
360		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
361			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
362		}
363		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
364			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
365		}
366		return sm.Code, tm.Code
367	}
368	
369	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
370	//
371	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
372	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
373	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
374	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
375		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
376		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON
377	
378		req := engine.Request{
379			Text:     text,
380			Source:   source,
381			Target:   target,
382			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
383			Stream:   stream,
384			Preset:   preset,
385			Extra:    instructions,
386			Pair:     pair,
387			PairHome: pairHome,
388			PairAway: pairAway,
389		}
390		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
391		if err != nil {
392			return nil, err
393		}
394	
395		var onTok func(string)
396		if stream {
397			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
398		}
399		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
400		if err != nil {
401			if stream {
402				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
403			}
404			return nil, err
405		}
406	
407		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
408		// failed and why, and how to switch.
409		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
410			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
411		}
412		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
413			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
414		}
415	
416		switch {
417		case flagJSON:
418			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
419			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
420			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
421				return nil, err
422			}
423		case stream:
424			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
425		default:
426			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
427		}
428		return res, nil
429	}
430
```

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	}
46	
47	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
48	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
49	type TranslateResult struct {
50		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
51		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
52		Target         string   `json:"target"`
53		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
54		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
55		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
56	
57		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
58		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
59		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
60	
61		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
62		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
63		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
64		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
65		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
66	
67		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
68		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
69		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
70	
71		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
72		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
73		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
74		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
75		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
76		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
77	
78		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
79		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
80		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
81		// Transient: not serialized.
82		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
83	}
84	
85	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
86	type DictEntry struct {
87		Word      string    `json:"word"`
88		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
89		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
90		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
91	}
92	
93	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
94	type Meaning struct {
95		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
96		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
97		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
98		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
99	}
100	
101	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
102	type Definition struct {
103		Text    string `json:"definition"`
104		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
105	}
106	
107	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
108	type ChunkKind int
109	
110	const (
111		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
112		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
113		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
114		ChunkDone
115		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
116		ChunkError
117	)
118	
119	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
120	type Chunk struct {
121		Kind   ChunkKind
122		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
123		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
124		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
125	}
126	
127	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
128	//
129	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
130	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
131	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
132	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
133	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
134	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
135	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
136	type Engine interface {
137		Name() string
138		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
139		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
140		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
141		Supports(m Mode) bool
142	}
143	
144	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
145	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
146	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
147		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
148		if err != nil {
149			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
150		} else {
151			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
152		}
153		close(ch)
154		return ch
155	}
156	
157	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
158	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
159	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
160	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
161		for c := range ch {
162			switch c.Kind {
163			case ChunkToken:
164				if onToken != nil {
165					onToken(c.Text)
166				}
167			case ChunkDone:
168				return c.Result, nil
169			case ChunkError:
170				return nil, c.Err
171			}
172		}
173		return nil, ErrNoResult
174	}
175
```
</details></tool-use>

8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).

11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
21	}
22	
23	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
24	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
25	type Mode int
26	
27	const (
28		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
29		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
30	)
31	
32	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
33	type Resolved struct {
34		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
35		Target       string
36		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
37		Provider     *Provider
38		Model        string
39		Tier         string
40		Preset       string
41		Instructions string
42		Pair         bool
43		PairWith     string
44		Stream       bool
45		Color        string
46		Debug        bool
47	
48		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
49		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
50		LiveTranslate bool
51		DebounceMs    int
52	
53		Cfg *Config
54	}
55	
56	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
57	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
58	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
59	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
60	
61	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
62	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
63		switch mode {
64		case ModeCLI:
65			return c.CLI
66		case ModeTUI:
67			return c.TUI
68		}
69		return nil
70	}
71	
72	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
73	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
74	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
75		if ov == nil {
76			return
77		}
78		if ov.Engine != nil {
79			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
80		}
81		if ov.Tier != nil {
82			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
83		}
84		if ov.Preset != nil {
85			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
86		}
87		if ov.Instructions != nil {
88			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
89		}
90		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
91			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
92		}
93		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
94			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
95		}
96		if ov.Pair != nil {
97			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
98		}
99		if ov.PairWith != nil {
100			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
101		}
102		if ov.Stream != nil {
103			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
104		}
105		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
106			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
107		}
108		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
109			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
110		}
111		if ov.Debug != nil {
112			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
113		}
114	}
115	
116	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
117	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
118	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
119		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
120		// the on-disk config is untouched).
121		g := c.General
122		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
123		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
124	
125		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
126			if flag != "" {
127				return flag
128			}
129			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
130				return v
131			}
132			return cfgVal
133		}
134	
135		r := Resolved{
136			Cfg:           c,
137			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
138			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
139			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
140			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
141			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
142			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
143			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
144			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
145			Color:         g.Color,
146			Stream:        g.Stream,
147			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
148			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
149			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
150		}
151	
152		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
153		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
154		if provName == "" {
155			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
156				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
157			} else {
158				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
159			}
160		}
161		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
162	
163		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
164		// provider's tier model.
165		cfgModel := ""
166		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
167			cfgModel = *ov.Model
168		}
169		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
170		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
171			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
172		}
173		return r
174	}
175	
176	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
177	// else the first configured provider, else "".
178	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
179		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
180			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
181				return name
182			}
183		}
184		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
185			return c.Providers[0].Name
186		}
187		return ""
188	}
189

12	
13	func newDictCmd() *cobra.Command {
14		c := &cobra.Command{
15			Use:   "dict",
16			Short: "Manage the local bilingual dictionary (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT)",
17		}
18		c.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
19			Use:   "update [cedict|ecdict|all]",
20			Short: "Download/build the local dictionaries (~67 MB one-time)",
21			Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
22			RunE:  runDictUpdate,
23		})
24		return c
25	}
26	
27	func runDictUpdate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
28		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
29		if err != nil {
30			return err
31		}
32		what := "all"
33		if len(args) == 1 {
34			what = args[0]
35		}
36		dir := engine.DictDir(cfg.Dict.Dir)
37		ctx := cmd.Context()
38		prog := func(s string) { fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  "+s) }
39	
40		if what == "cedict" || what == "all" {
41			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "CC-CEDICT (Chinese→English):\n")
42			if err := engine.DownloadCedict(ctx, cfg.Dict.CedictURL, engine.CedictPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
43				return fmt.Errorf("cedict: %w", err)
44			}
45			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.CedictPath(dir))
46		}
47		if what == "ecdict" || what == "all" {
48			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ECDICT (English→Chinese, this takes a minute):\n")
49			if err := engine.BuildEcdictDB(ctx, cfg.Dict.EcdictURL, engine.EcdictDBPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
50				return fmt.Errorf("ecdict: %w", err)
51			}
52			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.EcdictDBPath(dir))
53		}
54		if what != "cedict" && what != "ecdict" && what != "all" {
55			return fmt.Errorf("unknown target %q (use cedict|ecdict|all)", what)
56		}
57		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "done. Dictionary mode (^e) is now bilingual zh↔en.")
58		return nil
59	}
60

```text
---XDGPATH---
// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
//
// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
package xdgpath

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
)

// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
const app = "translate"

// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
func base(env, def string) string {
	if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
		return v
	}
	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
	if err != nil {
		home = "."
	}
	return filepath.Join(home, def)
}

// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }

// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }

// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }

// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }

// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }

// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }

// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
func EnsureDirs() error {
	for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
		if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
			return err
		}
	}
	return nil
}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
10	)
11	
12	// buildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
13	// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
14	// engine="smartauto" builds the smart default (dictionary for words, LLM for
15	// phrases, bidirectional); a named provider builds a single LLM engine.
16	func buildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
17		cfg := res.Cfg
18	
19		switch res.Engine {
20		case "auto":
21			return buildAutoChain(res)
22		case "smartauto":
23			// Needs an LLM provider for the word-miss fallback and phrase translation;
24			// without one, behave like the plain auto chain (google/dict).
25			if res.Provider == nil {
26				return buildAutoChain(res)
27			}
28			return smartAutoFromConfig(res), nil
29		case "google":
30			return googleFromConfig(cfg), nil
31		case "llm":
32			// "llm" means "the resolved provider" (already chosen by Resolve).
33			if res.Provider != nil {
34				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
35			}
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
37		default:
38			// A named provider (e.g. "copilot", "ollama").
39			if res.Provider != nil {
40				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
41			}
42			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
43		}
44	}
45	
46	// buildAutoChain builds the "auto" fallback Chain over the providers/google named
47	// in chain.order. (google/dict engines otherwise join only via smart-auto or the
48	// TUI ^e cycle.)
49	func buildAutoChain(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
50		cfg := res.Cfg
51		var engines []engine.Engine
52		for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
53			switch {
54			case cfg.ProviderByName(name) != nil:
55				p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
56				engines = append(engines, llmFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
57			case name == "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled:
58				engines = append(engines, googleFromConfig(cfg))
59			}
60			// "dict" is wired in via smart-auto / the TUI engine cycle.
61		}
62		if len(engines) == 0 {
63			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
64		}
65		if len(engines) == 1 {
66			return engines[0], nil
67		}
68		return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
69	}
70	
71	// llmFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
72	func llmFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
73		if model == "" {
74			model = p.Model
75		}
76		return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
77			Name:      p.Name,
78			BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
79			Model:     model,
80			APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
81		})
82	}
83	
84	// googleFromConfig builds the free Google engine from config.
85	func googleFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) *engine.GoogleEngine {
86		return engine.NewGoogle(engine.GoogleConfig{
87			Endpoint:  cfg.Google.Endpoint,
88			ExtraDT:   cfg.Google.ExtraDT,
89			UserAgent: cfg.Google.UserAgent,
90			Timeout:   time.Duration(cfg.Google.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
91		})
92	}
93	
94	// dictFromConfig builds the dictionary engine from config: the offline bilingual
95	// engine (source=local, default) or the dictionaryapi.dev engine (source=api).
96	func dictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
97		if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" {
98			return engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
99				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
100				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
101				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
102				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
103			})
104		}
105		var apiFb *engine.DictEngine
106		if cfg.Dict.APIFallback {
107			apiFb = engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
108				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
109				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
110				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
111				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
112			})
113		}
114		return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{
115			Dir:          cfg.Dict.Dir,
116			CedictURL:    cfg.Dict.CedictURL,
117			EcdictURL:    cfg.Dict.EcdictURL,
118			AutoDownload: cfg.Dict.AutoDownload,
119			Fuzzy:        cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
120			APIFallback:  apiFb,
121		})
122	}
123	
124	// smartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
125	// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
126	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
127	func smartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
128		cfg := res.Cfg
129		return engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
130			CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
131			Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
132		})
133	}
134	
135	// smartAutoFromConfig builds the smart-auto default: a single word/term is a
136	// dictionary lookup (smart-dict, with an LLM fallback), and a phrase is an LLM
137	// translation via the auto chain (so phrases keep provider→google resilience).
138	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
139	func smartAutoFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
140		llm, err := buildAutoChain(res)
141		if err != nil {
142			// No chain engines (unlikely once a provider exists): fall back to the bare
143			// resolved provider so phrases can still translate.
144			llm = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
145		}
146		return engine.NewSmartAuto(smartDictFromConfig(res), llm)
147	}
148	
149	// buildEngineSet builds the list of engines the TUI can cycle through with ^e:
150	// the resolved primary (translate), plus Google (fast, keyless), the dictionary
151	// (word lookup), and — when an LLM provider is available — the smart dictionary
152	// (dictionary with an LLM fallback), so the user can switch on the fly.
153	func buildEngineSet(res config.Resolved, primary engine.Engine) []tui.NamedEngine {
154		cfg := res.Cfg
155		primaryName := primary.Name() // "auto", "smart-auto", "copilot", "google", …
156		if primaryName == "" {
157			primaryName = "auto"
158		}
159		set := []tui.NamedEngine{{Name: primaryName, Engine: primary, Mode: engine.ModeTranslate}}
160		if primaryName != "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled {
161			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "google", Engine: googleFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeTranslate})
162		}
163		if cfg.Dict.Enabled {
164			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "dictionary", Engine: dictFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
165			if res.Provider != nil {
166				set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "smart-dict", Engine: smartDictFromConfig(res), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
167			}
168		}
169		return set
170	}
171
```
</details></tool-use>

I have a comprehensive picture of the architecture. Here is my report.

## Architecture Map: `translate` CLI/TUI

### 1. Language & framework
**Go** (module `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, Go 1.26.4 in `go.mod`, README says 1.25+). Key dependencies:
- **CLI framework:** `github.com/spf13/cobra` (+ `pflag`)
- **TUI:** Charm's **Bubble Tea v2** ecosystem under the new `charm.land/*` import paths — `charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `charm.land/bubbles/v2` (textarea, viewport, list, spinner, key), `charm.land/lipgloss/v2`, `charm.land/huh/v2` (forms/wizard)
- **Support libs:** `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go SQLite for ECDICT), `atotto/clipboard`, `whatlanggo` + `sahilm/fuzzy` + `agnivade/levenshtein` (language detect / fuzzy match), `pelletier/go-toml/v2` (config)

### 2. Main entry points & key files
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/main.go` — thin `main()`; sets up a cancellable `signal.NotifyContext` and calls `cmd.Execute(ctx)`.
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/root.go` — the heart of dispatch. `runRoot` decides between three modes: one-shot CLI (args), piped stdin (non-TTY), or interactive TUI (TTY, no args). Contains `oneShot`, `runTUI`, engine building, history recording.
- **TUI entry:** `runTUI` in `cmd/root.go` builds `tui.Params` and runs `tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p))`. The model lives in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/model.go`.
- **CLI subcommands** (all in `cmd/`): `define.go` (dictionary lookup), `config.go`, `lang.go`, `history.go`, `init.go` (huh wizard), `dictupdate.go` (`dict update cedict|ecdict`), `version.go`, `build.go`. Registered in `NewRootCmd` via `root.AddCommand(...)`.

### 3. Directory / module structure
```
main.go                     entry
cmd/                        cobra command tree (CLI front-end)
internal/
  config/    config.go, resolve.go   TOML config + precedence resolution (flag>env>[cli]/[tui]>[general])
  engine/    the shared backend abstraction (see below)
  lang/      detect.go, lang.go      fuzzy language resolution, script detection, pair routing
  tui/       Bubble Tea front-end (model/update/view/keys/styles/pickers/msgs/cache/history)
  store/     store.go, jsonl.go      history persistence (JSONL; sqlite backend option)
  state/     state.go                last-pair state (state.json)
  xdgpath/   paths.go                XDG config/data/state/cache dirs
  debug/     debug.go                gated decision logging
```
The **engine layer is the core abstraction** and is UI-agnostic: `internal/engine/engine.go` documents that it "imports zero TUI code" — both the CLI and TUI drive identical `engine.Engine` values and diverge only at presentation.

### 4. TUI library
Bubble Tea v2 (value-receiver model convention). `Model` (in `internal/tui/model.go`) composes a `textarea`, result `viewport`, several `list.Model` pickers (history, language, model, suggestions, preset), and a spinner. Files: `model.go` (state), `update.go` (Update loop / key handling / clipboard), `view.go` (rendering incl. `renderDictionary`/`renderResult`/`renderSuggestions`), `keys.go` (keymap), `styles.go`, `pickers.go`, `msgs.go`, `cache.go`, `history.go`. Keybindings are Ctrl-chords defined in `defaultKeys()` (`^y` copy, `^e` engine, `^t` lang, `^p` model, `^o` style, `^g` pair, etc.).

### 5. CLI framework mechanics
**Cobra.** `NewRootCmd()` (cmd/root.go:47) builds the root with `Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs` so bare text (`translate "hola"`) falls through to translation while real subcommands still route. Persistent flags (`--to/-t`, `--from/-f`, `--engine`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--tier`, `--preset`, `--pair`, `--json`, `--debug`, etc.) are collected into `config.Overrides` by `overrides()` and merged by `cfg.Resolve(...)`. `Execute(ctx)` runs `ExecuteContext`.

### 6. Build / run
From the **Justfile** (`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/Justfile`):
- `just build` → `go build -o translate .`
- `just run *ARGS` (e.g. `just run "hola mundo" --to en`), `just tui`, `just define WORD`, `just init`
- `just check` = `fmt` + `vet` + `build`; `just test` = `go test ./...`; `just install` copies to `~/.dotfiles/bin/translate`
- README: `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest`, or `go build -o translate .` then `./translate ...`. First run writes `~/.config/translate/config.toml`.

---

### Directly relevant context for a TTS/pronunciation feature (describing what exists, not proposing changes)

The engine already carries **pronunciation data** you'd feed to TTS:
- `engine.DictEntry` (in `internal/engine/engine.go`) has a `Phonetic string` field (IPA-style). It's populated three ways:
  - Free Dictionary API path (`internal/engine/dict.go`, `mergeEntries`) — IPA text from `phonetics[].text`.
  - CC-CEDICT (Chinese) path (`internal/engine/localdict.go`, `cedictResult`) — sets `Phonetic` to the entry's **Pinyin**; also uses Pinyin as the `PartOfSpeech` label per meaning.
  - ECDICT (English→Chinese) path (`ecdictResult`) — sets `Phonetic` from the ECDICT `phonetic` column.
- Dictionary lookups flow through `engine.Request{Mode: engine.ModeDict}` and return a `TranslateResult` whose `Dictionary *DictEntry` holds `Word` + `Phonetic` + `Meanings`.

Where it surfaces / where an action would hook in:
- **CLI:** `renderDict` in `cmd/define.go` prints `Word` + `Phonetic` on the head line.
- **TUI:** `renderDictionary` in `internal/tui/view.go` (lines ~197-205) renders the head with phonetic; the TUI already has a precedent for a "produce an artifact from the current result" keybinding — the **`^y` copy** action in `internal/tui/update.go` (~line 216, `keys.Copy` → `clipboard.WriteAll(m.copyText())`, `copyText()` at update.go:440).

External-process pattern: the codebase currently **shells out to nothing** — no `os/exec` usage anywhere (only `atotto/clipboard` is used for the copy feature). So there is no existing pattern for invoking a system TTS binary (`say`, `espeak`, etc.); audio playback would be new territory.

Config extensibility: config is TOML via `internal/config/config.go` with per-feature structs (`Dict`, `SmartDict`, `Google`, etc.) and a `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay mechanism (`Overlay` struct) — a natural place a TTS feature's settings would slot in.
