I now have a complete picture of the lookup flow, result shape, config system, engines, and language support. No text-to-speech, audio, or pronunciation-playback code exists anywhere in the repo yet (only the `Phonetic` text field). Here is the detailed report.

---

# Dictionary lookup, result shape, and config — how it works today

## 0. No TTS exists yet
A repo-wide grep for `tts|speech|pronounce|speak|audio|espeak|piper|afplay|voice` found no audio/pronunciation code (only incidental words like "speaks" in comments). The only pronunciation data that flows through the system today is a text `Phonetic` string (IPA for English, numbered pinyin for Chinese). There is a recent chat transcript at `/.specstory/history/2026-07-13_14-48-48Z-text-to-speach.md` (your session), but no implementation.

---

## 1. Dictionary / word-lookup flow (input → result)

The engine layer is pure Go (`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/`). Every engine implements one interface, `engine.Engine` (`engine.go:136`), returning a channel of `Chunk` with a "channel closed == finished" contract. Two request modes exist (`engine.go:14`): `ModeTranslate` and `ModeDict`.

There are two entry paths to a word lookup:

### Path A — the main translate path via `smart-auto` (the recommended default)
`SmartAutoEngine` (`/internal/engine/smartauto.go`) is registered under `ModeTranslate` but internally routes single words to a dictionary lookup:

- `SmartAutoEngine.Translate` (`smartauto.go:52`) calls `isLookup(req.Text)` (`smartauto.go:70`). `isLookup` returns true for a single whitespace-free token: Latin words up to 32 runes (letters + `-`/`'`), or a Han run up to 4 characters. Anything with internal whitespace is treated as a phrase.
- Word → sets `r.Mode = ModeDict` and delegates to the `smart` sub-engine (smart-dict). Phrase → `r.Mode = ModeTranslate`, delegates to the LLM/chain.

### Path B — `translate define <word>` (explicit CLI subcommand)
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/define.go`:
- `runDefine` (`define.go:32`) loads config, resolves settings, picks an engine via `defineEngine` (`define.go:69`): `--plain` or no provider → plain offline dictionary; `--smart` or `cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault` (default true) → smart-dict.
- Issues `engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict}` and drains it, then renders with `renderDict` (`define.go:81`).

### The smart-dict engine (dictionary + LLM fallback)
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/smartdict.go` — `SmartDictEngine` (`smartdict.go:32`), `Name()` = `"smart-dict"`, supports `ModeDict` only:

- `Translate` (`smartdict.go:62`) drains the wrapped plain dictionary fully, then calls `dictAnswered` (`smartdict.go:89`):
  - exact hit (`res.Dictionary != nil`) → return as-is;
  - a fuzzy suggestion within `CloseDistance` (default 1, English edit distance) → keep as "did you mean";
  - otherwise (hard miss, too-far fuzzy, or "not installed" note) → `fallback` to the LLM.
- `fallback` (`smartdict.go:106`) re-issues the request as `ModeTranslate` with `Preset = PresetDictionary`, target chosen by `smartTarget` (`smartdict.go:134`: Chinese input → `en`; English/other → the caller's target, else `zh`), and **stamps a Warning** onto the result so the downgrade is never silent (`smartdict.go:124`).

### The plain dictionary engines
Selected by `dictFromConfig` (`/cmd/build.go:96`) based on `cfg.Dict.Source`:

1. **`LocalDictEngine`** (`/internal/engine/localdict.go`, `source = "local"`, the default). `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. Routes by script in `Translate` (`localdict.go:63`): `lang.IsChinese(word)` → `lookupZh` (CC-CEDICT), else → `lookupEn` (ECDICT).
   - `lookupZh` (`localdict.go:74`): in-memory CC-CEDICT index (`/internal/engine/cedict.go`, `cedictIndex.lookup`). On miss with `Fuzzy`, emits `prefixSuggest` candidates (`cedict.go:82`, prefix-based, no edit distance).
   - `lookupEn` (`localdict.go:96`): SQLite point lookup against ECDICT (`/internal/engine/ecdict.go`, `ecdictDB.lookup`, `SELECT ... WHERE word_lc = ?`). If ECDICT isn't installed and `APIFallback` is set, defers to the dictionaryapi.dev engine; else on miss emits `nearestN` Levenshtein suggestions from `/usr/share/dict/words`.
2. **`DictEngine`** (`/internal/engine/dict.go`, `source = "api"`). `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. Hits `https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/{lang}/{word}` (`dict.go:132`, `fetch`), merges entries (`mergeEntries`, `dict.go:162`), and on 404 returns ranked `nearestN` suggestions from the local wordlist.

### Engine build wiring
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/build.go`:
- `buildEngine` (`build.go:16`) dispatches on `res.Engine`.
- `smartDictFromConfig` (`build.go:127`) = `engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(...), SmartDictConfig{...})`.
- `smartAutoFromConfig` (`build.go:139`) = `engine.NewSmartAuto(smartDictFromConfig(res), buildAutoChain(res))`.
- `buildEngineSet` (`build.go:153`) builds the list the TUI cycles with `^e`: primary translate engine, Google, plain `dictionary` (ModeDict), and (when a provider exists) `smart-dict` (ModeDict).

---

## 2. Result shape & phonetics

The typed result is `engine.TranslateResult` (`/internal/engine/engine.go:49`). The dictionary payload is:

```go
// engine.go:86
type DictEntry struct {
    Word      string    `json:"word"`
    Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`   // <-- pronunciation lives here
    Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
    SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
}
type Meaning struct {          // engine.go:94
    PartOfSpeech string
    Definitions  []Definition
    Synonyms, Antonyms []string
}
type Definition struct { Text, Example string } // engine.go:102
```

**Phonetics ARE present today, but only as a single free-text `Phonetic` string, and the format differs by source:**

- **English via ECDICT** (`ecdictResult`, `localdict.go:143`): `d.Phonetic = en.Phonetic` — the ECDICT `phonetic` column, an IPA-style string (e.g. `test` → `test`, `hello` → `hә'lәu`). No slashes.
- **English via dictionaryapi.dev** (`mergeEntries`, `dict.go:162-172`): IPA from the API `phonetic`/`phonetics[].text` field (e.g. `/tɛst/`).
- **Chinese via CC-CEDICT** (`cedictResult`, `localdict.go:124-135`): `d.Phonetic = entries[0].Pinyin`, which is CC-CEDICT's **space-separated numbered pinyin** (e.g. `ce4 shi4`), captured by the regex `^(\S+)\s+(\S+)\s+\[([^\]]*)\]\s+/(.*)/` in `cedict.go:13`. Each `Meaning.PartOfSpeech` is also set to that pinyin (`localdict.go:130`).

**Not present anywhere:** zhuyin/bopomofo, tone-mark (diacritic) pinyin conversion, per-sense phonetics, or any audio URL/data. Pinyin is kept as raw tone numbers. There is no phonetics-normalization layer.

### Where the result is rendered
- **`translate define` (CLI)** — `renderDict` (`/cmd/define.go:81`): header line is `Word + "  " + Phonetic`, then per-`Meaning` the `PartOfSpeech` and up to 4 definitions (+ examples). Plain text, pipe-safe.
- **TUI result pane** — `renderResult` → `renderDictionary` (`/internal/tui/view.go:166` and `view.go:197`): same `Word + "  " + Phonetic` header, then meanings (up to 3 defs each). `renderSuggestions` (`view.go:218`) renders the "did you mean" list.
- **`--json`** — both `runDefine` (`define.go:53`) and `oneShot` (`root.go:417`) emit the full `TranslateResult` (including the nested `DictEntry` with `phonetic`) via `json.NewEncoder`.
- **Caveat:** the *main* one-shot translate path `oneShot` (`/cmd/root.go:374`) prints only `res.Translation` (the gloss / first definition, set by `DictEntry.gloss()` at `dict.go:188`). So a word lookup through `smart-auto` in non-JSON CLI mode shows only the gloss, **not** the phonetic — phonetics surface only via `translate define`, the TUI, or `--json`.

The TUI dispatches result-related actions from `/internal/tui/update.go`. The closest analog for a future pronunciation action is the clipboard copy: key binding `Copy` = `ctrl+y` (`/internal/tui/keys.go:33`), handled at `update.go:217`, using `github.com/atotto/clipboard`; `copyText` (`update.go:441`) returns `m.result.Translation`. Model-picker/preset actions are no-ops in `ModeDict` (`update.go:187`, `update.go:193`).

---

## 3. Configuration system

**Format: TOML.** Schema and defaults live in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/config/config.go`. Parsed with `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`.

**Location:** `xdgpath.ConfigFile()` = `~/.config/translate/config.toml` (overridable via `$TRANSLATE_CONFIG`; see `/internal/xdgpath/paths.go` and `config.Path()` at `config.go:209`). `translate config path` / `translate config show` print it (`/cmd/config.go`).

**Top-level schema** (`Config`, `config.go:17`):
```go
type Config struct {
    General   General    `toml:"general"`
    CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"`   // per-front-end overrides
    TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"`
    Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
    Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"`        // [[provider]] array-of-tables
    Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
    Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
    SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
    History   History    `toml:"history"`
}
```

**`[general]`** (`General`, `config.go:49`) holds behavior defaults: `default_target`, `default_source`, `pair`, `pair_with`, `remember_last_pair`, `live_translate`, `debounce_ms`, `engine`, `tier`, `preset`, `instructions`, `alternatives_count`, `stream`, `color`, `debug`.

**Per-front-end `[cli]` / `[tui]` overlays** (`Overlay`, `config.go:32`): every field is a pointer, so `nil` means "inherit from `[general]`". Only keys the user wrote override. Fields include `engine`, `tier`, `preset`, `instructions`, `model`, `default_target`, `default_source`, `pair`, `pair_with`, `stream`, `live_translate`, `debounce_ms`, `debug`.

**`[smartdict]`** (`SmartDict`, `config.go:118`):
```go
type SmartDict struct {
    CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`  // default 1
    Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`// default "dictionary"
    DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`  // default true
}
```

**`[dict]`** (`Dict`, `config.go:93`): `enabled`, `source` (`local`|`api`), `endpoint`, `lang`, `fuzzy`, `wordlist`, and for local mode `dir`, `cedict_url`, `ecdict_url`, `auto_download`, `api_fallback`.

**Resolution precedence** — `Config.Resolve` (`/internal/config/resolve.go:118`): **flag > env (`TRANSLATE_*`) > `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay > `[general]` > built-in default**. `applyOverlay` (`resolve.go:74`) copies non-nil overlay fields onto a `General` value copy; `pick` (`resolve.go:125`) applies flag/env/config precedence. Output is a `Resolved` struct (`resolve.go:33`) consumed by `buildEngine` and the TUI.

**Defaults & first-run:** `config.Default()` (`config.go:137`) returns the seed config, written on first run by `config.Load()` (`config.go:218`); `Load` starts from `Default()` before unmarshalling so omitted keys keep sane values. `Save` (`config.go:239`) writes atomically (temp + rename).

**How a new option is added:** add a typed field with a `toml:"..."` tag to the relevant struct in `config.go`, set its default in `Default()`, thread it into the corresponding engine config in `/cmd/build.go` (e.g. `dictFromConfig`, `smartDictFromConfig`), and optionally add an `Overlay` pointer field + a case in `applyOverlay` if it should be per-front-end overridable, plus a flag in `/cmd/root.go` and an `Overrides` field + `pick` line in `resolve.go` if it should be flag/env-settable. The `init` wizard (`/cmd/init.go`, using `huh`) writes a subset of these.

---

## 4. Engines and how they are selected

Concrete engines (all in `/internal/engine/`), each with a `Name()`:
- **`LLMEngine`** (`llm.go`) — `Name()` returns the provider name (`copilot`, `ollama`, `openrouter`, …). Speaks OpenAI `/chat/completions` or, for `claude-*` models, the Anthropic `/v1/messages` API (SSE streaming). Honors `Preset`, `Pair`, `Extra`.
- **`GoogleEngine`** (`google.go`) — `Name()` = `"google"`. Free `translate_a/single` endpoint, translate-only.
- **`DictEngine`** (`dict.go`) — `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. dictionaryapi.dev + local fuzzy.
- **`LocalDictEngine`** (`localdict.go`) — `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. CC-CEDICT + ECDICT offline.
- **`SmartDictEngine`** (`smartdict.go`) — `Name()` = `"smart-dict"`. Dictionary + LLM fallback.
- **`SmartAutoEngine`** (`smartauto.go`) — `Name()` = `"smart-auto"`. Word→smart-dict, phrase→LLM.
- **`Chain`** (`chain.go`) — `Name()` = `"auto"`. Ordered fallback over engines (per `chain.order`), 5s per-engine budget.

**Selection** — `res.Engine` (from config/flag/env) drives `buildEngine` (`/cmd/build.go:16`):
- `"auto"` → `buildAutoChain` over `chain.order` (default `["copilot","ollama","google","dict"]`, `config.go:154`).
- `"smartauto"` → `smartAutoFromConfig` (falls back to the auto chain if no provider).
- `"google"` → Google engine.
- `"llm"` or a **named provider** → single `LLMEngine` for the resolved provider.

The **built-in default** in `config.Default()` is `engine = "auto"` (`config.go:147`), but the `init` wizard's recommended/first option is `"smartauto"` (`/cmd/init.go:58`) — this is the "smart-auto default" referenced in commit `d30acfe`.

**Providers** (`[[provider]]`, `Provider` at `config.go:73`): `name`, `type` (`openai`|`ollama`|`openrouter`|`litellm`|`generic`), `base_url`, `model`, `model_fast`, `model_max`, `api_key_env`. **Tiers** map to models via `Provider.ModelForTier` (`config.go:276`): `default`/`fast`/`max` → `ModelDefault="claude-sonnet-5"`, `ModelFast="claude-haiku-4-5"` (out-of-box default tier), `ModelMax="claude-opus-4-8"` (`config.go:130`). Provider resolution: explicit `--provider`/`TRANSLATE_PROVIDER`, else the engine name if it's a provider, else `firstLLMProvider()` (`resolve.go:178`).

---

## 5. Supported languages / pairs

Defined in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/lang/lang.go`, `var table` (`lang.go:30`) — a fixed table of ~35 languages, each `{Code, Name, Aliases}`:

`en, zh, zh-TW (traditional), zh-CN (simplified), es, fr, de, ja, ko, pt, it, ru, ar, hi, nl, pl, tr, vi, th, id, uk, sv, cs, el, he, ro, hu, fi, da, no, fa, bn, ta, ms, ca`.

- Aliases carry native scripts and ISO-639-2 codes (e.g. `zh` aliases `中文/汉语/漢語/普通话/mandarin/zho/chi`).
- `lang.Resolve` (`lang.go:94`) does typo-tolerant fuzzy matching (Levenshtein) and returns ranked alternatives; `"auto"`/`""` pass through as auto-detect.
- `lang.List()` (`lang.go:147`) feeds the TUI/init language pickers; `lang.Name(code)` (`lang.go:154`) expands codes to names for prompts.

**Language-pair scope by engine:**
- **LLM translate** — any source→any target in the table (codes expanded to names in the prompt, `/internal/engine/prompt.go:93`). Supports bidirectional **pair mode** (`Pair`/`PairHome`/`PairAway`) via `pairDirective` (`prompt.go:81`).
- **Google** — whatever the free endpoint supports.
- **Dictionary lookups are effectively Chinese↔English only.** The offline dictionary is CC-CEDICT (zh→en) + ECDICT (en→zh), routed purely by script (`localdict.go:63`, `lang.IsChinese`). The TUI footer even hardcodes the label `"dictionary (zh↔en)"` (`/internal/tui/view.go:127`). English fuzzy suggestions are gated to English wordlists (`dict.go:90`).

Chinese detection used for routing lives in `/internal/lang/detect.go` (`lang.IsChinese`), also used by `smartTarget` and `isLookup`.

---

- Rendering points: `renderDict` (`/cmd/define.go:81`) and `renderDictionary` (`/internal/tui/view.go:197`).
- TUI action dispatch + a close analog (clipboard copy): `/internal/tui/keys.go:33`, `/internal/tui/update.go:217` and `copyText` at `update.go:441`.
- Config extension points: structs + `Default()` in `/internal/config/config.go`; wiring in `/cmd/build.go`; overlay/flag plumbing in `/internal/config/resolve.go` and `/cmd/root.go`.
- Language set / script routing: `/internal/lang/lang.go`, `/internal/lang/detect.go`.
</output>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I've got the full picture now—the dictionary download process fetches gzipped CC-CEDICT and ECDICT CSV files via HTTP and stores them as SQLite databases under DataDir/dict. I should also mention the debug package in internal/debug/debug.go since that's relevant for logging.

Now I'm ready to finalize the report covering all six questions: HTTP networking uses Go's standard library with per-engine *http.Client instances configured with timeouts; LLM calls go through a configurable base URL (defaulting to copilot-proxy at localhost:4141/v1) with Bearer token authentication from environment variables, supporting both OpenAI and Anthropic endpoints via streaming SSE channels; and there's no audio functionality implemented.

For subprocesses, the codebase currently has no os/exec usage — the only external interaction is clipboard operations through the atotto/clipboard library, which shells out internally but isn't called directly by the project. Adding TTS would introduce subprocess spawning as a new pattern.

Caching operates in two layers: an in-memory session cache in the TUI (unbounded, keyed by preset/engine/model/source/target/text) and a persistent disk-based layer. External dependencies include clipboard handling, charm UI libraries, and cobra for CLI, with TTS requiring either new dependencies or shell commands. API keys are environment-variable based—each provider specifies its env var name (like OPENROUTER_API_KEY) which is read once during initialization, with no keyring or file storage, only the variable name in config. Settings can also be overridden via TRANSLATE_* environment variables.

The config file lives at ~/.config/translate/config.toml (or a custom path via TRANSLATE_CONFIG), and the init command checks for the OpenRouter API key. I'm now writing up the full report with actual file paths, function names, and key code snippets for auth headers and endpoint building, plus noting where TTS would naturally integrate into the existing architecture.

# Translate CLI/TUI — infrastructure for a TTS/audio feature

Go project (`module github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, go 1.26). Charm/Bubble Tea TUI + Cobra CLI. The engine layer (`internal/engine/`) is pure Go, context-driven, and TUI-free; both the CLI and TUI drive the same `Engine` values.

## 1. HTTP / network requests + LLM tracing

**Library: Go standard library `net/http` only.** No requests/httpx/reqwest/axios equivalent, no third-party HTTP client. Each engine holds its own `*http.Client` with a per-engine `Timeout`, and all requests use `http.NewRequestWithContext` for cancellation.

Call sites:
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/llm.go` — the LLM engine (copilot-proxy, Ollama, OpenRouter, etc.)
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/google.go` — free Google `translate_a/single` endpoint
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/dict.go` — `dictionaryapi.dev`
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/dictdata.go` — downloads CC-CEDICT (gzip) + ECDICT (CSV)
- `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/init.go` — a GET health probe

**LLM request flow** (`internal/engine/llm.go`):

`LLMConfig` (lines 21–27) carries `Name`, `BaseURL`, `Model`, `APIKeyEnv`, `Timeout`. `NewLLM` (line 37) builds the engine and resolves the key once from the environment:
```go
key := ""
if cfg.APIKeyEnv != "" {
    key = strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv))
}
return &LLMEngine{cfg: cfg, key: key, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
```

- **Base URL / endpoint:** `endpoint(path)` (line 64) = `strings.TrimRight(BaseURL,"/") + path`. Default copilot-proxy base URL is `http://localhost:4141/v1` (see config below).
- **Auth:** `auth(r)` (line 57) sets `Authorization: Bearer <key>` only when a key exists. copilot-proxy uses an empty `APIKeyEnv`, so *no* auth header is sent. Anthropic path uses `anthropicAuth` (line 429) which sets `x-api-key` instead of Bearer.
- **Transport dispatch:** `Translate` (line 180) normalizes the model, then routes `claude*` models (`isAnthropicModel`, line 173) to `translateAnthropic` → POST `/messages` with header `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01` and `max_tokens: 4096`; all others go to `translateOpenAI` → POST `/chat/completions`. copilot-proxy serves Claude models ONLY via `/messages` (they 400 on `/chat/completions`).
- **Streaming:** when `req.Stream`, sets `Accept: text/event-stream`; a goroutine issues the request and pushes `engine.Chunk` values onto a buffered channel (`make(chan Chunk, 32)`). `readSSE` (line 440, OpenAI `data:` lines, `[DONE]`/`finish_reason`) and `readAnthropicSSE` (line 485, `content_block_delta`/`message_stop`) parse the stream with a `bufio.Scanner` (1 MB max line). Both detect truncation (`length` / `max_tokens`) and call `markTruncated`. Non-streaming decodes with `json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode`.
- **Model listing:** `Models` (line 211) GETs `/models`; `Available` (line 69) is an 800 ms GET `/models` health probe.

**Engine wiring** (`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/build.go`): `llmFromProvider` (line 72) maps a `config.Provider` → `engine.LLMConfig{Name, BaseURL, Model, APIKeyEnv}`. `buildAutoChain` (line 49) assembles a fallback `engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second)` over `chain.order`.

The uniform contract (`internal/engine/engine.go`): `Translate(ctx, Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)` returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal `ChunkDone`/`ChunkError`. `engine.Drain` (line 160) consumes it for the CLI with an `onToken` callback; the TUI drives the channel from its Update loop. `Request`/`TranslateResult`/`DictEntry` types are all here. Note `DictEntry.Phonetic` (line 87) exists but is text only.

## 2. Audio / sound / media handling

**None. There is zero audio, TTS, sound, or media code in the project.** Searches for `audio|play|sound|mp3|wav|ffplay|afplay|mpv|pyaudio|sounddevice|speak|tts|espeak|voice|pronounce|pronunciation|say` across `*.go` and docs returned only false positives (a code comment "the transports this engine speaks" in `llm.go:209`, and unrelated "say hi" test strings). No audio dependency in `go.mod`. The only phonetics-adjacent thing is the text-only `DictEntry.Phonetic` field.

## 3. Spawning external subprocesses / shell commands

**None. There is no `os/exec` usage anywhere in the codebase** (`exec.Command`, `exec.CommandContext`, `exec.LookPath` all return zero matches). No `PATH` binary is ever shelled out to by this project's own code.

The closest thing to an external-process interaction is clipboard support via the third-party library `github.com/atotto/clipboard`, used at `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/update.go:219` (`clipboard.WriteAll(txt)`). That library internally shells out to `pbcopy`/`xclip`, but the project never invokes `exec` itself. So a TTS feature that plays audio via `afplay`/`say`/`ffplay`/`mpv` would introduce the first `os/exec` usage in the codebase — there is no existing pattern to copy.

## 4. Caching layer

Three distinct persistence/caching mechanisms exist; none caches LLM responses persistently.

1. **Session translation cache (in-memory, TUI only)** — `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/cache.go`. `cacheStore = map[cacheKey]*engine.TranslateResult`, unbounded, session-scoped, **not persisted** ("LLM output is nondeterministic so it is not persisted across sessions"). `cacheKey` is `{preset, engineName, model, source, target, text}`; built by `cacheKeyFor` (line 13).

2. **Translation history (persistent)** — `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/store/`. `Store` interface (`store.go`) with an append-only JSONL implementation (`jsonl.go`, `OpenJSONL`) at `~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl` (`xdgpath.HistoryFile()`). In-memory fuzzy search via `github.com/sahilm/fuzzy`. Records are `store.Record` (JSON lines). Config `History.Backend` allows `jsonl | sqlite` but **only jsonl is implemented** (sqlite backend is a documented future drop-in).

3. **Dictionary data cache (persistent, sqlite)** — `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/dictdata.go` and `ecdict.go`. Downloaded CC-CEDICT (gzip) and ECDICT (CSV) are built into a **sqlite** DB under the data dir (default `<XDG data>/dict`, i.e. `~/.local/share/translate/dict`), opened read-only via `sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+path+"?_pragma=query_only(true)")` using pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite`. Populated by `translate dict update`.

## 5. External dependencies (relevant to audio/TTS or infra)

**No audio/TTS libraries are present.** Full direct-dependency list from `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/go.mod`:
- TUI/CLI: `charm.land/bubbles/v2`, `charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `charm.land/huh/v2`, `charm.land/lipgloss/v2`, `github.com/spf13/cobra`, `golang.org/x/term`
- Utilities: `github.com/atotto/clipboard` (clipboard — shells to pbcopy/xclip), `github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo` (offline language detect), `github.com/agnivade/levenshtein`, `github.com/sahilm/fuzzy`, `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`, `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go sqlite, used for dictionary data)

Nothing here helps with audio playback. HTTP is stdlib. Any TTS/playback capability would require either a new dependency or shelling out to a system binary (see §3).

## 6. API keys / auth tokens — storage & reading

**Keys live in environment variables, referenced by name from the config file. No keyring, no key value stored on disk.**

- `config.Provider.APIKeyEnv` (`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/config/config.go:80`) stores only the **name** of the env var, e.g. the default OpenRouter provider sets `APIKeyEnv: "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"` (config.go:175). copilot-proxy and Ollama have empty `APIKeyEnv` → no auth header.
- The key value is read once via `os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv)` in `engine.NewLLM` (`llm.go:43`), applied as `Authorization: Bearer` (OpenAI) or `x-api-key` (Anthropic).
- `cmd/init.go:36` reads `os.Getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")` to detect whether that key is present during setup.

**Config file location:** `~/.config/translate/config.toml`, overridable via `$TRANSLATE_CONFIG` (`config.Path()`, config.go:209). Paths resolved by `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/xdgpath/paths.go` (honors `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`/`XDG_DATA_HOME`/`XDG_STATE_HOME`, else `~/.config`, `~/.local/share`, `~/.local/state`, dirs created 0700, config written atomically). Config is TOML via `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`.

**Settings env vars** (`internal/config/resolve.go`, precedence flag > `TRANSLATE_*` env > `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay > `[general]` > default): `TRANSLATE_SOURCE`, `TRANSLATE_TARGET`, `TRANSLATE_ENGINE`, `TRANSLATE_TIER`, `TRANSLATE_PRESET`, `TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS`, `TRANSLATE_PAIR`, `TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH`, `TRANSLATE_PROVIDER`, `TRANSLATE_MODEL`, `TRANSLATE_DEBUG`, plus `TRANSLATE_CONFIG`.

---

## Summary for a TTS feature

- Network layer to imitate: stdlib `net/http` with a per-engine `*http.Client{Timeout}` and `http.NewRequestWithContext`; auth via `os.Getenv(APIKeyEnv)` → `Authorization: Bearer` / `x-api-key`; streaming via SSE `bufio.Scanner` (`internal/engine/llm.go`). A cloud-TTS call (e.g. OpenAI `/audio/speech`) would fit the same `LLMConfig`/`endpoint()`/`auth()` shape and read its key the same way.
- There is **no existing audio playback, no `os/exec`, and no audio dependency** — all three would be net-new. Local playback would mean the first `os/exec` in the repo (e.g. `afplay` on macOS) or a new Go audio dep.
- Persistence patterns to reuse: session `map` cache (`internal/tui/cache.go`), XDG data dir for cached audio files (`xdgpath.DataDir()` → `~/.local/share/translate`), and the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` already used for dictionary data if an audio cache index were wanted.
- Clipboard (`internal/tui/update.go:219`) is the sole precedent for "acting on the current result" from the TUI, a natural analog for a "play this translation" action.

Note: I have no Write tool and this was a read-only exploration, so no plan file was created — findings are reported above.

# Dictionary lookup, result shape, and config — how it works today

## 0. No TTS exists yet
A repo-wide grep for `tts|speech|pronounce|speak|audio|espeak|piper|afplay|voice` found no audio/pronunciation code (only incidental words like "speaks" in comments). The only pronunciation data that flows through the system today is a text `Phonetic` string (IPA for English, numbered pinyin for Chinese). There is a recent chat transcript at `/.specstory/history/2026-07-13_14-48-48Z-text-to-speach.md` (your session), but no implementation.

---

## 1. Dictionary / word-lookup flow (input → result)

The engine layer is pure Go (`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/`). Every engine implements one interface, `engine.Engine` (`engine.go:136`), returning a channel of `Chunk` with a "channel closed == finished" contract. Two request modes exist (`engine.go:14`): `ModeTranslate` and `ModeDict`.

There are two entry paths to a word lookup:

### Path A — the main translate path via `smart-auto` (the recommended default)
`SmartAutoEngine` (`/internal/engine/smartauto.go`) is registered under `ModeTranslate` but internally routes single words to a dictionary lookup:

- `SmartAutoEngine.Translate` (`smartauto.go:52`) calls `isLookup(req.Text)` (`smartauto.go:70`). `isLookup` returns true for a single whitespace-free token: Latin words up to 32 runes (letters + `-`/`'`), or a Han run up to 4 characters. Anything with internal whitespace is treated as a phrase.
- Word → sets `r.Mode = ModeDict` and delegates to the `smart` sub-engine (smart-dict). Phrase → `r.Mode = ModeTranslate`, delegates to the LLM/chain.

### Path B — `translate define <word>` (explicit CLI subcommand)
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/define.go`:
- `runDefine` (`define.go:32`) loads config, resolves settings, picks an engine via `defineEngine` (`define.go:69`): `--plain` or no provider → plain offline dictionary; `--smart` or `cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault` (default true) → smart-dict.
- Issues `engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict}` and drains it, then renders with `renderDict` (`define.go:81`).

### The smart-dict engine (dictionary + LLM fallback)
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/smartdict.go` — `SmartDictEngine` (`smartdict.go:32`), `Name()` = `"smart-dict"`, supports `ModeDict` only:

- `Translate` (`smartdict.go:62`) drains the wrapped plain dictionary fully, then calls `dictAnswered` (`smartdict.go:89`):
  - exact hit (`res.Dictionary != nil`) → return as-is;
  - a fuzzy suggestion within `CloseDistance` (default 1, English edit distance) → keep as "did you mean";
  - otherwise (hard miss, too-far fuzzy, or "not installed" note) → `fallback` to the LLM.
- `fallback` (`smartdict.go:106`) re-issues the request as `ModeTranslate` with `Preset = PresetDictionary`, target chosen by `smartTarget` (`smartdict.go:134`: Chinese input → `en`; English/other → the caller's target, else `zh`), and **stamps a Warning** onto the result so the downgrade is never silent (`smartdict.go:124`).

### The plain dictionary engines
Selected by `dictFromConfig` (`/cmd/build.go:96`) based on `cfg.Dict.Source`:

1. **`LocalDictEngine`** (`/internal/engine/localdict.go`, `source = "local"`, the default). `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. Routes by script in `Translate` (`localdict.go:63`): `lang.IsChinese(word)` → `lookupZh` (CC-CEDICT), else → `lookupEn` (ECDICT).
   - `lookupZh` (`localdict.go:74`): in-memory CC-CEDICT index (`/internal/engine/cedict.go`, `cedictIndex.lookup`). On miss with `Fuzzy`, emits `prefixSuggest` candidates (`cedict.go:82`, prefix-based, no edit distance).
   - `lookupEn` (`localdict.go:96`): SQLite point lookup against ECDICT (`/internal/engine/ecdict.go`, `ecdictDB.lookup`, `SELECT ... WHERE word_lc = ?`). If ECDICT isn't installed and `APIFallback` is set, defers to the dictionaryapi.dev engine; else on miss emits `nearestN` Levenshtein suggestions from `/usr/share/dict/words`.
2. **`DictEngine`** (`/internal/engine/dict.go`, `source = "api"`). `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. Hits `https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/{lang}/{word}` (`dict.go:132`, `fetch`), merges entries (`mergeEntries`, `dict.go:162`), and on 404 returns ranked `nearestN` suggestions from the local wordlist.

### Engine build wiring
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/build.go`:
- `buildEngine` (`build.go:16`) dispatches on `res.Engine`.
- `smartDictFromConfig` (`build.go:127`) = `engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(...), SmartDictConfig{...})`.
- `smartAutoFromConfig` (`build.go:139`) = `engine.NewSmartAuto(smartDictFromConfig(res), buildAutoChain(res))`.
- `buildEngineSet` (`build.go:153`) builds the list the TUI cycles with `^e`: primary translate engine, Google, plain `dictionary` (ModeDict), and (when a provider exists) `smart-dict` (ModeDict).

---

## 2. Result shape & phonetics

The typed result is `engine.TranslateResult` (`/internal/engine/engine.go:49`). The dictionary payload is:

```go
// engine.go:86
type DictEntry struct {
    Word      string    `json:"word"`
    Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`   // <-- pronunciation lives here
    Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
    SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
}
type Meaning struct {          // engine.go:94
    PartOfSpeech string
    Definitions  []Definition
    Synonyms, Antonyms []string
}
type Definition struct { Text, Example string } // engine.go:102
```

**Phonetics ARE present today, but only as a single free-text `Phonetic` string, and the format differs by source:**

- **English via ECDICT** (`ecdictResult`, `localdict.go:143`): `d.Phonetic = en.Phonetic` — the ECDICT `phonetic` column, an IPA-style string (e.g. `test` → `test`, `hello` → `hә'lәu`). No slashes.
- **English via dictionaryapi.dev** (`mergeEntries`, `dict.go:162-172`): IPA from the API `phonetic`/`phonetics[].text` field (e.g. `/tɛst/`).
- **Chinese via CC-CEDICT** (`cedictResult`, `localdict.go:124-135`): `d.Phonetic = entries[0].Pinyin`, which is CC-CEDICT's **space-separated numbered pinyin** (e.g. `ce4 shi4`), captured by the regex `^(\S+)\s+(\S+)\s+\[([^\]]*)\]\s+/(.*)/` in `cedict.go:13`. Each `Meaning.PartOfSpeech` is also set to that pinyin (`localdict.go:130`).

**Not present anywhere:** zhuyin/bopomofo, tone-mark (diacritic) pinyin conversion, per-sense phonetics, or any audio URL/data. Pinyin is kept as raw tone numbers. There is no phonetics-normalization layer.

### Where the result is rendered
- **`translate define` (CLI)** — `renderDict` (`/cmd/define.go:81`): header line is `Word + "  " + Phonetic`, then per-`Meaning` the `PartOfSpeech` and up to 4 definitions (+ examples). Plain text, pipe-safe.
- **TUI result pane** — `renderResult` → `renderDictionary` (`/internal/tui/view.go:166` and `view.go:197`): same `Word + "  " + Phonetic` header, then meanings (up to 3 defs each). `renderSuggestions` (`view.go:218`) renders the "did you mean" list.
- **`--json`** — both `runDefine` (`define.go:53`) and `oneShot` (`root.go:417`) emit the full `TranslateResult` (including the nested `DictEntry` with `phonetic`) via `json.NewEncoder`.
- **Caveat:** the *main* one-shot translate path `oneShot` (`/cmd/root.go:374`) prints only `res.Translation` (the gloss / first definition, set by `DictEntry.gloss()` at `dict.go:188`). So a word lookup through `smart-auto` in non-JSON CLI mode shows only the gloss, **not** the phonetic — phonetics surface only via `translate define`, the TUI, or `--json`.

The TUI dispatches result-related actions from `/internal/tui/update.go`. The closest analog for a future pronunciation action is the clipboard copy: key binding `Copy` = `ctrl+y` (`/internal/tui/keys.go:33`), handled at `update.go:217`, using `github.com/atotto/clipboard`; `copyText` (`update.go:441`) returns `m.result.Translation`. Model-picker/preset actions are no-ops in `ModeDict` (`update.go:187`, `update.go:193`).

---

## 3. Configuration system

**Format: TOML.** Schema and defaults live in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/config/config.go`. Parsed with `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`.

**Location:** `xdgpath.ConfigFile()` = `~/.config/translate/config.toml` (overridable via `$TRANSLATE_CONFIG`; see `/internal/xdgpath/paths.go` and `config.Path()` at `config.go:209`). `translate config path` / `translate config show` print it (`/cmd/config.go`).

**Top-level schema** (`Config`, `config.go:17`):
```go
type Config struct {
    General   General    `toml:"general"`
    CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"`   // per-front-end overrides
    TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"`
    Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
    Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"`        // [[provider]] array-of-tables
    Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
    Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
    SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
    History   History    `toml:"history"`
}
```

**`[general]`** (`General`, `config.go:49`) holds behavior defaults: `default_target`, `default_source`, `pair`, `pair_with`, `remember_last_pair`, `live_translate`, `debounce_ms`, `engine`, `tier`, `preset`, `instructions`, `alternatives_count`, `stream`, `color`, `debug`.

**Per-front-end `[cli]` / `[tui]` overlays** (`Overlay`, `config.go:32`): every field is a pointer, so `nil` means "inherit from `[general]`". Only keys the user wrote override. Fields include `engine`, `tier`, `preset`, `instructions`, `model`, `default_target`, `default_source`, `pair`, `pair_with`, `stream`, `live_translate`, `debounce_ms`, `debug`.

**`[smartdict]`** (`SmartDict`, `config.go:118`):
```go
type SmartDict struct {
    CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`  // default 1
    Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`// default "dictionary"
    DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`  // default true
}
```

**`[dict]`** (`Dict`, `config.go:93`): `enabled`, `source` (`local`|`api`), `endpoint`, `lang`, `fuzzy`, `wordlist`, and for local mode `dir`, `cedict_url`, `ecdict_url`, `auto_download`, `api_fallback`.

**Resolution precedence** — `Config.Resolve` (`/internal/config/resolve.go:118`): **flag > env (`TRANSLATE_*`) > `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay > `[general]` > built-in default**. `applyOverlay` (`resolve.go:74`) copies non-nil overlay fields onto a `General` value copy; `pick` (`resolve.go:125`) applies flag/env/config precedence. Output is a `Resolved` struct (`resolve.go:33`) consumed by `buildEngine` and the TUI.

**Defaults & first-run:** `config.Default()` (`config.go:137`) returns the seed config, written on first run by `config.Load()` (`config.go:218`); `Load` starts from `Default()` before unmarshalling so omitted keys keep sane values. `Save` (`config.go:239`) writes atomically (temp + rename).

**How a new option is added:** add a typed field with a `toml:"..."` tag to the relevant struct in `config.go`, set its default in `Default()`, thread it into the corresponding engine config in `/cmd/build.go` (e.g. `dictFromConfig`, `smartDictFromConfig`), and optionally add an `Overlay` pointer field + a case in `applyOverlay` if it should be per-front-end overridable, plus a flag in `/cmd/root.go` and an `Overrides` field + `pick` line in `resolve.go` if it should be flag/env-settable. The `init` wizard (`/cmd/init.go`, using `huh`) writes a subset of these.

---

## 4. Engines and how they are selected

Concrete engines (all in `/internal/engine/`), each with a `Name()`:
- **`LLMEngine`** (`llm.go`) — `Name()` returns the provider name (`copilot`, `ollama`, `openrouter`, …). Speaks OpenAI `/chat/completions` or, for `claude-*` models, the Anthropic `/v1/messages` API (SSE streaming). Honors `Preset`, `Pair`, `Extra`.
- **`GoogleEngine`** (`google.go`) — `Name()` = `"google"`. Free `translate_a/single` endpoint, translate-only.
- **`DictEngine`** (`dict.go`) — `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. dictionaryapi.dev + local fuzzy.
- **`LocalDictEngine`** (`localdict.go`) — `Name()` = `"dictionary"`. CC-CEDICT + ECDICT offline.
- **`SmartDictEngine`** (`smartdict.go`) — `Name()` = `"smart-dict"`. Dictionary + LLM fallback.
- **`SmartAutoEngine`** (`smartauto.go`) — `Name()` = `"smart-auto"`. Word→smart-dict, phrase→LLM.
- **`Chain`** (`chain.go`) — `Name()` = `"auto"`. Ordered fallback over engines (per `chain.order`), 5s per-engine budget.

**Selection** — `res.Engine` (from config/flag/env) drives `buildEngine` (`/cmd/build.go:16`):
- `"auto"` → `buildAutoChain` over `chain.order` (default `["copilot","ollama","google","dict"]`, `config.go:154`).
- `"smartauto"` → `smartAutoFromConfig` (falls back to the auto chain if no provider).
- `"google"` → Google engine.
- `"llm"` or a **named provider** → single `LLMEngine` for the resolved provider.

The **built-in default** in `config.Default()` is `engine = "auto"` (`config.go:147`), but the `init` wizard's recommended/first option is `"smartauto"` (`/cmd/init.go:58`) — this is the "smart-auto default" referenced in commit `d30acfe`.

**Providers** (`[[provider]]`, `Provider` at `config.go:73`): `name`, `type` (`openai`|`ollama`|`openrouter`|`litellm`|`generic`), `base_url`, `model`, `model_fast`, `model_max`, `api_key_env`. **Tiers** map to models via `Provider.ModelForTier` (`config.go:276`): `default`/`fast`/`max` → `ModelDefault="claude-sonnet-5"`, `ModelFast="claude-haiku-4-5"` (out-of-box default tier), `ModelMax="claude-opus-4-8"` (`config.go:130`). Provider resolution: explicit `--provider`/`TRANSLATE_PROVIDER`, else the engine name if it's a provider, else `firstLLMProvider()` (`resolve.go:178`).

---

## 5. Supported languages / pairs

Defined in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/lang/lang.go`, `var table` (`lang.go:30`) — a fixed table of ~35 languages, each `{Code, Name, Aliases}`:

`en, zh, zh-TW (traditional), zh-CN (simplified), es, fr, de, ja, ko, pt, it, ru, ar, hi, nl, pl, tr, vi, th, id, uk, sv, cs, el, he, ro, hu, fi, da, no, fa, bn, ta, ms, ca`.

- Aliases carry native scripts and ISO-639-2 codes (e.g. `zh` aliases `中文/汉语/漢語/普通话/mandarin/zho/chi`).
- `lang.Resolve` (`lang.go:94`) does typo-tolerant fuzzy matching (Levenshtein) and returns ranked alternatives; `"auto"`/`""` pass through as auto-detect.
- `lang.List()` (`lang.go:147`) feeds the TUI/init language pickers; `lang.Name(code)` (`lang.go:154`) expands codes to names for prompts.

**Language-pair scope by engine:**
- **LLM translate** — any source→any target in the table (codes expanded to names in the prompt, `/internal/engine/prompt.go:93`). Supports bidirectional **pair mode** (`Pair`/`PairHome`/`PairAway`) via `pairDirective` (`prompt.go:81`).
- **Google** — whatever the free endpoint supports.
- **Dictionary lookups are effectively Chinese↔English only.** The offline dictionary is CC-CEDICT (zh→en) + ECDICT (en→zh), routed purely by script (`localdict.go:63`, `lang.IsChinese`). The TUI footer even hardcodes the label `"dictionary (zh↔en)"` (`/internal/tui/view.go:127`). English fuzzy suggestions are gated to English wordlists (`dict.go:90`).

Chinese detection used for routing lives in `/internal/lang/detect.go` (`lang.IsChinese`), also used by `smartTarget` and `isLookup`.

---

### Quick map for a future TTS/pronunciation feature (files you'd touch, described, not proposed)
- Pronunciation source data already flows as `DictEntry.Phonetic` (`engine.go:88`), populated at `localdict.go:128/144` and `dict.go:165`.
- Rendering points: `renderDict` (`/cmd/define.go:81`) and `renderDictionary` (`/internal/tui/view.go:197`).
- TUI action dispatch + a close analog (clipboard copy): `/internal/tui/keys.go:33`, `/internal/tui/update.go:217` and `copyText` at `update.go:441`.
- Config extension points: structs + `Default()` in `/internal/config/config.go`; wiring in `/cmd/build.go`; overlay/flag plumbing in `/internal/config/resolve.go` and `/cmd/root.go`.
- Language set / script routing: `/internal/lang/lang.go`, `/internal/lang/detect.go`.

- Config is TOML (github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2) in internal/config/config.go: top-level `Config` with `General`, per-front-end `CLI *Overlay`/`TUI *Overlay`, `Dict`, `SmartDict`, etc. `Default()` (~config.go:137) seeds defaults; resolution precedence flag>env(TRANSLATE_*)>overlay>general>default in internal/config/resolve.go (`Resolve` ~line 118, `Resolved` struct ~line 33, `pick` ~line 125, `applyOverlay` ~line 74). CLI flags declared in cmd/root.go via `overrides()`; env vars threaded in resolve.go.
- Language/pair handling: internal/lang/lang.go (`table`, `Resolve`, `Name`, `List`), internal/lang/detect.go (`IsChinese`). Translation prompt uses pair home/away: internal/engine/prompt.go (`pairDirective` ~line 81). Config `General` has `Pair`, `PairWith`, `DefaultSource`, `DefaultTarget`, `RememberLastPair`.
- Platform of the developer: macOS. Verified available binaries on this machine: `say` (/usr/bin/say, rich zh_CN/zh_TW/zh_HK + en voices), `afplay`, `ffplay` (/usr/local/bin/ffplay). NOT present: espeak, mpv, mpg123, spd-say.

## LOCKED design decisions (from the user — honor exactly)
1. **Cross-platform**, but each OS has its own fallback chain. Design a `Speaker` abstraction with:
   - Native offline backends per OS: macOS `say`; Linux `espeak-ng`/`espeak`/`spd-say`; Windows PowerShell SAPI (System.Speech.Synthesis).
   - A cross-platform ONLINE backend: **Google Translate TTS** (GET https://translate.google.com/translate_tts?ie=UTF-8&client=tw-ob&tl={lang}&q={text}, returns MP3), played via a discovered player (`afplay`/`ffplay` on macOS; `mpv`/`ffplay`/`mpg123`/`paplay` on Linux; native on Windows). Needs local MP3 caching in the XDG cache dir.
   - Per-OS default fallback ordering (native offline first, then Google online, or configurable). Graceful degradation with a clear message if no backend/player is found. This is the FIRST os/exec usage in the repo — establish a small, well-contained pattern (use exec.CommandContext for cancellation).
2. **What to speak = the secondary/foreign ("副") language.** The user's native is Chinese (zh/zh-TW); they want to hear the foreign side (usually English). Design the selection logic:
   - Determine the "foreign/away" language from pair home/away or default_source/default_target (or a `[tts]` preference). 
   - Speak whichever side (source input vs translation/definition result) is in that foreign language; pick the matching voice by detected language of the chosen text.
   - **TUI tab-focus override:** the TUI recently gained focus/tab between panes (input textarea vs result viewport). If a pane is explicitly focused, speak THAT pane's content. You MUST read internal/tui/model.go, update.go, keys.go, view.go to find the focus state field/mechanism and cite exactly how focus is tracked and how to read "which pane is focused". Integrate the speak action with it.
3. **No phonetic text changes.** Audio only.
4. Surface in BOTH front-ends: a CLI flag (e.g. `--speak/-s`) on root + `define` (and consider a `translate speak <text> [--lang]` subcommand), and a TUI keybinding analogous to `ctrl+y` copy (propose a key; note ctrl+s flow-control caveat and that Bubble Tea raw mode disables IXON).

## Deliverable — produce a detailed plan covering:
1. **Package/module layout**: propose an `internal/tts/` (or `internal/speak/`) package with a `Speaker` interface and backend implementations. Give the interface signature(s), how OS detection + fallback chain works (runtime.GOOS + exec.LookPath), and how it stays testable (inject a "runner" for exec so tests don't spawn processes).
2. **Language→voice mapping**: for macOS `say`, map lang codes (en, zh, zh-TW, zh-HK, ja, ...) to voice names (Tingting/Meijia/Sinji/en default). For Google TTS, map to `tl=` codes. Sensible defaults + config override.
3. **Text/side selection logic** ("副語言" + focus): a pure function that, given (source text, source lang, result/translation text, target lang, pair home/away or foreign-pref, and optional TUI focused-pane), returns (textToSpeak, langToSpeak). Cite the runtime source/target/pair values available (Resolved struct in resolve.go; TUI model fields).
4. **Config schema**: a new `[tts]` struct in config.go (fields: enabled, backend/order, prefer_foreign or foreign default, per-lang voice map, rate, google endpoint, cache dir, auto-speak on/off default off), its `Default()` seed, and whether it needs `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay + flag/env plumbing. Follow the "how to add a config option" pattern.
5. **CLI wiring**: exact flags, where `overrides()`/`resolve.go` change, how `define.go` and `oneShot` (cmd/root.go ~374) trigger speaking after render, and the standalone `speak` subcommand shape.
6. **TUI wiring**: the new key in keys.go, handler in update.go (mirror the Copy case), how it reads focus + current result, async playback via a `tea.Cmd` (so the UI doesn't block) returning a msg, and a footer hint (view.go ~line 127 hardcodes footer labels — cite it).
7. **Caching & cancellation**: Google MP3 cache path (xdgpath — read internal/xdgpath/paths.go for the cache dir helper; if none exists say so), cache key, and context cancellation on exit.
8. **Error handling & UX**: no-player-found, network failure (Google), unsupported language, empty text.
9. **Testing**: unit tests for selection logic + voice mapping + fallback ordering with an injected fake runner; how to smoke-test end-to-end (`just build`, `translate speak`, TUI key).
10. **Risks/edge cases**: ffplay needs `-nodisp -autoexit`; say handles text directly (no file); Google endpoint rate-limits/needs User-Agent; long text; concurrent/overlapping playback (debounce or kill previous); shell-injection safety (pass args, never a shell string).

1	// Package tui implements the interactive Bubble Tea (v2, charm.land/*) front-end.
2	// It imports the shared engine layer but the engine layer never imports it.
3	package tui
4	
5	import (
6		"context"

8	
9		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"

20	// NamedEngine is one selectable engine in the TUI's ^e cycle.
21	type NamedEngine struct {
22		Name   string // "auto", "google", "dictionary", …
23		Engine engine.Engine
24		Mode   engine.Mode
25	}
26	
27	// Params configures a TUI session.
28	type Params struct {
29		Engines       []NamedEngine      // selectable engines; index 0 is the default
30		ModelSource   engine.ModelLister // fetches the model list for the model picker (may be nil)
31		ModelProvider string             // provider name the model override applies to
32		Store         store.Store        // nil when history is disabled
33		Source        string
34		Target        string
35		Pair          bool   // bidirectional pair mode
36		PairWith      string // the "away" language (resolved code)
37		Model         string // display model id for the footer (initial)
38		Preset        string // LLM prompt style
39		Instructions  string // extra system-prompt guidance
40		Live          bool   // live-debounce default state
41		DebounceMs    int
42	}
43	
44	type status int
45	
46	const (
47		statusIdle status = iota
48		statusTyping
49		statusTranslating
50		statusDone
51		statusError
52	)
53	
54	// focusPane selects which pane keyboard navigation drives. Global bindings
55	// (Enter, ^y, ^e, …) work regardless; only ordinary/scroll keys are routed.
56	type focusPane int
57	
58	const (
59		focusInput  focusPane = iota // typing + cursor navigation in the textarea (default)
60		focusOutput                  // keyboard scrolling of the result viewport
61	)
62	
63	// inputH is the fixed height (rows) of the input textarea box; shared by the
64	// layout and the click-to-focus hit test.
65	const inputH = 4
66	
67	// Model is the Bubble Tea model. All methods use value receivers and return the
68	// modified copy (the v2 convention) to avoid "state didn't stick" bugs.
69	type Model struct {
70		p    Params
71		base context.Context
72	
73		ta          textarea.Model
74		vp          viewport.Model
75		hist        list.Model
76		langList    list.Model
77		modelList   list.Model
78		suggestList list.Model
79		presetList  list.Model
80		sp          spinner.Model
81		keys        keyMap
82		st          styles
83	
84		width, height int
85		ready         bool
86		overlay       overlayKind
87		focus         focusPane // input (default) vs output; drives scroll/typing routing
88		resultH       int       // result viewport height (kept fixed to avoid layout jumps)
89	
90		source   string
91		target   string
92		pair     bool
93		pairWith string
94		preset   string
95		live     bool
96		engIdx   int // index into p.Engines (the active engine)
97	
98		// model picker state (session-cached)
99		cachedModels  []string
100		modelOverride string
101		modelsLoading bool
102		modelsErr     error
103	
104		// curEngine/curModel reflect the engine that actually served the last
105		// result (may differ from the selected engine when the auto chain falls back).
106		curEngine string
107		curModel  string
108	
109		status status
110		result *engine.TranslateResult
111		err    error
112		flash  string // transient footer notice (e.g. "copied ✓")
113	
114		// request lifecycle: one monotonic seq drives debounce-collapse, cancel, and
115		// stale-stream-drop. cancel/stream/streamBuf belong to the in-flight request.
116		// streamBuf is a plain string (NOT strings.Builder): the model is copied by
117		// value on every Update, and copying a used Builder panics.
118		seq         int
119		inflight    int
120		cancel      context.CancelFunc
121		stream      <-chan engine.Chunk
122		streamBuf   string
123		debounce    time.Duration
124		lastInput   string     // input text that produced the in-flight/last request
125		lastDoneKey cacheKey   // key of the last COMPLETED result (skip identical re-runs)
126		pendingKey  cacheKey   // cache key of the in-flight request
127		cache       cacheStore // session result cache (live cache-hit = instant, no API call)
128	
129		// Truncation auto-retry: a truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is
130		// re-fired once automatically, since a fresh request almost always completes.
131		// autoRetryKey is the pendingKey we've already retried once (so we retry at
132		// most once per input); retrying drives the "retrying" placeholder label.
133		autoRetryKey cacheKey
134		retrying     bool
135	}
136	
137	// active returns the currently selected engine.
138	func (m Model) active() NamedEngine {
139		if len(m.p.Engines) == 0 {
140			return NamedEngine{}
141		}
142		return m.p.Engines[m.engIdx%len(m.p.Engines)]
143	}
144	
145	// New builds a TUI model.
146	func New(ctx context.Context, p Params) Model {
147		ta := textarea.New()
148		ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
149		ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
150		ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
151		// Enter triggers translation, so move newline insertion to Alt+Enter.
152		ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"))
153		ta.Focus()
154	
155		debounce := time.Duration(p.DebounceMs) * time.Millisecond
156		if debounce <= 0 {
157			debounce = 400 * time.Millisecond
158		}
159	
160		hist := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
161		hist.Title = "History (↵ recall · esc close)"
162		hist.SetShowHelp(false)
163	
164		langList := list.New(langItems(), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
165		langList.Title = "Target language (↵ select · esc close)"
166		langList.SetShowHelp(false)
167	
168		modelList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
169		modelList.Title = "Model (↵ select · esc close)"
170		modelList.SetShowHelp(false)
171	
172		suggestList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
173		suggestList.Title = "Did you mean? (↵ look up · esc close)"
174		suggestList.SetShowHelp(false)
175	
176		preset := p.Preset
177		if preset == "" {
178			preset = engine.PresetConcise
179		}
180		presetList := list.New(presetItems(preset), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
181		presetList.Title = "Style (↵ select · esc close)"
182		presetList.SetShowHelp(false)
183	
184		sp := spinner.New()
185		sp.Spinner = spinner.MiniDot
186	
187		// Soft-wrap long paragraph lines to the viewport width; without this a single
188		// long (unwrapped) translation line is clipped and its tail is lost on screen.
189		vp := viewport.New()
190		vp.SoftWrap = true
191	
192		return Model{
193			p:           p,
194			base:        ctx,
195			ta:          ta,
196			vp:          vp,
197			hist:        hist,
198			langList:    langList,
199			modelList:   modelList,
200			suggestList: suggestList,
201			presetList:  presetList,
202			sp:          sp,
203			keys:        defaultKeys(),
204			st:          newStyles(),
205			source:      p.Source,
206			target:      p.Target,
207			pair:        p.Pair,
208			pairWith:    p.PairWith,
209			preset:      preset,
210			live:        p.Live,
211			status:      statusIdle,
212			debounce:    debounce,
213			cache:       cacheStore{},
214		}
215	}
216	
217	// Pair returns the current source and target language codes (read by the caller
218	// after the program exits to persist the last pair).
219	func (m Model) Pair() (source, target string) { return m.source, m.target }
220	
221	// Init requests the initial window size and starts the cursor blink + spinner.
222	func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
223		return tea.Batch(textarea.Blink, m.sp.Tick, tea.RequestWindowSize)
224	}

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	}
46	
47	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
48	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
49	type TranslateResult struct {
50		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
51		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
52		Target         string   `json:"target"`
53		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
54		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
55		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
56	
57		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
58		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
59		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
60	
61		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
62		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
63		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
64		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
65		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
66	
67		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
68		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
69		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
70	
71		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
72		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
73		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
74		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
75		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
76		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
77	
78		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
79		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
80		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
81		// Transient: not serialized.
82		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
83	}
84	
85	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
86	type DictEntry struct {
87		Word      string    `json:"word"`
88		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
89		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
90		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
91	}
92	
93	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
94	type Meaning struct {
95		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
96		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
97		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
98		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
99	}
100	
101	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
102	type Definition struct {
103		Text    string `json:"definition"`
104		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
105	}
106	
107	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
108	type ChunkKind int
109	
110	const (
111		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
112		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
113		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
114		ChunkDone
115		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
116		ChunkError
117	)
118	
119	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
120	type Chunk struct {
121		Kind   ChunkKind
122		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
123		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
124		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
125	}
126	
127	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
128	//
129	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
130	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
131	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
132	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
133	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
134	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
135	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
136	type Engine interface {
137		Name() string
138		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
139		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
140		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
141		Supports(m Mode) bool
142	}
143	
144	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
145	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
146	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
147		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
148		if err != nil {
149			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
150		} else {
151			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
152		}
153		close(ch)
154		return ch
155	}
156	
157	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
158	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
159	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
160	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
161		for c := range ch {
162			switch c.Kind {
163			case ChunkToken:
164				if onToken != nil {
165					onToken(c.Text)
166				}
167			case ChunkDone:
168				return c.Result, nil
169			case ChunkError:
170				return nil, c.Err
171			}
172		}
173		return nil, ErrNoResult
174	}
175
```

16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
22		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
23		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
24		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
25		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
26		History   History    `toml:"history"`
27	}
28	
29	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
30	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
31	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
32	type Overlay struct {
33		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
34		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
35		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
36		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
37		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
38		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
39		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
40		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
41		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
42		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
43		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
44		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
45		Debug         *bool   `toml:"debug,omitempty"`
46	}
47	
48	// General holds behavior settings.
49	type General struct {
50		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
51		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
52		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
53		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
54		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`

118	type SmartDict struct {

120		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
121		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.

208	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
209	func Path() string {
210		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
211			return p
212		}
213		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
214	}
215	
216	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
217	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
218	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
219		p := Path()
220		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
221		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
222			cfg = Default()
223			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
224				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
225			}
226			return cfg, true, nil
227		}
228		if err != nil {
229			return nil, false, err
230		}
231		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
232		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
233			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
234		}
235		return cfg, false, nil
236	}
237	
238	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
239	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
240		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
241			return err
242		}
243		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
244		if err != nil {
245			return err
246		}
247		p := Path()
248		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
249		if err != nil {
250			return err
251		}
252		tmpName := tmp.Name()
253		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
254		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
255			tmp.Close()
256			return err
257		}
258		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
259			return err
260		}
261		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
262	}
263	
264	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
265	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
266		for i := range c.Providers {
267			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
268				return &c.Providers[i]
269			}
270		}
271		return nil
272	}
273	
274	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
275	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
276	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
277		switch tier {
278		case "fast":
279			if p.ModelFast != "" {
280				return p.ModelFast
281			}
282		case "max":
283			if p.ModelMax != "" {
284				return p.ModelMax
285			}
286		}
287		return p.Model
288	}
289

 `./internal/config/resolve.go`

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
21	}
22	
23	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
24	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
25	type Mode int
26	
27	const (
28		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
29		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
30	)
31	
32	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
33	type Resolved struct {
34		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
35		Target       string
36		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
37		Provider     *Provider
38		Model        string
39		Tier         string
40		Preset       string
41		Instructions string
42		Pair         bool
43		PairWith     string
44		Stream       bool
45		Color        string
46		Debug        bool
47	
48		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
49		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
50		LiveTranslate bool
51		DebounceMs    int
52	
53		Cfg *Config
54	}
55	
56	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
57	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
58	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
59	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
60	
61	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
62	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
63		switch mode {
64		case ModeCLI:
65			return c.CLI
66		case ModeTUI:
67			return c.TUI
68		}
69		return nil
70	}
71	
72	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
73	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
74	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
75		if ov == nil {
76			return
77		}
78		if ov.Engine != nil {
79			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
80		}
81		if ov.Tier != nil {
82			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
83		}
84		if ov.Preset != nil {
85			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
86		}
87		if ov.Instructions != nil {
88			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
89		}
90		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
91			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
92		}
93		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
94			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
95		}
96		if ov.Pair != nil {
97			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
98		}
99		if ov.PairWith != nil {
100			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
101		}
102		if ov.Stream != nil {
103			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
104		}
105		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
106			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
107		}
108		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
109			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
110		}
111		if ov.Debug != nil {
112			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
113		}
114	}
115	
116	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
117	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
118	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
119		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
120		// the on-disk config is untouched).
121		g := c.General
122		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
123		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
124	
125		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
126			if flag != "" {
127				return flag
128			}
129			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
130				return v
131			}
132			return cfgVal
133		}
134	
135		r := Resolved{
136			Cfg:           c,
137			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
138			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
139			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
140			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
141			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
142			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
143			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
144			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
145			Color:         g.Color,
146			Stream:        g.Stream,
147			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
148			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
149			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
150		}
151	
152		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
153		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
154		if provName == "" {
155			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
156				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
157			} else {
158				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
159			}
160		}
161		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
162	
163		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
164		// provider's tier model.
165		cfgModel := ""
166		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
167			cfgModel = *ov.Model
168		}
169		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
170		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
171			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
172		}
173		return r
174	}
175	
176	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
177	// else the first configured provider, else "".
178	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
179		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
180			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
181				return name
182			}
183		}
184		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
185			return c.Providers[0].Name
186		}
187		return ""
188	}
189
```
