# Cross-Platform TTS / Pronunciation — Implementation Plan

## 0. Verified ground truth (anchors an engineer will touch)

- **No `os/exec`, no `runtime.GOOS`, no `exec.LookPath`, no cache-dir helper anywhere** (grep confirmed). This feature introduces the first `os/exec` usage — it must be small and well-contained.
- **HTTP precedent** (copy this exactly): `internal/engine/google.go:29-44` — per-engine `&http.Client{Timeout}`, `http.NewRequestWithContext` (`google.go:74`), `httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", …)` (`google.go:78`), URL built with `url.Values` + `.Encode()` (`google.go:57-66`).
- **TUI focus mechanism** (locked-decision #2): field `focus focusPane` on the model at `internal/tui/model.go:87`; the enum `focusInput`/`focusOutput` at `model.go:56-61`; toggled by `SwitchFocus` (`tab`/`shift+tab`, `keys.go:19` + `keys.go:36`) in the handler at `update.go:259-268` (blurs the textarea on `focusOutput`, re-focuses on `focusInput`); the view highlights the result box when `m.focus == focusOutput` at `view.go:33`. So "which pane is focused" is read directly off `m.focus`.
- **Current result / input in the TUI**: `m.result *engine.TranslateResult` (`model.go:110`), input text `m.ta.Value()`, and runtime language state `m.source`/`m.target`/`m.pair`/`m.pairWith` (`model.go:90-94`). The existing "act on result" precedent is `copyText()` at `update.go:441` and the Copy case at `update.go:217-226`.
- **CLI runtime pair/lang values** are already computed in `runRoot`: `src, tgt := resolvePair(...)` (`cmd/root.go:141`), `pairWith` (`root.go:142-146`), `res.Pair`/`res.PairWith` (`internal/config/resolve.go:42-43`), effective per-input target via `effectiveTarget()` (`root.go:200-207`). `oneShot` returns `*engine.TranslateResult` (`root.go:374`, `:428`).
- **Language helpers**: `lang.PairTarget(home,away,text)` (`internal/lang/detect.go:64`), `lang.IsChinese` (`detect.go:23`), `lang.Detect`→ISO-639-1 or "" (`detect.go:13`), `lang.Name` (`lang.go:154`). `baseCode` (strip region, `detect.go:106`) is **unexported** — we will add an exported `lang.Base`.
- **Config**: top-level `Config` (`config.go:17-27`), `Overlay` (`config.go:32-46`), `General` (`config.go:49-65`), `Google` struct as a template (`config.go:84-90`), `Default()` seed (`config.go:137-206`). Resolution: `Overrides` (`resolve.go:9-21`), `Resolved` (`resolve.go:33-54`), `Resolve`/`pick`/`applyOverlay` (`resolve.go:118/125/74`). `define.go` reads `cfg.Dict` directly (`define.go:37`) — precedent for reading a config sub-struct without threading it through `Resolved`.
- **xdgpath** has `ConfigDir/DataDir/StateDir` via `base(env,def)` (`paths.go:19-37`) and `EnsureDirs` (`paths.go:49`) — **no `CacheDir`**. We add one.
- **TUI async/msg plumbing** to mirror: `internal/tui/msgs.go` (`flashCmd` `:26`, `waitStream` `:39`, `flashClearMsg` `:23`), `m.flash` field (`model.go:112`), flash handling in `Update` (`update.go:64-66`). Footer labels are hardcoded at `view.go:132` (dict mode) and `view.go:160` (translate mode); footer height reserves the widest state via `footerContent(true)` (`view.go:97`).
- Build/test: `go 1.26.4`, module `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`; `just build` / `just test` / `just check` (justfile). Test convention is table-driven (`internal/config/resolve_test.go`, `internal/engine/smartauto_test.go`).

---

## 1. Package/module layout — `internal/tts/`

New package, UI-agnostic (importable by both `cmd/*` and `internal/tui`), stdlib `net/http` + `os/exec` only.

```
internal/tts/
  tts.go        # Speaker interface, Options, New(), fallback orchestrator, OS detection
  runner.go     # runner interface (exec injection) + execRunner (LookPath/CommandContext)
  native.go     # native offline backend; per-OS argv built from a GOOS param (no build tags)
  google.go     # Google translate_tts backend: download MP3 (net/http) + play via player
  player.go     # audio-player discovery + play (afplay/ffplay/mpv/mpg123/…)
  voices.go     # lang→say/espeak voice maps; lang→google tl normalization
  select.go     # pure text/side + language selection ("副語言" + focus override)
  cache.go      # MP3 cache path + key (sha256)
  errors.go     # ErrNoBackend, ErrNoPlayer, ErrEmptyText
  tts_test.go select_test.go voices_test.go
```

### Interface & orchestrator (tts.go)

```go
type Speaker interface {
    Name() string                                    // "say" | "espeak-ng" | "google"
    Available() bool                                 // binary/player present; cheap, no network
    Speak(ctx context.Context, text, lang string) error // blocks until playback ends / ctx cancelled
}

type Options struct {
    Order     []string          // e.g. ["native","google"]; default per-OS
    Rate      int               // words/min for say/espeak; 0 => backend default
    Voices    map[string]string // lang -> native voice override (from config)
    GoogleURL string            // default https://translate.google.com/translate_tts
    UserAgent string
    Timeout   time.Duration
    CacheDir  string            // "" => xdgpath cache/tts
    Player    string            // "" => auto-discover
    goos      string            // injected in tests; defaults to runtime.GOOS
    runner    runner            // injected in tests; defaults to execRunner
}

func New(opt Options) Speaker   // builds a *fallback wrapping the ordered backends
```

`fallback` (unexported) implements `Speaker`: on `Speak` it iterates its backends, skips `!Available()`, calls the first available `Speak`, and on error continues to the next, aggregating errors; returns `ErrNoBackend` if none were available. OS detection lives in `New`: `goos := opt.goos; if goos=="" { goos = runtime.GOOS }`, then it constructs `newNative(goos, runner, voices, rate)` and `newGoogle(...)` and orders them by `opt.Order`.

### Runner injection (runner.go) — keeps exec testable

```go
type runner interface {
    look(name string) (string, error)                       // exec.LookPath
    run(ctx context.Context, name string, stdin string, args ...string) error // exec.CommandContext; stdin optional
}
type execRunner struct{}
```

`execRunner.run` builds `exec.CommandContext(ctx, name, args...)`, sets `cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(stdin)` when non-empty, discards stdout/stderr, and `Run()`s — so **ctx cancellation kills the child process** (the requested cancellation semantics). Tests inject a `fakeRunner` that records `(name,args,stdin)` and returns programmed `look` hits, so **no process is ever spawned in tests**.

### Native backend (native.go) — single file, GOOS as a parameter (no build tags)

Referencing `"say"`, `"espeak-ng"`, `"powershell"` as strings compiles on every OS; `runner.look` just fails for absent ones. This is what makes darwin/linux/windows argv unit-testable from a single test binary. Per-OS argv (text passed as a **discrete arg or via stdin** — never a shell string):

- **darwin**: `say [-v VOICE] [-r RATE] TEXT`. For very long text (> ~1000 chars) switch to `say -f -` with text on stdin (avoids arg limits; no temp file).
- **linux**: try `espeak-ng`/`espeak`: `espeak-ng [-v VOICE] [-s RATE] TEXT`; else `spd-say [-l LANG] [-r ...] -- TEXT`.
- **windows**: `powershell -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "<script>"` where the script is a fixed literal that does `Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Speech; $s=New-Object System.Speech.Synthesis.SpeechSynthesizer; $s.SelectVoiceByHints(...); $s.Speak([Console]::In.ReadToEnd())` and **the text is fed on stdin** (`runner.run(..., stdin=text)`), so no quoting/injection.

`Available()` = `look(primaryBinary)` succeeds for the OS.

### Google backend (google.go) + player (player.go)

`newGoogle` holds its own `*http.Client{Timeout}` (mirror `engine/google.go:43`). `Speak`:
1. `tl := googleTL(lang)` (voices.go). Split text into ≤200-char chunks on sentence/space boundaries (Google 400s on long `q`).
2. For each chunk build `GoogleURL + "?" + url.Values{ie:UTF-8, client:tw-ob, tl:tl, q:chunk}.Encode()`, GET with `User-Agent` header (`http.NewRequestWithContext`), append MP3 bytes.
3. Write the concatenated MP3 to the cache path (see §7) if not already present, then hand the file to `player.play(ctx, file)`.
`Available()` = a player is discoverable (network is probed lazily at Speak time, not in Available, so it can sit as a fallback without a network round-trip).

**Player discovery (player.go)**, ordered by OS, exec via runner:
- darwin: `afplay FILE` → `ffplay -nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet FILE`
- linux: `mpv --no-video --really-quiet FILE` → `ffplay -nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet FILE` → `mpg123 -q FILE`
- windows: PowerShell WMPlayer COM (MP3) or fall through to native SAPI as the Windows default.

---

## 2. Language → voice mapping (voices.go)

Lookup order for every backend: exact code (e.g. `zh-TW`) → `lang.Base(code)` → backend default (omit the voice flag). Config `Voices` map merges on top of the built-ins.

**macOS `say`** (voices verified present on this host):
```
en→"Samantha"  zh/zh-CN→"Tingting"  zh-TW→"Meijia"  zh-HK→"Sinji"
ja→"Kyoko"  ko→"Yuna"  fr→"Thomas"  es→"Monica"  de→"Anna"  it→"Alice"  ru→"Milena"
```
If `say` errors on a missing voice, retry once without `-v` (graceful degrade). 

**Google `tl` normalization**: identity for base codes; `zh`/`zh-CN`→`zh-CN`, `zh-TW`/`zh-HK`→`zh-TW`, everything else → `lang.Base`.

**espeak-ng**: `zh`→`cmn`, `zh-HK`→`yue`, `ja`→`ja`, `ko`→`ko`, else `lang.Base`.

Add **`lang.Base(code string) string`** in `internal/lang/detect.go` (export the existing `baseCode` logic at `detect.go:106`) so tts and voices reuse it rather than re-implementing region stripping.

---

## 3. Text/side selection logic (select.go) — "副語言" + focus (pure function)

```go
type Side int
const ( SideAuto Side = iota; SideSource; SideResult )

type SelectInput struct {
    SourceText, SourceLang string // user input + resolved source code ("auto"/"" ok)
    ResultText, ResultLang string // translation OR dict head word + effective target code
    Foreign  string               // the 副/away language to prefer ("" => derive)
    Forced   Side                 // TUI focus override; SideAuto elsewhere
}
type Choice struct{ Text, Lang string }

func Select(in SelectInput) (Choice, bool) // bool=false when nothing speakable
```

Algorithm (concrete):
1. `Forced == SideSource` → `Choice{SourceText, langOf(SourceText, SourceLang)}`; if empty, fall through to auto.
2. `Forced == SideResult` → `Choice{ResultText, langOf(ResultText, ResultLang)}`; if empty, fall through.
3. `SideAuto`: compute `sl := langOf(SourceText, SourceLang)`, `rl := langOf(ResultText, ResultLang)`. Determine `foreign`:
   - `Foreign` if set; else "prefer the non-Chinese side" (user is zh-native): if exactly one of `{sl,rl}` is non-`zh`, that's `foreign`; if ambiguous, default `foreign = rl` (the translation).
   - Speak `ResultText` if `Base(rl)==Base(foreign)`, else `SourceText` if `Base(sl)==Base(foreign)`, else default to `ResultText`. Guard: if the chosen side is empty, use the other; if both empty return `false`.

`langOf(text, hint)`: `lang.IsChinese(text)` → refine to `hint` when `Base(hint)=="zh"` (keeps zh-TW/zh-HK region for the voice), else `"zh"`; else `lang.Detect(text)` if non-empty; else `hint`. Region (zh-TW vs zh-CN) can only come from the hint code, never from detection — that's why the caller passes `SourceLang`/`ResultLang`.

**Runtime values available to callers:** CLI — `src`, `effTgt`, `r.Translation` / `r.Dictionary`, `pairWith` (root.go:141-146,169-174). TUI — `m.source`, `m.target`, `m.result`, `m.ta.Value()`, `m.pairWith` (model.go:90-94,110).

---

## 4. Config schema (config.go)

Add a top-level `TTS TTS` field to `Config` (after `SmartDict`, `config.go:25`) and the struct (model it on `Google`, `config.go:84-90`):

```go
type TTS struct {
    Enabled       bool              `toml:"enabled"`
    AutoSpeak     bool              `toml:"auto_speak"`        // CLI: speak every result w/o --speak (default false)
    Order         []string          `toml:"order,omitempty"`   // ["native","google"]
    Foreign       string            `toml:"foreign,omitempty"` // 副 language; "" => derive
    PreferForeign bool              `toml:"prefer_foreign"`    // auto-side selection (default true)
    Rate          int               `toml:"rate,omitempty"`    // words/min; 0 => backend default
    Voices        map[string]string `toml:"voices,omitempty"`  // lang -> native voice
    GoogleTTSURL  string            `toml:"google_tts_url,omitempty"`
    UserAgent     string            `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
    TimeoutMs     int               `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
    CacheDir      string            `toml:"cache_dir,omitempty"`
    Player        string            `toml:"player,omitempty"`
}
```

`Default()` seed (add after the `SmartDict` block, `config.go:196-200`):
```go
TTS: TTS{
    Enabled: true, AutoSpeak: false, Order: []string{"native", "google"},
    Foreign: "", PreferForeign: true,
    GoogleTTSURL: "https://translate.google.com/translate_tts",
    UserAgent: "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli", TimeoutMs: 5000,
},
```

**Overlay / Resolved / env plumbing — deliberately NOT needed for v1.** `--speak` is a per-invocation trigger (like `--json`, `flagJSON` at `root.go:41`), not a resolved setting, so it does not go through `Resolve`. Everything else is read directly from `cfg.TTS`, exactly as `define.go:37` reads `cfg.Dict`. This keeps `Resolved` (`resolve.go:33`) and `applyOverlay` (`resolve.go:74`) untouched. (If per-front-end `auto_speak` is wanted later, add an `*bool` to `Overlay` and one line in `applyOverlay` — noted as a follow-up, not v1.)

Config sub-tests go in a new `internal/config/tts_test.go` verifying `Default().TTS` seeds and that unknown keys don't clobber (Load starts from `Default()`, `config.go:231`).

## 5. CLI wiring

**Flags (root.go):** add `flagSpeak bool`, `flagSpeakLang string` to the var block (`root.go:30-44`); register as **persistent** flags (so `define` and `speak` inherit) after `root.go:77`:
```go
f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud")
f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")
```
`overrides()` (`root.go:90`) is **not** touched — speak is not a `Resolved` field.

**`runRoot` (root.go):** after each `oneShot` result is printed and `recordAndRemember` runs — both the args branch (`root.go:170-174`) and the stdin branch (`root.go:186-192`) — add, guarded by `(flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak) && cfg.TTS.Enabled`:
```go
speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, pairWith) // helper in a new cmd/speak.go
```
`speakResult` builds `tts.New(optsFromCfg(cfg))`, computes `tts.Select(SelectInput{SourceText:text, SourceLang:src, ResultText:r.Translation, ResultLang:effTgt, Foreign:foreignFor(cfg,res,pairWith), Forced:SideAuto})` (or `--speak-lang` overrides `Choice.Lang`), then `sp.Speak(ctx, choice.Text, choice.Lang)`; on error prints `translate: speech unavailable (%v)` to stderr (never fatal). Speaking happens **after** stdout render so audio never interleaves with streamed tokens.

**`define.go`:** after `renderDict` (`define.go:62`), if `flagSpeak`, speak the looked-up word itself: `text = res2.Dictionary.Word` (fallback `res2.Translation`), `lang = detect(word)` — define pronounces the entry regardless of foreign preference.

**Standalone subcommand — new `cmd/speak.go`:**
```
translate speak <text...> [--lang code] [--backend native|google] [--voice name]
```
`newSpeakCmd`: `config.Load()`, build `tts.New`, resolve lang from `--lang`/`--speak-lang` or `lang.Detect(text)`, `Speak(cmd.Context(), text, lang)`. Register in `root.AddCommand(...)` at `root.go:80`. This is the fastest end-to-end smoke path.

## 6. TUI wiring

**Key (keys.go):** add `Speak key.Binding` to `keyMap` (`keys.go:7-21`) and to `defaultKeys` (`keys.go:24-38`):
```go
Speak: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+s"), key.WithHelp("^s", "speak")),
```
Note in a comment: `ctrl+s` is XOFF flow-control only under cooked-mode terminals; Bubble Tea puts the TTY in **raw mode (IXON disabled)**, so `ctrl+s` is delivered as a normal key here — safe. (Fallback keys if desired: `ctrl+b` or `ctrl+j`.)

**Handler (update.go):** add a case in `handleKey` mirroring the Copy case (`update.go:217-226`), before the `focusOutput` scroll block:
```go
case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Speak):
    if m.p.Speaker == nil { m.flash = "tts disabled"; return m, flashCmd() }
    forced := SideAuto
    if m.focus == focusOutput { forced = tts.SideResult } // explicit output focus → speak result
    in := tts.SelectInput{
        SourceText: m.ta.Value(), SourceLang: m.source,
        ResultText: resultSpeakText(m.result), ResultLang: m.target,
        Foreign: m.p.Foreign, Forced: forced,
    }
    ch, ok := tts.Select(in)
    if !ok { m.flash = "nothing to speak"; return m, flashCmd() }
    m.stopSpeak()                       // debounce: cancel any in-flight playback
    ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
    m.speakCancel = cancel
    m.flash = "speaking…"
    return m, tea.Batch(flashCmd(), speakCmd(m.p.Speaker, ctx, ch))
```

Design note on focus (locked-decision #2): `focusInput` is the *default*, not an explicit choice, so only `focusOutput` (user pressed Tab, `update.go:259-268`) forces `SideResult`; `focusInput` uses auto/副 selection so a foreign input can still be heard. Cite `m.focus` at `model.go:87`.

**Async playback (msgs.go):** add
```go
type speakDoneMsg struct{ err error }
func speakCmd(sp tts.Speaker, ctx context.Context, ch tts.Choice) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg { return speakDoneMsg{sp.Speak(ctx, ch.Text, ch.Lang)} }
}
```
Handle `speakDoneMsg` in `Update` (next to `flashClearMsg`, `update.go:64`): set `m.flash = ""` on success or `"speak failed"` on error, clear `m.speakCancel`. Because `Speak` runs inside the `tea.Cmd` goroutine, **the UI never blocks**.

**Model state (model.go):** add `speakCancel context.CancelFunc` (near `cancel`, `model.go:120`) and a `stopSpeak()` helper (mirror `cancelInflight`, `update.go:420`). Call `stopSpeak()` in the Quit (`update.go:174-176`) and Clear (`update.go:246`) cases so exit/clear kills playback.

**Params plumbing (root.go `runTUI` + tui.Params):** build the speaker once in `runTUI` (`root.go:319-353`) from `cfg.TTS` and add two `tui.Params` fields (`model.go:28-42`): `Speaker tts.Speaker` and `Foreign string`. Set `m.p.Speaker`/`m.p.Foreign` in `New`. `tui` importing `tts` is fine (tts is UI-agnostic, like `engine`).

**Footer hint (view.go):** append `^s speak` to both hardcoded help strings — dict mode at `view.go:132` and translate mode at `view.go:160`. Height auto-reserves via `footerContent(true)` (`view.go:97`), so wrapping is handled.

## 7. Caching & cancellation

**Add `xdgpath.CacheDir()`** (mirror `DataDir`, `paths.go:34`):
```go
func CacheDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CACHE_HOME", ".cache"), app) }
```
tts joins its own subdir `<CacheDir>/tts` and `os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o700)` lazily on first write (do **not** force it into `EnsureDirs` at `paths.go:49` — cache is optional). `cfg.TTS.CacheDir` overrides.

**Cache key (cache.go):** `sha256.Sum256([]byte(tl + "\x00" + text))` → hex, file `<dir>/<hash>.mp3`. On Speak: if the file exists and is non-empty, skip download and play it; else download → write atomically (temp + rename, like `config.Save` at `config.go:248-261`) → play. Cache is unbounded for v1 (document, like the session cache note at `internal/tui/cache.go:24`; an LRU/size cap is a follow-up).

**Cancellation:** every exec goes through `exec.CommandContext(ctx, …)` and HTTP through `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, …)`. The ctx is `cmd.Context()` (CLI, already SIGINT-cancellable) or `m.base`-derived (TUI, cancelled by `stopSpeak`/Quit). Cancelling the ctx kills `say`/`afplay`/`ffplay` mid-playback.

---

## 8. Error handling & UX

- **No backend/player found** → `fallback.Speak` returns `ErrNoBackend`; CLI: `translate: speech unavailable (no TTS backend found; install espeak-ng or a player like ffplay)`; TUI flash `no TTS backend`.
- **Network failure (Google)** → wrapped error; if native ran first (default `Order`) it never reaches Google. If Google is first/only and fails, the aggregate error surfaces; TUI flash `speak failed`.
- **Unsupported language** → voice-map miss falls back to the backend default voice (omit `-v`); still speaks. Google `tl` miss → base code; a 4xx surfaces as an error.
- **Empty text** → `Select` returns `false`; no-op (`nothing to speak`). `Speak` also guards empty text (`ErrEmptyText`), mirroring `engine.ErrEmptyInput` (`engine/errors.go`).
- Define sentinels in `internal/tts/errors.go` following `internal/engine/errors.go`.

---

## 9. Testing

- **`select_test.go`** (table-driven, style of `smartauto_test.go`): `foreign=en` + Chinese source + English result → speaks result/en; English source + Chinese result → speaks source/en; `Forced=SideResult`/`SideSource` overrides; region preserved (zh-TW hint kept for a Chinese pick); empty-side fallback; both-empty → `ok=false`.
- **`voices_test.go`**: `say`/espeak/google mappings for `en, zh, zh-CN, zh-TW, zh-HK, ja`; `lang.Base` fallback; config `Voices` override wins.
- **`tts_test.go`** with a `fakeRunner`: assert fallback **ordering** (native before google, skip unavailable via programmed `look` hits); assert `run` argv is `["say","-v","Meijia","測試"]`-shaped (**no shell string**, text is a discrete arg); assert Windows path feeds text on **stdin**; assert ctx cancellation propagates (cancelled ctx → `run` sees `ctx.Err()`). Backends are constructed directly with the fake `runner` + injected `goos`, so all three OSes are exercised from one `go test` run — no build tags, no real processes.
- **`config` test**: `Default().TTS` seeds; TOML round-trips.
- **Smoke (manual)**: `just build` then `./translate speak "hello world"`; `./translate speak "測試一下" --lang zh-TW`; `./translate "hola mundo" --to en --speak`; `./translate define ephemeral --speak`; TUI via `just tui` — type + Enter, press `^s` (input focus → foreign/副 side), then Tab (→ `focusOutput`) + `^s` (speaks the result pane). Use `--debug` / the TUI debug log (`root.go:225`) to confirm the chosen side+lang.

---

## 10. Risks / edge cases (each addressed above)

- **`ffplay` must be `-nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet`** or it opens a window and hangs; `afplay FILE` is plain (player.go).
- **`say` takes text directly** (no temp file); long text (>~1000 chars) switches to `say -f -` on stdin to dodge argv limits.
- **Google `translate_tts`** needs `client=tw-ob` + a browser-ish `User-Agent`, rate-limits (429), and 400s on long `q` → chunk to ≤200 chars and concat MP3 parts.
- **Long text** capped (~1000 chars) to bound both Google requests and runaway audio.
- **Overlapping playback**: TUI debounces via `stopSpeak`/`speakCancel`; CLI is synchronous.
- **Shell-injection safety**: never a shell string — always `exec.CommandContext(ctx, bin, args...)` with text as a discrete arg or on stdin, and `url.Values.Encode()` for the Google query (mirror `engine/google.go:65`).
- **Region voices** (zh-HK Sinji) may be absent → retry `say` without `-v`, then default.
- **Windows PowerShell startup latency** (~300-700ms) — acceptable; noted.

13		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
14		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
18	)
19	
20	// NamedEngine is one selectable engine in the TUI's ^e cycle.
21	type NamedEngine struct {
22		Name   string // "auto", "google", "dictionary", …
23		Engine engine.Engine
24		Mode   engine.Mode
25	}
26	
27	// Params configures a TUI session.
28	type Params struct {
29		Engines       []NamedEngine      // selectable engines; index 0 is the default
30		ModelSource   engine.ModelLister // fetches the model list for the model picker (may be nil)
31		ModelProvider string             // provider name the model override applies to
32		Store         store.Store        // nil when history is disabled
33		Source        string
34		Target        string
35		Pair          bool   // bidirectional pair mode
36		PairWith      string // the "away" language (resolved code)
37		Model         string // display model id for the footer (initial)
38		Preset        string // LLM prompt style
39		Instructions  string // extra system-prompt guidance
40		Live          bool   // live-debounce default state
41		DebounceMs    int
42	}
43	
44	type status int
45	
46	const (
47		statusIdle status = iota
48		statusTyping
49		statusTranslating
50		statusDone
51		statusError
52	)
53	
54	// focusPane selects which pane keyboard navigation drives. Global bindings
55	// (Enter, ^y, ^e, …) work regardless; only ordinary/scroll keys are routed.
56	type focusPane int
57	
58	const (
59		focusInput  focusPane = iota // typing + cursor navigation in the textarea (default)
60		focusOutput                  // keyboard scrolling of the result viewport
61	)
62	
63	// inputH is the fixed height (rows) of the input textarea box; shared by the
64	// layout and the click-to-focus hit test.
65	const inputH = 4
66	
67	// Model is the Bubble Tea model. All methods use value receivers and return the
68	// modified copy (the v2 convention) to avoid "state didn't stick" bugs.
69	type Model struct {
70		p    Params
71		base context.Context
72	
73		ta          textarea.Model
74		vp          viewport.Model
75		hist        list.Model
76		langList    list.Model
77		modelList   list.Model
78		suggestList list.Model
79		presetList  list.Model
80		sp          spinner.Model
81		keys        keyMap
82		st          styles
83	
84		width, height int
85		ready         bool
86		overlay       overlayKind
87		focus         focusPane // input (default) vs output; drives scroll/typing routing
88		resultH       int       // result viewport height (kept fixed to avoid layout jumps)
89	
90		source   string
91		target   string
92		pair     bool
93		pairWith string
94		preset   string
95		live     bool
96		engIdx   int // index into p.Engines (the active engine)
97	
98		// model picker state (session-cached)
99		cachedModels  []string
100		modelOverride string
101		modelsLoading bool
102		modelsErr     error
103	
104		// curEngine/curModel reflect the engine that actually served the last
105		// result (may differ from the selected engine when the auto chain falls back).
106		curEngine string
107		curModel  string
108	
109		status status
110		result *engine.TranslateResult
111		err    error
112		flash  string // transient footer notice (e.g. "copied ✓")
113	
114		// request lifecycle: one monotonic seq drives debounce-collapse, cancel, and
115		// stale-stream-drop. cancel/stream/streamBuf belong to the in-flight request.
116		// streamBuf is a plain string (NOT strings.Builder): the model is copied by
117		// value on every Update, and copying a used Builder panics.
118		seq         int
119		inflight    int
120		cancel      context.CancelFunc
121		stream      <-chan engine.Chunk
122		streamBuf   string
123		debounce    time.Duration
124		lastInput   string     // input text that produced the in-flight/last request
125		lastDoneKey cacheKey   // key of the last COMPLETED result (skip identical re-runs)
126		pendingKey  cacheKey   // cache key of the in-flight request
127		cache       cacheStore // session result cache (live cache-hit = instant, no API call)
128	
129		// Truncation auto-retry: a truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is
130		// re-fired once automatically, since a fresh request almost always completes.
131		// autoRetryKey is the pendingKey we've already retried once (so we retry at
132		// most once per input); retrying drives the "retrying" placeholder label.
133		autoRetryKey cacheKey
134		retrying     bool
135	}
136	
137	// active returns the currently selected engine.
138	func (m Model) active() NamedEngine {
139		if len(m.p.Engines) == 0 {
140			return NamedEngine{}
141		}
142		return m.p.Engines[m.engIdx%len(m.p.Engines)]
143	}
144	
145	// New builds a TUI model.
146	func New(ctx context.Context, p Params) Model {
147		ta := textarea.New()
148		ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
149		ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
150		ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
151		// Enter triggers translation, so move newline insertion to Alt+Enter.
152		ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"))
153		ta.Focus()
154	
155		debounce := time.Duration(p.DebounceMs) * time.Millisecond
156		if debounce <= 0 {
157			debounce = 400 * time.Millisecond
158		}
159	
160		hist := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
161		hist.Title = "History (↵ recall · esc close)"
162		hist.SetShowHelp(false)
163	
164		langList := list.New(langItems(), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
165		langList.Title = "Target language (↵ select · esc close)"
166		langList.SetShowHelp(false)
167	
168		modelList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
169		modelList.Title = "Model (↵ select · esc close)"
170		modelList.SetShowHelp(false)
171	
172		suggestList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
173		suggestList.Title = "Did you mean? (↵ look up · esc close)"
174		suggestList.SetShowHelp(false)
175	
176		preset := p.Preset
177		if preset == "" {
178			preset = engine.PresetConcise
179		}
180		presetList := list.New(presetItems(preset), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
181		presetList.Title = "Style (↵ select · esc close)"
182		presetList.SetShowHelp(false)
183	
184		sp := spinner.New()
185		sp.Spinner = spinner.MiniDot
186	
187		// Soft-wrap long paragraph lines to the viewport width; without this a single
188		// long (unwrapped) translation line is clipped and its tail is lost on screen.
189		vp := viewport.New()
190		vp.SoftWrap = true
191	
192		return Model{
193			p:           p,
194			base:        ctx,
195			ta:          ta,
196			vp:          vp,
197			hist:        hist,
198			langList:    langList,
199			modelList:   modelList,
200			suggestList: suggestList,
201			presetList:  presetList,
202			sp:          sp,
203			keys:        defaultKeys(),
204			st:          newStyles(),
205			source:      p.Source,
206			target:      p.Target,
207			pair:        p.Pair,
208			pairWith:    p.PairWith,
209			preset:      preset,
210			live:        p.Live,
211			status:      statusIdle,
212			debounce:    debounce,
213			cache:       cacheStore{},
214		}
215	}
216	
217	// Pair returns the current source and target language codes (read by the caller
218	// after the program exits to persist the last pair).
219	func (m Model) Pair() (source, target string) { return m.source, m.target }
220	
221	// Init requests the initial window size and starts the cursor blink + spinner.
222	func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
223		return tea.Batch(textarea.Blink, m.sp.Tick, tea.RequestWindowSize)
224	}
225
```

1	package tui

3	import "charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
4	
5	// keyMap holds the TUI key bindings. It grows across slices; slice 4 uses the
6	// core three, later slices add live-toggle, engine cycle, language swap, etc.
7	type keyMap struct {
8		Translate   key.Binding
9		Newline     key.Binding
10		ToggleLive  key.Binding
11		CycleEngine key.Binding
12		PickLang    key.Binding
13		PickModel   key.Binding
14		PickPreset  key.Binding
15		TogglePair  key.Binding
16		Copy        key.Binding
17		History     key.Binding
18		Clear       key.Binding
19		SwitchFocus key.Binding
20		Quit        key.Binding
21	}
22	
23	func defaultKeys() keyMap {
24		return keyMap{
25			Translate:   key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("enter", "ctrl+enter"), key.WithHelp("↵", "translate")),
26			Newline:     key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"), key.WithHelp("⌥↵", "newline")),
27			ToggleLive:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+l"), key.WithHelp("^l", "live")),
28			CycleEngine: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+e"), key.WithHelp("^e", "engine")),
29			PickLang:    key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+t"), key.WithHelp("^t", "lang")),
30			PickModel:   key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+p"), key.WithHelp("^p", "model")),
31			PickPreset:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+o"), key.WithHelp("^o", "style")),
32			TogglePair:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+g"), key.WithHelp("^g", "pair")),
33			Copy:        key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+y"), key.WithHelp("^y", "copy")),
34			History:     key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+r"), key.WithHelp("^r", "history")),
35			Clear:       key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+u"), key.WithHelp("^u", "clear")),
36			SwitchFocus: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("tab", "shift+tab"), key.WithHelp("⇥", "focus")),
37			Quit:        key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+c", "esc"), key.WithHelp("^c", "quit")),
38		}
39	}

 `./internal/tui/update.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
8		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
10		"github.com/atotto/clipboard"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
16	)
17	
18	// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
19	// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
20	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
21		switch msg := msg.(type) {
22		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
23			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
24			m.relayout()
25			m.ready = true
26			return m, nil
27	
28		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
29			return m.handleKey(msg)
30	
31		case debounceMsg:
32			if msg.seq != m.seq {
33				return m, nil // a newer keystroke arrived; this tick is stale
34			}
35			return m.launch(false)
36	
37		case historyLoadedMsg:
38			m.hist.SetItems(toListItems(msg.items))
39			return m, nil
40	
41		case modelsLoadedMsg:
42			m.modelsLoading = false
43			m.modelsErr = msg.err
44			m.cachedModels = msg.models
45			m.modelList.SetItems(modelItems(msg.models, m.currentModelID()))
46			return m, nil
47	
48		case tea.MouseWheelMsg:
49			return m.handleMouseWheel(msg)
50	
51		case tea.MouseClickMsg:
52			return m.handleMouseClick(msg)
53	
54		case spinner.TickMsg:
55			var cmd tea.Cmd
56			m.sp, cmd = m.sp.Update(msg)
57			// Animate the placeholder inside the (fixed-height) viewport so the
58			// layout never jumps while waiting for the first token.
59			if m.status == statusTranslating && m.streamBuf == "" {
60				m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder())
61			}
62			return m, cmd
63	
64		case flashClearMsg:
65			m.flash = ""
66			return m, nil
67	
68		case streamMsg:
69			return m.handleStream(msg)
70		}
71	
72		var cmd tea.Cmd
73		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
74		return m, cmd
75	}
76	
77	// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
78	// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
79	// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
80	func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
81		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
82			return m, nil
83		}
84		switch msg.chunk.Kind {
85		case engine.ChunkToken:
86			m.retrying = false // real tokens are flowing; drop the "retrying" label
87			m.streamBuf += msg.chunk.Text
88			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf))
89			m.vp.GotoBottom()
90			return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for the next token
91	
92		case engine.ChunkDone:
93			m.inflight = 0
94			m.status = statusDone
95			m.err = nil
96			m.lastDoneKey = m.pendingKey // remember what was translated (skip re-runs)
97			if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
98				r := *msg.chunk.Result
99				// A truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is re-fired once
100				// automatically — a fresh request almost always completes. Only after a
101				// second truncation do we settle on the partial text + ⚠.
102				if r.Truncated && m.autoRetryKey != m.pendingKey {
103					m.autoRetryKey = m.pendingKey
104					nm, cmd := m.launch(true) // force a fresh fetch of the same input
105					mm := nm.(Model)
106					mm.retrying = true
107					mm.vp.SetContent(mm.translatingPlaceholder())
108					return mm, cmd
109				}
110				m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{} // settled (complete, or gave up after the retry)
111				m.retrying = false
112				m.result = msg.chunk.Result
113				// A truncated result keeps its partial text on screen (with a ⚠), but
114				// must not be cached or persisted as if it were the real answer — so a
115				// manual retry (Enter) actually re-fetches instead of replaying it.
116				if !r.Truncated {
117					m.cache[m.pendingKey] = msg.chunk.Result // write-through (force overwrites)
118				}
119				if r.Engine != "" {
120					m.curEngine = r.Engine
121				}
122				if r.Model != "" {
123					m.curModel = r.Model
124				}
125				m.relayout() // engine/model segment width may have changed
126				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(r))
127				m.vp.GotoTop() // show a completed result from its start, not scrolled
128				// Don't persist a truncated result, or a suggestions-only result
129				// (empty translation, no entry).
130				if !r.Truncated && (r.Dictionary != nil || r.Translation != "") {
131					return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.p.Store, m.recordFor(r))
132				}
133				return m, nil
134			}
135			return m, nil
136	
137		case engine.ChunkError:
138			m.inflight = 0
139			m.retrying = false
140			m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{}
141			m.status = statusError
142			m.err = msg.chunk.Err
143			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))
144			return m, nil
145		}
146		return m, nil
147	}
148	
149	// isTextInput reports whether a key press produced a printable, non-whitespace
150	// character (Key.Text is set only for printable characters). Space/tab are
151	// treated as navigation so they can page the viewport instead of typing.
152	func isTextInput(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) bool {
153		return msg.Text != "" && strings.TrimSpace(msg.Text) != ""
154	}
155	
156	func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
157		// An open overlay (history / language / model picker) captures keys.
158		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
159			switch msg.String() {
160			case "ctrl+c":
161				m.cancelInflight()
162				return m, tea.Quit
163			case "esc":
164				m.overlay = overlayNone
165				return m, nil
166			case "enter":
167				return m.overlaySelect()
168			}
169			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
170			return m, cmd
171		}
172	
173		switch {
174		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
175			m.cancelInflight()
176			return m, tea.Quit
177	
178		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.History):
179			m.overlay = overlayHistory
180			return m, loadHistoryCmd(m.p.Store)
181	
182		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickLang):
183			m.overlay = overlayLang
184			return m, nil
185	
186		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickModel):
187			if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
188				return m, nil // no model concept in dictionary mode
189			}
190			return m.openModelPicker()
191	
192		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickPreset):
193			if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
194				return m, nil // presets are LLM translate styles
195			}
196			m.presetList.SetItems(presetItems(m.preset))
197			m.overlay = overlayPreset
198			return m, nil
199	
200		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.TogglePair):
201			m.pair = !m.pair
202			// Pair mode is a no-op when "away" equals the target; pick a distinct away.
203			if m.pair && (m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target)) {
204				if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
205					m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
206				} else {
207					m.pairWith = "en"
208				}
209			}
210			m.relayout() // footer pair segment changed
211			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
212				return m.launch(false)
213			}
214			m.clearResult()
215			return m, nil
216	
217		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Copy):
218			if txt := m.copyText(); txt != "" {
219				if err := clipboard.WriteAll(txt); err != nil {
220					m.flash = "copy failed"
221				} else {
222					m.flash = "copied ✓"
223				}
224				return m, flashCmd()
225			}
226			return m, nil
227	
228		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
229			m.live = !m.live
230			return m, nil
231	
232		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.CycleEngine):
233			if n := len(m.p.Engines); n > 1 {
234				m.engIdx = (m.engIdx + 1) % n
235				m.curEngine, m.curModel = "", ""
236				m.relayout() // footer content (engine/mode) changed
237				// Only re-translate automatically in live mode; otherwise switch the
238				// engine, clear the now-stale result, and wait for Enter.
239				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
240					return m.launch(true)
241				}
242				m.clearResult()
243			}
244			return m, nil
245	
246		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
247			m.cancelInflight()
248			m.seq++
249			m.ta.Reset()
250			m.focus = focusInput
251			m.ta.Focus()
252			m.result = nil
253			m.err = nil
254			m.streamBuf = ""
255			m.status = statusIdle
256			m.vp.SetContent("")
257			return m, nil
258	
259		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.SwitchFocus):
260			// Toggle which pane the keyboard drives. Blurring the textarea also dims its
261			// border (View keys off ta.Focused()); the result box highlights via m.focus.
262			if m.focus == focusInput {
263				m.focus = focusOutput
264				m.ta.Blur()
265				return m, nil
266			}
267			m.focus = focusInput
268			return m, m.ta.Focus()
269	
270		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
271			// After a dictionary miss the input still equals the missed word, so a
272			// second Enter means "help me choose" — open the ranked suggestions.
273			if m.result != nil && len(m.result.Suggestions) > 0 &&
274				strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == m.lastInput {
275				m.suggestList.SetItems(suggestItems(m.result.Suggestions))
276				m.overlay = overlaySuggest
277				return m, nil
278			}
279			return m.launch(true)
280		}
281	
282		// Output pane focused: the keyboard scrolls the result viewport. A printable
283		// character (not whitespace) snaps focus back to the input and is typed there,
284		// so the user can just start typing without pressing Tab first.
285		if m.focus == focusOutput {
286			if isTextInput(msg) {
287				m.focus = focusInput
288				cmd := m.ta.Focus()
289				var tcmd tea.Cmd
290				m.ta, tcmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
291				return m, tea.Batch(cmd, tcmd)
292			}
293			var cmd tea.Cmd
294			m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
295			return m, cmd
296		}
297	
298		// Ordinary keystroke: update the textarea, then (re)arm the debounce.
299		var cmd tea.Cmd
300		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
301	
302		if strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == "" {
303			// Input cleared: cancel in-flight work and clear the result pane.
304			m.cancelInflight()
305			m.seq++
306			m.result = nil
307			m.streamBuf = ""
308			m.status = statusIdle
309			m.vp.SetContent("")
310			return m, cmd
311		}
312	
313		if m.live {
314			m.seq++            // invalidate any pending debounce tick
315			m.cancelInflight() // stop any in-flight request; its results become stale
316			m.status = statusTyping
317			return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq, m.debounce))
318		}
319		return m, cmd
320	}
321	
322	// translatingPlaceholder renders the fixed-height "translating…" placeholder,
323	// upgraded to a "retrying" label while an auto-retry after a truncation is
324	// in flight so the user sees why a second request fired.
325	func (m Model) translatingPlaceholder() string {
326		label := "translating…"
327		if m.retrying {
328			label = "translating… · retrying (stream truncated)"
329		}
330		return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render(label)
331	}
332	
333	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
334	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
335	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
336	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
337	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
338		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
339		if text == "" {
340			m.status = statusIdle
341			return m, nil
342		}
343		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
344		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
345			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
346		}
347		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
348		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
349		if !force {
350			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
351				m.cancelInflight()
352				m.status = statusDone
353				m.err = nil
354				m.result = cached
355				if cached.Engine != "" {
356					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
357				}
358				if cached.Model != "" {
359					m.curModel = cached.Model
360				}
361				m.streamBuf = ""
362				m.lastInput = text
363				m.lastDoneKey = key
364				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
365				return m, nil
366			}
367		}
368		m.seq++
369		if m.cancel != nil {
370			m.cancel()
371		}
372		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
373		m.cancel = cancel
374		m.inflight = m.seq
375		m.status = statusTranslating
376		m.streamBuf = ""
377		m.retrying = false // a fresh launch clears any stale "retrying" label
378		m.err = nil
379		m.result = nil
380		m.lastInput = text
381		m.pendingKey = key
382		m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder()) // fixed-height placeholder
383	
384		seq := m.seq
385		ne := m.active()
386		target := m.target
387		pairOn := m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate
388		if pairOn {
389			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
390			debug.Logf("tui pair route: home=%s away=%s → target=%s", m.target, m.pairWith, target)
391		}
392		debug.Logf("tui launch: engine=%s mode=%d target=%s preset=%s", ne.Name, ne.Mode, target, m.preset)
393		req := engine.Request{
394			Text:          text,
395			Source:        m.source,
396			Target:        target,
397			Mode:          ne.Mode,
398			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
399			Model:         m.modelOverride,
400			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
401			Preset:        m.preset,
402			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
403			Pair:          pairOn,
404			PairHome:      m.target,
405			PairAway:      m.pairWith,
406		}
407		ch, err := ne.Engine.Translate(ctx, req)
408		if err != nil {
409			e := err
410			return m, func() tea.Msg {
411				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
412			}
413		}
414		m.stream = ch
415		return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
416	}
417	
418	// cancelInflight cancels the current request (if any) and marks it as no longer
419	// in flight so late chunks are dropped.
420	func (m *Model) cancelInflight() {
421		if m.cancel != nil {
422			m.cancel()
423			m.cancel = nil
424		}
425		m.inflight = 0
426	}
427	
428	// clearResult drops the shown result. Used when a setting (engine/lang/model/
429	// style/pair) changes without an immediate re-translate, so the pane never shows
430	// output that's stale relative to the (now different) footer.
431	func (m *Model) clearResult() {
432		m.cancelInflight()
433		m.result = nil
434		m.streamBuf = ""
435		m.err = nil
436		m.status = statusIdle
437		m.vp.SetContent("")
438	}
439	
440	// copyText returns the text to place on the clipboard for the current result.
441	func (m Model) copyText() string {
442		if m.result == nil {
443			return ""
444		}
445		return strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
446	}
447	
448	// currentModelID is the model id currently in effect (for the ✓ marker).
449	func (m Model) currentModelID() string {
450		if m.modelOverride != "" {
451			return m.modelOverride
452		}
453		return m.curModel
454	}
455	
456	// openModelPicker opens the model overlay, fetching the model list once per session.
457	func (m Model) openModelPicker() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
458		m.overlay = overlayModel
459		if m.cachedModels == nil && !m.modelsLoading && m.modelsErr == nil {
460			m.modelsLoading = true
461			return m, fetchModelsCmd(m.p.ModelSource)
462		}
463		return m, nil
464	}
465	
466	// overlayListUpdate forwards a message to the active overlay's list.
467	func (m *Model) overlayListUpdate(msg tea.Msg) tea.Cmd {
468		var cmd tea.Cmd
469		switch m.overlay {
470		case overlayHistory:
471			m.hist, cmd = m.hist.Update(msg)
472		case overlayLang:
473			m.langList, cmd = m.langList.Update(msg)
474		case overlayModel:
475			m.modelList, cmd = m.modelList.Update(msg)
476		case overlaySuggest:
477			m.suggestList, cmd = m.suggestList.Update(msg)
478		case overlayPreset:
479			m.presetList, cmd = m.presetList.Update(msg)
480		}
481		return cmd
482	}
483	
484	// overlaySelect applies the highlighted overlay item and closes the overlay.
485	func (m Model) overlaySelect() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
486		switch m.overlay {
487		case overlayHistory:
488			// Recall is an explicit "show me this" action: always translate.
489			if it, ok := m.hist.SelectedItem().(histItem); ok {
490				m.ta.SetValue(it.rec.Input)
491				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
492				m.overlay = overlayNone
493				return m.launch(true)
494			}
495		case overlayLang:
496			if it, ok := m.langList.SelectedItem().(langItem); ok {
497				m.target = it.code
498				m.overlay = overlayNone
499				m.relayout() // pair segment width changed
500				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
501					return m.launch(true)
502				}
503				m.clearResult()
504				return m, nil
505			}
506		case overlayModel:
507			if it, ok := m.modelList.SelectedItem().(modelItem); ok {
508				m.modelOverride = it.id
509				m.overlay = overlayNone
510				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
511					return m.launch(true)
512				}
513				m.clearResult()
514				return m, nil
515			}
516		case overlaySuggest:
517			// Picking a suggestion updates the input and looks it up.
518			if it, ok := m.suggestList.SelectedItem().(suggestItem); ok {
519				m.ta.SetValue(it.word)
520				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
521				m.overlay = overlayNone
522				return m.launch(true)
523			}
524		case overlayPreset:
525			if it, ok := m.presetList.SelectedItem().(presetItem); ok {
526				m.preset = it.id
527				m.overlay = overlayNone
528				m.relayout() // style segment width changed
529				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
530					return m.launch(false)
531				}
532				m.clearResult()
533				return m, nil
534			}
535		}
536		m.overlay = overlayNone
537		return m, nil
538	}
539	
540	// handleMouseWheel scrolls the active overlay list, or the result viewport.
541	func (m Model) handleMouseWheel(msg tea.MouseWheelMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
542		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
543			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
544			return m, cmd
545		}
546		var cmd tea.Cmd
547		m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
548		return m, cmd
549	}
550	
551	// handleMouseClick routes clicks. In an overlay it forwards to the list; in
552	// normal mode a click focuses the pane it lands in (so the keyboard scrolls it),
553	// except a click on the footer row opens the language picker.
554	func (m Model) handleMouseClick(msg tea.MouseClickMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
555		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
556			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
557			return m, cmd
558		}
559		if m.height > 0 && msg.Y == m.height-1 {
560			m.overlay = overlayLang
561			return m, nil
562		}
563		// The input box occupies the first inputH+2 rows (border + inputH + border);
564		// everything below it (down to the footer) is the result box.
565		if msg.Y < inputH+2 {
566			if m.focus != focusInput {
567				m.focus = focusInput
568				return m, m.ta.Focus()
569			}
570			return m, nil
571		}
572		if m.focus != focusOutput {
573			m.focus = focusOutput
574			m.ta.Blur()
575		}
576		return m, nil
577	}
578	
579	// recordFor builds a history record for a completed result.
580	func (m Model) recordFor(res engine.TranslateResult) store.Record {
581		src := m.source
582		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
583			src = res.DetectedSource
584		}
585		target := m.target
586		if res.Target != "" {
587			target = res.Target // effective target (may differ in pair mode)
588		}
589		return store.Record{
590			SourceLang:   src,
591			TargetLang:   target,
592			Engine:       res.Engine,
593			Model:        res.Model,
594			Input:        m.lastInput,
595			Output:       res.Translation,
596			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
597			Notes:        res.Notes,
598		}
599	}
600	
601	// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. The input/result
602	// boxes are rendered at .Width(m.width-2); lipgloss treats that as the TOTAL
603	// width, so the inner content area is that minus the box frame (border 2 +
604	// padding 2 = 4) = m.width-6. Wrapping content to anything wider makes each line
605	// overflow the box and get re-wrapped, orphaning a character per line.
606	func (m *Model) relayout() {
607		if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
608			return
609		}
610		footerH := m.footerHeight() // wraps to width; may be >1 on narrow terminals
611		compW := m.width - 6
612		if compW < 1 {
613			compW = 1
614		}
615		resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
616		if resultH < 3 {
617			resultH = 3
618		}
619		m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
620		m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
621		m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
622		m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
623		m.resultH = resultH
624		overlayH := m.height - 2 // overlays use a single-line footer
625		if overlayH < 1 {
626			overlayH = 1
627		}
628		m.hist.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
629		m.langList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
630		m.modelList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
631		m.suggestList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
632		m.presetList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
633	}
634

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
8		lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
12	)
13	
14	// View renders the two-box layout (input, result) plus a status/help footer.
15	// AltScreen + mouse are set declaratively (the v2 way).
16	func (m Model) View() tea.View {
17		if !m.ready {
18			return altView("initializing…")
19		}
20		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
21			return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, m.overlayBody(), m.overlayFooter()))
22		}
23	
24		inStyle := m.st.input
25		if m.ta.Focused() {
26			inStyle = m.st.inputHi
27		}
28		input := inStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Render(m.ta.View())
29	
30		// The result box is kept at a fixed height (viewport height) so the layout
31		// never jumps between the "translating…" placeholder and streamed output.
32		resStyle := m.st.result
33		if m.focus == focusOutput {
34			resStyle = m.st.resultHi
35		}
36		result := resStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Height(m.resultH).Render(m.vp.View())
37		footer := m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.statusLine())
38	
39		return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, input, result, footer))
40	}
41	
42	// overlayBody renders the active picker's list (or its loading/empty state).
43	func (m Model) overlayBody() string {
44		switch m.overlay {
45		case overlayHistory:
46			return m.hist.View()
47		case overlayLang:
48			return m.langList.View()
49		case overlayModel:
50			switch {
51			case m.modelsLoading:
52				return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("loading models…")
53			case m.modelsErr != nil:
54				return m.st.warn.Render("⚠ " + m.modelsErr.Error())
55			case len(m.cachedModels) == 0:
56				return m.st.warn.Render("no models available (is the provider up?)")
57			default:
58				return m.modelList.View()
59			}
60		case overlaySuggest:
61			return m.suggestList.View()
62		case overlayPreset:
63			return m.presetList.View()
64		}
65		return ""
66	}
67	
68	func (m Model) overlayFooter() string {
69		hint := "↵ select  esc close  ^c quit"
70		switch m.overlay {
71		case overlayHistory:
72			hint = "↵ recall  esc close  ^c quit"
73		case overlaySuggest:
74			hint = "↵ look up  esc close  ^c quit"
75		}
76		return m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.st.dim.Render(hint))
77	}
78	
79	// altView wraps content in a full-screen (alt-screen) tea.View with mouse on.
80	func altView(content string) tea.View {
81		v := tea.NewView(content)
82		v.AltScreen = true
83		v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
84		return v
85	}
86	
87	// statusLine renders the footer with the current transient state.
88	func (m Model) statusLine() string { return m.footerContent(false) }
89	
90	// footerHeight measures how many rows the footer occupies at the current width.
91	// It reserves the widest transient state ("translating") so the layout never
92	// jumps when that segment appears mid-flight.
93	func (m Model) footerHeight() int {
94		if m.width <= 0 {
95			return 1
96		}
97		return lg.Height(m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.footerContent(true)))
98	}
99	
100	// footerContent builds the footer string. forceState reserves the transient
101	// "translating" segment (for height measurement); otherwise the actual state is
102	// shown. lipgloss word-wraps it to the width, so narrow terminals get 2+ rows.
103	func (m Model) footerContent(forceState bool) string {
104		live := m.st.liveOff.Render("live○")
105		if m.live {
106			live = m.st.liveOn.Render("live●")
107		}
108	
109		var state string
110		if forceState {
111			state = m.st.dim.Render("⠿ translating")
112		} else if m.flash != "" {
113			state = m.st.liveOn.Render(m.flash)
114		} else {
115			switch m.status {
116			case statusTranslating:
117				state = m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("translating")
118			case statusError:
119				state = m.st.errText.Render("error")
120			}
121		}
122	
123		// Dictionary mode: no source→target pair (English-only lookups per script).
124		if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
125			left := strings.Join([]string{
126				m.st.label.Render("dictionary (zh↔en)"),
127				live,
128			}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
129			if state != "" {
130				left += "  " + state
131			}
132			return left + "  " + m.st.dim.Render("↵ define  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
133		}
134	
135		pair := fmt.Sprintf("%s→%s", lang.Name(m.source), lang.Name(m.target))
136		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
137			pair = fmt.Sprintf("pair %s⇄%s", m.target, m.pairWith) // compact codes
138		}
139	
140		// Engine segment: the selected engine name, plus the model that actually
141		// served the last result (once known).
142		engineSeg := m.active().Name
143		if m.curEngine != "" && m.curEngine != m.active().Name {
144			engineSeg = m.curEngine // chain fell back to a specific engine
145		}
146		if m.curModel != "" {
147			engineSeg = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", engineSeg, m.curModel)
148		}
149	
150		left := strings.Join([]string{
151			m.st.label.Render(pair),
152			m.st.dim.Render(engineSeg),
153			m.st.dim.Render("style:" + m.preset),
154			live,
155		}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
156		if state != "" {
157			left += "  " + state
158		}
159	
160		help := m.st.dim.Render("↵ translate  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^t lang  ^p model  ^o style  ^g pair  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
161		return left + "  " + help
162	}
163	
164	// renderResult formats a completed translation, a dictionary entry, or a ranked
165	// "did you mean" list when a dictionary lookup missed.
166	func (m Model) renderResult(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
167		if res.Dictionary != nil {
168			return m.renderDictionary(res)
169		}
170		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
171			return m.renderSuggestions(res)
172		}
173		var b strings.Builder
174		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(res.Translation))
175	
176		if res.DetectedSource != "" && (m.source == "" || m.source == "auto") {
177			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("detected: "+lang.Name(res.DetectedSource)))
178		}
179		for _, a := range res.Alternatives {
180			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("~ "+a))
181		}
182		if res.Notes != "" {
183			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+res.Notes))
184		}
185		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
186			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("⚠ "+w))
187		}
188		// A truncation warning already tells the user to press Enter to retry; the
189		// engine-switch hint only fits chain-fallback warnings, so skip it there.
190		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && !res.Truncated {
191			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("(^e switch engine · check the model/provider)"))
192		}
193		return b.String()
194	}
195	
196	// renderDictionary formats a dictionary lookup for the result pane.
197	func (m Model) renderDictionary(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
198		d := res.Dictionary
199		var b strings.Builder
200		head := d.Word
201		if d.Phonetic != "" {
202			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
203		}
204		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(head))
205		for _, mn := range d.Meanings {
206			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render(mn.PartOfSpeech))
207			for i, def := range mn.Definitions {
208				if i >= 3 {
209					break
210				}
211				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("• "+def.Text))
212			}
213		}
214		return b.String()
215	}
216	
217	// renderSuggestions renders the ranked "did you mean" list for a dictionary miss.
218	func (m Model) renderSuggestions(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
219		var b strings.Builder
220		b.WriteString(m.st.dim.Render("no exact match — did you mean:"))
221		for i, w := range res.Suggestions {
222			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d.", i+1)) + " " + m.st.alt.Render(w))
223		}
224		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("↵ choose"))
225		return b.String()
226	}
227

1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strings"
14	
15		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
16		"golang.org/x/term"
17	
18		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
19	
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
26		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
27		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
28	)
29	
30	var (
31		flagTo           string
32		flagFrom         string
33		flagModel        string
34		flagProvider     string
35		flagEngine       string
36		flagTier         string
37		flagPreset       string
38		flagInstructions string
39		flagPair         bool
40		flagPairWith     string
41		flagJSON         bool
42		flagNoHistory    bool
43		flagDebug        bool
44	)
45	
46	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
47	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
48		root := &cobra.Command{
49			Use:   "translate [text...]",
50			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
51			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
52				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
53				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
54				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
55			Version:       buildVersion(),
56			SilenceUsage:  true,
57			SilenceErrors: true,
58			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
59			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
60			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
61			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
62			RunE: runRoot,
63		}
64		f := root.PersistentFlags()
65		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
66		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
67		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
68		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
69		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
70		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
71		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
72		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
73		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
74		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
75		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
76		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
77		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
78	
79		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
80		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd())
81		return root
82	}
83	
84	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
85	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
86		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
87	}
88	
89	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
90	func overrides() config.Overrides {
91		return config.Overrides{
92			Source:       flagFrom,
93			Target:       flagTo,
94			Engine:       flagEngine,
95			Provider:     flagProvider,
96			Model:        flagModel,
97			Tier:         flagTier,
98			Preset:       flagPreset,
99			Instructions: flagInstructions,
100			Pair:         flagPair,
101			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
102			Debug:        flagDebug,
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
107	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
108	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
109	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
110		if len(args) > 0 {
111			return config.ModeCLI
112		}
113		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
114			return config.ModeCLI
115		}
116		return config.ModeTUI
117	}
118	
119	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
120		ctx := cmd.Context()
121	
122		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
123		if err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126		if created {
127			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
128		}
129	
130		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
131		if res.Debug {
132			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
133			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
134			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
135				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
136			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
137				debug.Enable(w)
138			}
139		}
140		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
141		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
142		pairWith := ""
143		if res.Pair {
144			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
145			pairWith = pwm.Code
146		}
147		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s",
148			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith)
149		if res.Pair && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
150			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
151		}
152	
153		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
154			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
155		}
156		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
157		if err != nil {
158			return err
159		}
160	
161		st := openStore(cfg)
162		if st != nil {
163			defer st.Close()
164		}
165	
166		switch {
167		case len(args) > 0:
168			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
169			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
170			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
171			if err != nil {
172				return err
173			}
174			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
175			return nil
176	
177		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
178			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
179			if err != nil {
180				return err
181			}
182			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
183			if text == "" {
184				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
185			}
186			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
187			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
188			if err != nil {
189				return err
190			}
191			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
192			return nil
193	
194		default:
195			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
196		}
197	}
198	
199	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
200	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
201		if pair && away != "" {
202			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
203			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
204			return t
205		}
206		return home
207	}
208	
209	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
210	func clip(s string, n int) string {
211		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
212		r := []rune(s)
213		if len(r) > n {
214			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
215		}
216		return s
217	}
218	
219	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
220	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
221	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
222		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
223			return nil
224		}
225		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
226			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
227		if err != nil {
228			return nil
229		}
230		return f
231	}
232	
233	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
234	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
235	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
236		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
237			return
238		}
239		stt, err := state.Load()
240		if err != nil || stt == nil {
241			return
242		}
243		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
244			res.Source = stt.Source
245			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
246		}
247		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
248			res.Target = stt.Target
249			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
250		}
251	}
252	
253	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
254	// suppressed with --no-history.
255	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
256		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
257			return nil
258		}
259		path := cfg.History.Path
260		if path == "" {
261			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
262		}
263		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
264		if err != nil {
265			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
266			return nil
267		}
268		return st
269	}
270	
271	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
272	//
273	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
274	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
275	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
276	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
277		if st != nil {
278			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
279		}
280		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
281			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
282		}
283	}
284	
285	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
286	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
287		src := source
288		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
289			src = res.DetectedSource
290		}
291		return store.Record{
292			SourceLang:   src,
293			TargetLang:   target,
294			Engine:       res.Engine,
295			Model:        res.Model,
296			Input:        input,
297			Output:       res.Translation,
298			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
299			Notes:        res.Notes,
300		}
301	}
302	
303	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
304	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
305		mode := "fixed"
306		if source == "auto" {
307			mode = "auto"
308		}
309		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
310			Source:     source,
311			Target:     target,
312			SourceMode: mode,
313			Engine:     engineName,
314		})
315	}
316	
317	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
318	// pair on exit.
319	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
320		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
321		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
322		modelProvider := ""
323		if res.Provider != nil {
324			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
325			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
326		}
327		p := tui.Params{
328			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
329			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
330			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
331			Store:         st,
332			Source:        source,
333			Target:        target,
334			Pair:          res.Pair,
335			PairWith:      pairWith,
336			Model:         res.Model,
337			Preset:        res.Preset,
338			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
339			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
340			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
341		}
342		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
343		if err != nil {
344			return err
345		}
346		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
347			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
348				s, t := fm.Pair()
349				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
350			}
351		}
352		return nil
353	}
354	
355	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
356	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
357	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
358		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
359		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
360		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
361			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
362		}
363		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
364			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
365		}
366		return sm.Code, tm.Code
367	}
368	
369	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
370	//
371	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
372	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
373	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
374	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
375		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
376		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON
377	
378		req := engine.Request{
379			Text:     text,
380			Source:   source,
381			Target:   target,
382			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
383			Stream:   stream,
384			Preset:   preset,
385			Extra:    instructions,
386			Pair:     pair,
387			PairHome: pairHome,
388			PairAway: pairAway,
389		}
390		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
391		if err != nil {
392			return nil, err
393		}
394	
395		var onTok func(string)
396		if stream {
397			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
398		}
399		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
400		if err != nil {
401			if stream {
402				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
403			}
404			return nil, err
405		}
406	
407		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
408		// failed and why, and how to switch.
409		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
410			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
411		}
412		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
413			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
414		}
415	
416		switch {
417		case flagJSON:
418			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
419			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
420			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
421				return nil, err
422			}
423		case stream:
424			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
425		default:
426			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
427		}
428		return res, nil
429	}
430
```
