1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		return nil
64	}
65	
66	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
67	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
68	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
69	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
70		cfg := res.Cfg
71		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
72			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
73		}
74		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
75			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
76		}
77		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
78	}
79	
80	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
81	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
82		d := res.Dictionary
83		if d == nil {
84			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
85				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
86			}
87			return res.Translation + "\n"
88		}
89		var b strings.Builder
90		head := d.Word
91		if d.Phonetic != "" {
92			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
93		}
94		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
95		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
96			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
97			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
98				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
99					break
100				}
101				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
102				if def.Example != "" {
103					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
104				}
105			}
106		}
107		return b.String()
108	}
109

1	// Package lang resolves language names/codes with typo tolerance and provides
2	// offline language detection. It is shared by the CLI and the TUI.

4	
5	import (

10	)

12	// Lang is one known language: its ISO-639-1 code, English name, and aliases
13	// (native names, ISO-639-2 codes, common misspellings).
14	type Lang struct {
15		Code    string
16		Name    string
17		Aliases []string
18	}
19	
20	// Match is the result of resolving a query to a language.
21	type Match struct {
22		Code  string

24		Score float64 // 1.0 == exact; lower for fuzzy
25		Exact bool
26	}

60		{"no", "norwegian", []string{"nor", "norsk", "nb", "nn"}},
61		{"fa", "persian", []string{"fas", "per", "farsi", "فارسی"}},
62		{"bn", "bengali", []string{"ben", "বাংলা"}},
63		{"ta", "tamil", []string{"tam", "தமிழ்"}},
64		{"ms", "malay", []string{"msa", "may", "bahasa melayu"}},
65		{"ca", "catalan", []string{"cat", "català"}},
66	}
67	
68	// candidate is one searchable token (code, name, or alias) pointing at a Lang.
69	type candidate struct {
70		token string
71		lang  *Lang
72		// weight scales the fuzzy score: exact code/name matches are preferred.
73		primary bool
74	}
75	
76	var candidates = buildCandidates()
77	
78	func buildCandidates() []candidate {
79		var cs []candidate
80		for i := range table {
81			l := &table[i]
82			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Code), lang: l, primary: true})
83			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Name), lang: l, primary: true})
84			for _, a := range l.Aliases {
85				cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(a), lang: l})
86			}
87		}
88		return cs
89	}
90	
91	// Resolve maps a free-form query to a language, tolerating typos. It returns the
92	// best match plus ranked alternatives (for TUI disambiguation). "auto" and ""
93	// pass through as an auto-detect sentinel (Code "auto", Exact true).
94	func Resolve(q string) (Match, []Match) {
95		norm := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(q))
96		if norm == "" || norm == "auto" {
97			return Match{Code: "auto", Name: "auto-detect", Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
98		}
99	
100		// Exact match on any code / name / alias.
101		for _, c := range candidates {
102			if c.token == norm {
103				return Match{Code: c.lang.Code, Name: c.lang.Name, Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
104			}
105		}
106	
107		// Fuzzy: rank candidates by normalized Levenshtein similarity.
108		type scored struct {
109			lang  *Lang
110			score float64
111		}
112		best := map[string]scored{} // dedupe by language code, keep best score
113		for _, c := range candidates {
114			d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(norm, c.token)
115			maxLen := max(len(norm), len(c.token))
116			if maxLen == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			sim := 1 - float64(d)/float64(maxLen)
120			if !c.primary {
121				sim -= 0.05 // slight preference for code/name over alias
122			}
123			if cur, ok := best[c.lang.Code]; !ok || sim > cur.score {
124				best[c.lang.Code] = scored{lang: c.lang, score: sim}
125			}
126		}
127	
128		ranked := make([]Match, 0, len(best))
129		for _, s := range best {
130			ranked = append(ranked, Match{Code: s.lang.Code, Name: s.lang.Name, Score: s.score})
131		}
132		sort.Slice(ranked, func(i, j int) bool { return ranked[i].Score > ranked[j].Score })
133	
134		if len(ranked) == 0 || ranked[0].Score < 0.5 {
135			// No confident match; echo the query back as an opaque code so callers
136			// can still attempt it (e.g. a code we don't have in the table).
137			return Match{Code: norm, Name: norm, Score: 0}, ranked
138		}
139		top := 3
140		if len(ranked) < top {
141			top = len(ranked)
142		}
143		return ranked[0], ranked[1:top]
144	}
145	
146	// List returns all known languages (for the target-language picker).
147	func List() []Lang {
148		out := make([]Lang, len(table))
149		copy(out, table)
150		return out
151	}
152	
153	// Name returns the English name for a code, or the code itself if unknown.
154	func Name(code string) string {
155		if strings.EqualFold(code, "auto") || code == "" {
156			return "auto-detect"
157		}
158		for i := range table {
159			if strings.EqualFold(table[i].Code, code) {
160				return table[i].Name
161			}
162		}
163		return code
164	}
165
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package lang
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5		"unicode"
6	
7		"github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo"
8	)
9	
10	// Detect returns a best-effort ISO-639-1 source-language code for text, or "" if
11	// the guess is not confident. Used to fill DetectedSource on the LLM path (where
12	// the model returns only the translation) and as a fallback when APIs are down.
13	func Detect(text string) string {
14		info := whatlanggo.Detect(text)
15		if info.Confidence < 0.5 {
16			return ""
17		}
18		return info.Lang.Iso6391()
19	}
20	
21	// IsChinese reports whether text contains any Han characters — used to route
22	// dictionary lookups (Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT).
23	func IsChinese(text string) bool {
24		for _, r := range text {
25			if unicode.Is(unicode.Han, r) {
26				return true
27			}
28		}
29		return false
30	}
31	
32	// containsCJK reports whether text contains any CJK-script rune (Han, Hiragana,
33	// Katakana, or Hangul). This is a reliable, offline signal for routing a
34	// CJK/non-CJK language pair — unlike trigram detection, it is confident even on
35	// very short input.
36	func containsCJK(text string) bool {
37		for _, r := range text {
38			if unicode.Is(unicode.Han, r) || unicode.Is(unicode.Hiragana, r) ||
39				unicode.Is(unicode.Katakana, r) || unicode.Is(unicode.Hangul, r) {
40				return true
41			}
42		}
43		return false
44	}
45	
46	// isCJKLang reports whether a language code is Chinese, Japanese, or Korean.
47	func isCJKLang(code string) bool {
48		switch baseCode(code) {
49		case "zh", "ja", "ko":
50			return true
51		}
52		return false
53	}
54	
55	// PairTarget implements bidirectional "pair" mode: detect which of the two pair
56	// languages the text is written in and return the OTHER one (so "foreign → my
57	// language, my language → foreign" both work from one input box).
58	//
59	// When exactly one side of the pair is a CJK language, routing is decided purely
60	// by script presence — reliable and offline, and symmetric regardless of which
61	// side is "home". This is what makes short Latin input like "test" route to the
62	// CJK side instead of falling through to the wrong language. Same-script pairs
63	// (e.g. en⇄es) fall back to best-effort trigram detection.
64	func PairTarget(home, away, text string) string {
65		if away == "" || away == home {
66			return home
67		}
68		homeCJK, awayCJK := isCJKLang(home), isCJKLang(away)
69		if homeCJK != awayCJK {
70			cjk, latin := home, away
71			if awayCJK {
72				cjk, latin = away, home
73			}
74			if containsCJK(text) {
75				return latin // CJK text → the non-CJK language
76			}
77			return cjk // non-CJK text → the CJK language
78		}
79		// Same-script pair (both or neither CJK): best-effort, symmetric detection.
80		inHome, inAway := inLang(text, home), inLang(text, away)
81		if inHome && !inAway {
82			return away
83		}
84		if inAway && !inHome {
85			return home
86		}
87		return home // inconclusive → default to home
88	}
89	
90	// inLang reports whether text is (best-effort) in the language of code. Chinese
91	// uses a reliable Han-script check; others fall back to offline detection.
92	func inLang(text, code string) bool {
93		base := baseCode(code)
94		switch base {
95		case "", "auto":
96			return false
97		case "zh":
98			return IsChinese(text)
99		default:
100			d := Detect(text)
101			return d != "" && d == base
102		}
103	}
104	
105	// baseCode strips a region suffix: "zh-TW" → "zh".
106	func baseCode(code string) string {
107		code = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(code))
108		if i := strings.IndexByte(code, '-'); i > 0 {
109			return code[:i]
110		}
111		return code
112	}
113

```text
1	// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
2	//
3	// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
4	// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
5	// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
6	// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
7	// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
8	package xdgpath
9	
10	import (
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13	)
14	
15	// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
16	const app = "translate"
17	
18	// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
19	func base(env, def string) string {
20		if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
21			return v
22		}
23		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
24		if err != nil {
25			home = "."
26		}
27		return filepath.Join(home, def)
28	}
29	
30	// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
31	func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }
32	
33	// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
34	func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }
35	
36	// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
37	func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }
38	
39	// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
40	func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }
41	
42	// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
43	func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }
44	
45	// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
46	func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }
47	
48	// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
49	func EnsureDirs() error {
50		for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
51			if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
52				return err
53			}
54		}
55		return nil
56	}
57
```

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"net/url"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
14	)
15	
16	// GoogleConfig configures the free, unofficial Google translate_a/single endpoint.
17	type GoogleConfig struct {
18		Endpoint  string
19		ExtraDT   []string // extra dt slots, e.g. "bd" (bilingual dict), "at" (alternates)
20		UserAgent string
21		Timeout   time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// GoogleEngine is a keyless translation engine using Google's web endpoint.
25	// It is unofficial and best-effort: it can rate-limit (HTTP 429) or change shape
26	// without notice, so it lives at the end of the fallback chain.
27	type GoogleEngine struct {
28		cfg  GoogleConfig
29		http *http.Client
30	}
31	
32	// NewGoogle builds a Google engine with sensible defaults.
33	func NewGoogle(cfg GoogleConfig) *GoogleEngine {
34		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
35			cfg.Endpoint = "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single"
36		}
37		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
38			cfg.Timeout = 4 * time.Second
39		}
40		if cfg.UserAgent == "" {
41			cfg.UserAgent = "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli"
42		}
43		return &GoogleEngine{cfg: cfg, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
44	}
45	
46	// Name returns "google".
47	func (e *GoogleEngine) Name() string { return "google" }
48	
49	// Supports reports that Google handles translation only (not dictionary).
50	func (e *GoogleEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
51	
52	func (e *GoogleEngine) buildURL(req Request) string {
53		sl := req.Source
54		if sl == "" {
55			sl = "auto"
56		}
57		q := url.Values{}
58		q.Set("client", "gtx")
59		q.Set("sl", sl)
60		q.Set("tl", req.Target)
61		q.Set("dt", "t")
62		for _, dt := range e.cfg.ExtraDT {
63			q.Add("dt", dt)
64		}
65		q.Set("q", req.Text)
66		return e.cfg.Endpoint + "?" + q.Encode()
67	}
68	
69	// Translate performs one keyless translation (non-streaming).
70	func (e *GoogleEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
71		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
72			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
73		}
74		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.buildURL(req), nil)
75		if err != nil {
76			return nil, err
77		}
78		httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
79	
80		resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
81		if err != nil {
82			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: %w", err)), nil
83		}
84		defer resp.Body.Close()
85		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
86			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
87			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))), nil
88		}
89	
90		var data []json.RawMessage
91		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&data); err != nil {
92			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: decode: %w", err)), nil
93		}
94		res, err := parseGoogle(data, req)
95		if err != nil {
96			return single(nil, err), nil
97		}
98		res.Engine = e.Name()
99		return single(res, nil), nil
100	}
101	
102	// parseGoogle defensively walks the nested-array response. Positions: data[0] is
103	// an array of [translated, original, ...] segments (long text splits into many);
104	// data[2] is the detected source language.
105	func parseGoogle(data []json.RawMessage, req Request) (*TranslateResult, error) {
106		if len(data) < 1 {
107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: empty payload")
108		}
109		var segs []json.RawMessage
110		if err := json.Unmarshal(data[0], &segs); err != nil {
111			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: bad segments: %w", err)
112		}
113		var b strings.Builder
114		for _, s := range segs {
115			var parts []json.RawMessage
116			if json.Unmarshal(s, &parts) != nil || len(parts) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			var txt string
120			if json.Unmarshal(parts[0], &txt) == nil {
121				b.WriteString(txt)
122			}
123		}
124		res := &TranslateResult{Translation: strings.TrimSpace(b.String()), Target: req.Target}
125		if res.Translation == "" {
126			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: no translation in response")
127		}
128		if len(data) > 2 {
129			var det string
130			if json.Unmarshal(data[2], &det) == nil {
131				res.DetectedSource = det
132			}
133		}
134		return res, nil
135	}
136	
137	// Detect uses the offline detector (cheap, no network round-trip).
138	func (e *GoogleEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
139		return lang.Detect(text), nil
140	}
141	
142	// Available probes the endpoint with a tiny translation request.
143	func (e *GoogleEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
144		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
145		defer cancel()
146		u := e.buildURL(Request{Text: "a", Source: "auto", Target: "en"})
147		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
148		if err != nil {
149			return false
150		}
151		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
152		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
153		if err != nil {
154			return false
155		}
156		defer resp.Body.Close()
157		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
158		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
159	}
160
```

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
8	)
9	
10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- ALWAYS translate into the target language. A single word, name, technical term, or
31	  loanword written in a DIFFERENT language must still be translated — never echo it back.
32	  Only when the text is ALREADY in the target language should you return it (lightly
33	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
34	
35	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
36	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
37	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
38	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
39	
40	Rules:
41	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
42	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
43	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
44	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
45	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
46	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
47	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
48	
49	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
50	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
51	that also gives brief usage examples.
52	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
53	
54	Rules:
55	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
56	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
57	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
58	- Format each example on two lines:
59	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
60	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
61	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
62	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
63	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
64	
65	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
66	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
67		switch preset {
68		case PresetContextual:
69			return translateSystemPromptContextual
70		case PresetDictionary:
71			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
72		default:
73			return translateSystemPromptConcise
74		}
75	}
76	
77	// pairDirective is appended to the system prompt in bidirectional pair mode. It
78	// makes the model own language detection (more robust than a pre-computed target
79	// for short/ambiguous input) and forbids echoing the input — the exact failure
80	// this fixes ("test" → "test" instead of "測試").
81	const pairDirective = `Bidirectional mode: the text is written in ONE of %s or %s. Detect which of the two it is, and translate it into the OTHER one. ALWAYS translate — never return the text unchanged, even for a single word, a proper name, a technical term, or a loanword.`
82	
83	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
84	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
85	Target language: %s
86	
87	Text:
88	%s`
89	
90	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
91	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
92	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
93	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
94		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
95		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
96			src = "auto"
97		} else {
98			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
99		}
100		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
101		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
102		// In pair mode, let the model detect the input language and route to the other
103		// side, and never echo — this is appended to (not a replacement of) the preset
104		// so the chosen output format (concise/contextual/dictionary) is preserved.
105		if req.Pair && req.PairHome != "" && req.PairAway != "" {
106			system += "\n\n" + fmt.Sprintf(pairDirective, lang.Name(req.PairHome), lang.Name(req.PairAway))
107		}
108		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
109			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
110		}
111		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
112	}
113
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/msgs.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9	)
10	
11	// debounceMsg fires debounce after the last keystroke. It carries the seq it was
12	// armed with; a newer keystroke bumps m.seq so the stale tick is ignored.
13	type debounceMsg struct{ seq int }
14	
15	// streamMsg carries one engine Chunk (token, done, or error), stamped with the
16	// request's seq so cancelled/superseded events are dropped in O(1).
17	type streamMsg struct {
18		seq   int
19		chunk engine.Chunk
20	}
21	
22	// flashClearMsg clears a transient footer notice.
23	type flashClearMsg struct{}
24	
25	// flashCmd shows a transient footer notice for ~1.5s.
26	func flashCmd() tea.Cmd {
27		return tea.Tick(1500*time.Millisecond, func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return flashClearMsg{} })
28	}
29	
30	// armDebounce schedules a debounceMsg after d. tea.Tick fires exactly once, so N
31	// keystrokes arm N ticks; the seq guard collapses them to the last.
32	func armDebounce(seq int, d time.Duration) tea.Cmd {
33		return tea.Tick(d, func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return debounceMsg{seq: seq} })
34	}
35	
36	// waitStream reads the next Chunk from ch and wraps it as a seq-stamped msg. The
37	// engine contract guarantees a terminal ChunkDone/ChunkError before close, so a
38	// closed channel without one is surfaced as an error.
39	func waitStream(ch <-chan engine.Chunk, seq int) tea.Cmd {
40		return func() tea.Msg {
41			c, ok := <-ch
42			if !ok {
43				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: engine.ErrNoResult}}
44			}
45			return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: c}
46		}
47	}
48
```
</details></tool-use>

1	package tui

3	import "github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
4	
5	// cacheKey identifies a translation result for the session cache. It is
6	// comparable, so it can be a map key. Keying on the *selected* engine name
7	// (e.g. "auto") means a chain fallback still caches under the user-visible engine.
8	type cacheKey struct {
9		preset, engineName, model, source, target, text string
10	}
11	
12	// cacheKeyFor builds the key for the given input text under the current settings.
13	func (m Model) cacheKeyFor(text string) cacheKey {
14		return cacheKey{
15			preset:     m.preset,
16			engineName: m.active().Name,
17			model:      m.modelOverride,
18			source:     m.source,
19			target:     m.target,
20			text:       text,
21		}
22	}
23	
24	// cacheStore is the session result cache. Session-scoped and unbounded (small);
25	// LLM output is nondeterministic so it is not persisted across sessions.
26	type cacheStore map[cacheKey]*engine.TranslateResult
27

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/debug/debug.go`

```text
1	// Package debug provides an opt-in diagnostic log. When enabled it writes
2	// timestamped trace lines describing the intermediate decisions (config
3	// resolution, pair-mode routing, word-vs-phrase classification, dictionary
4	// hit/miss, chain fallback) so an unexpected translation can be diagnosed.
5	//
6	// It is a cheap no-op until Enable is called. The one-shot CLI enables it to
7	// stderr; the TUI enables it to a log file, because its alt-screen hides stderr.
8	package debug
9	
10	import (
11		"fmt"
12		"io"
13		"sync"
14		"time"
15	)
16	
17	var (
18		mu  sync.Mutex
19		out io.Writer // nil => disabled
20	)
21	
22	// Enable turns debug logging on, directing trace lines to w. Passing nil turns
23	// it back off. Safe to call once at startup.
24	func Enable(w io.Writer) {
25		mu.Lock()
26		defer mu.Unlock()
27		out = w
28	}
29	
30	// Enabled reports whether debug logging is currently on.
31	func Enabled() bool {
32		mu.Lock()
33		defer mu.Unlock()
34		return out != nil
35	}
36	
37	// Logf writes one trace line when enabled; otherwise it does nothing.
38	func Logf(format string, args ...any) {
39		mu.Lock()
40		defer mu.Unlock()
41		if out == nil {
42			return
43		}
44		ts := time.Now().Format("15:04:05.000")
45		fmt.Fprintf(out, "%s translate[debug] "+format+"\n", append([]any{ts}, args...)...)
46	}
47
```
</details></tool-use>

=== justfile ===
# translate — dev tasks (run `just` to list)

# show available recipes
default:
    @just --list

# build the binary into ./translate
build:
    go build -o translate .

# run: `just run "hola mundo" --to en`  ·  `just run` for the TUI
run *ARGS:
    go run . {{ARGS}}

# launch the interactive TUI
tui:
    go run .

# dictionary lookup: `just define ephemeral`
define WORD:
    go run . define {{WORD}}

# guided config wizard
init:
    go run . init

# gofmt, vet, and build
check: fmt vet build

# format the source tree
fmt:
    gofmt -w cmd internal main.go

# static analysis
vet:
    go vet ./...

# tidy go.mod / go.sum
tidy:
    go mod tidy

# run tests
test:
    go test ./...

# install into the chezmoi bin dir (first on PATH)
install: build
    install -m 0755 translate ~/.dotfiles/bin/translate

# remove build artifacts
clean:
    rm -f translate
=== engine errors.go ===
package engine

import "errors"

// Sentinel errors returned across the engine layer.
var (
	// ErrEmptyInput is returned when a request has no text to translate.
	ErrEmptyInput = errors.New("engine: empty input")
	// ErrNoResult is returned when a channel closed without a terminal result.
	ErrNoResult = errors.New("engine: stream closed without a result")
	// ErrNoEngineForMode is returned by the chain when no engine supports the mode.
	ErrNoEngineForMode = errors.New("engine: no engine supports this mode")
	// ErrAllEnginesFailed is returned by the chain when every candidate failed.
	ErrAllEnginesFailed = errors.New("engine: all engines failed")
	// ErrNoDictEntry is returned when a dictionary lookup finds nothing.
	ErrNoDictEntry = errors.New("engine: no dictionary entry")
)

8	// overlay field inherits [general].
9	func TestResolvePerModeOverlay(t *testing.T) {

11		c.General.Preset = "contextual"
12		c.General.Tier = "fast"
13		c.CLI = &Overlay{Preset: strptr("concise"), Tier: strptr("max")}

63		}
64	}
65	
66	// The overlay's model becomes the config-level fallback for the model pick.
67	func TestResolveOverlayModel(t *testing.T) {
68		c := Default()
69		c.TUI = &Overlay{Model: strptr("claude-opus-4-8")}
70		if got := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeTUI); got.Model != "claude-opus-4-8" {
71			t.Errorf("TUI model = %q, want claude-opus-4-8", got.Model)
72		}
73		// CLI has no overlay model → falls through to the provider's tier model.
74		cli := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeCLI)
75		if cli.Model == "claude-opus-4-8" {
76			t.Errorf("CLI model = %q, should not inherit the TUI overlay model", cli.Model)
77		}
78	}
79	
80	// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are resolved through the overlay (TUI-only knobs).
81	func TestResolveOverlayLiveTranslate(t *testing.T) {
82		c := Default()
83		c.General.LiveTranslate = false
84		c.General.DebounceMs = 700
85		c.TUI = &Overlay{LiveTranslate: boolptr(true), DebounceMs: intptr(300)}
86		tui := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeTUI)
87		if !tui.LiveTranslate || tui.DebounceMs != 300 {
88			t.Errorf("TUI live=%v debounce=%d, want true/300", tui.LiveTranslate, tui.DebounceMs)
89		}
90		cli := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeCLI)
91		if cli.LiveTranslate || cli.DebounceMs != 700 {
92			t.Errorf("CLI live=%v debounce=%d, want false/700 (inherited)", cli.LiveTranslate, cli.DebounceMs)
93		}
94	}

96	func boolptr(b bool) *bool { return &b }
97	func intptr(i int) *int    { return &i }

# Cross-Platform TTS / Pronunciation — Implementation Plan

## 0. Verified ground truth (anchors an engineer will touch)

- **No `os/exec`, no `runtime.GOOS`, no `exec.LookPath`, no cache-dir helper anywhere** (grep confirmed). This feature introduces the first `os/exec` usage — it must be small and well-contained.
- **HTTP precedent** (copy this exactly): `internal/engine/google.go:29-44` — per-engine `&http.Client{Timeout}`, `http.NewRequestWithContext` (`google.go:74`), `httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", …)` (`google.go:78`), URL built with `url.Values` + `.Encode()` (`google.go:57-66`).
- **TUI focus mechanism** (locked-decision #2): field `focus focusPane` on the model at `internal/tui/model.go:87`; the enum `focusInput`/`focusOutput` at `model.go:56-61`; toggled by `SwitchFocus` (`tab`/`shift+tab`, `keys.go:19` + `keys.go:36`) in the handler at `update.go:259-268` (blurs the textarea on `focusOutput`, re-focuses on `focusInput`); the view highlights the result box when `m.focus == focusOutput` at `view.go:33`. So "which pane is focused" is read directly off `m.focus`.
- **Current result / input in the TUI**: `m.result *engine.TranslateResult` (`model.go:110`), input text `m.ta.Value()`, and runtime language state `m.source`/`m.target`/`m.pair`/`m.pairWith` (`model.go:90-94`). The existing "act on result" precedent is `copyText()` at `update.go:441` and the Copy case at `update.go:217-226`.
- **CLI runtime pair/lang values** are already computed in `runRoot`: `src, tgt := resolvePair(...)` (`cmd/root.go:141`), `pairWith` (`root.go:142-146`), `res.Pair`/`res.PairWith` (`internal/config/resolve.go:42-43`), effective per-input target via `effectiveTarget()` (`root.go:200-207`). `oneShot` returns `*engine.TranslateResult` (`root.go:374`, `:428`).
- **Language helpers**: `lang.PairTarget(home,away,text)` (`internal/lang/detect.go:64`), `lang.IsChinese` (`detect.go:23`), `lang.Detect`→ISO-639-1 or "" (`detect.go:13`), `lang.Name` (`lang.go:154`). `baseCode` (strip region, `detect.go:106`) is **unexported** — we will add an exported `lang.Base`.
- **Config**: top-level `Config` (`config.go:17-27`), `Overlay` (`config.go:32-46`), `General` (`config.go:49-65`), `Google` struct as a template (`config.go:84-90`), `Default()` seed (`config.go:137-206`). Resolution: `Overrides` (`resolve.go:9-21`), `Resolved` (`resolve.go:33-54`), `Resolve`/`pick`/`applyOverlay` (`resolve.go:118/125/74`). `define.go` reads `cfg.Dict` directly (`define.go:37`) — precedent for reading a config sub-struct without threading it through `Resolved`.
- **xdgpath** has `ConfigDir/DataDir/StateDir` via `base(env,def)` (`paths.go:19-37`) and `EnsureDirs` (`paths.go:49`) — **no `CacheDir`**. We add one.
- **TUI async/msg plumbing** to mirror: `internal/tui/msgs.go` (`flashCmd` `:26`, `waitStream` `:39`, `flashClearMsg` `:23`), `m.flash` field (`model.go:112`), flash handling in `Update` (`update.go:64-66`). Footer labels are hardcoded at `view.go:132` (dict mode) and `view.go:160` (translate mode); footer height reserves the widest state via `footerContent(true)` (`view.go:97`).
- Build/test: `go 1.26.4`, module `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`; `just build` / `just test` / `just check` (justfile). Test convention is table-driven (`internal/config/resolve_test.go`, `internal/engine/smartauto_test.go`).

---

## 1. Package/module layout — `internal/tts/`

New package, UI-agnostic (importable by both `cmd/*` and `internal/tui`), stdlib `net/http` + `os/exec` only.

```
internal/tts/
  tts.go        # Speaker interface, Options, New(), fallback orchestrator, OS detection
  runner.go     # runner interface (exec injection) + execRunner (LookPath/CommandContext)
  native.go     # native offline backend; per-OS argv built from a GOOS param (no build tags)
  google.go     # Google translate_tts backend: download MP3 (net/http) + play via player
  player.go     # audio-player discovery + play (afplay/ffplay/mpv/mpg123/…)
  voices.go     # lang→say/espeak voice maps; lang→google tl normalization
  select.go     # pure text/side + language selection ("副語言" + focus override)
  cache.go      # MP3 cache path + key (sha256)
  errors.go     # ErrNoBackend, ErrNoPlayer, ErrEmptyText
  tts_test.go select_test.go voices_test.go
```

### Interface & orchestrator (tts.go)

```go
type Speaker interface {
    Name() string                                    // "say" | "espeak-ng" | "google"
    Available() bool                                 // binary/player present; cheap, no network
    Speak(ctx context.Context, text, lang string) error // blocks until playback ends / ctx cancelled
}

type Options struct {
    Order     []string          // e.g. ["native","google"]; default per-OS
    Rate      int               // words/min for say/espeak; 0 => backend default
    Voices    map[string]string // lang -> native voice override (from config)
    GoogleURL string            // default https://translate.google.com/translate_tts
    UserAgent string
    Timeout   time.Duration
    CacheDir  string            // "" => xdgpath cache/tts
    Player    string            // "" => auto-discover
    goos      string            // injected in tests; defaults to runtime.GOOS
    runner    runner            // injected in tests; defaults to execRunner
}

func New(opt Options) Speaker   // builds a *fallback wrapping the ordered backends
```

`fallback` (unexported) implements `Speaker`: on `Speak` it iterates its backends, skips `!Available()`, calls the first available `Speak`, and on error continues to the next, aggregating errors; returns `ErrNoBackend` if none were available. OS detection lives in `New`: `goos := opt.goos; if goos=="" { goos = runtime.GOOS }`, then it constructs `newNative(goos, runner, voices, rate)` and `newGoogle(...)` and orders them by `opt.Order`.

### Runner injection (runner.go) — keeps exec testable

```go
type runner interface {
    look(name string) (string, error)                       // exec.LookPath
    run(ctx context.Context, name string, stdin string, args ...string) error // exec.CommandContext; stdin optional
}
type execRunner struct{}
```

`execRunner.run` builds `exec.CommandContext(ctx, name, args...)`, sets `cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(stdin)` when non-empty, discards stdout/stderr, and `Run()`s — so **ctx cancellation kills the child process** (the requested cancellation semantics). Tests inject a `fakeRunner` that records `(name,args,stdin)` and returns programmed `look` hits, so **no process is ever spawned in tests**.

### Native backend (native.go) — single file, GOOS as a parameter (no build tags)

Referencing `"say"`, `"espeak-ng"`, `"powershell"` as strings compiles on every OS; `runner.look` just fails for absent ones. This is what makes darwin/linux/windows argv unit-testable from a single test binary. Per-OS argv (text passed as a **discrete arg or via stdin** — never a shell string):

- **darwin**: `say [-v VOICE] [-r RATE] TEXT`. For very long text (> ~1000 chars) switch to `say -f -` with text on stdin (avoids arg limits; no temp file).
- **linux**: try `espeak-ng`/`espeak`: `espeak-ng [-v VOICE] [-s RATE] TEXT`; else `spd-say [-l LANG] [-r ...] -- TEXT`.
- **windows**: `powershell -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "<script>"` where the script is a fixed literal that does `Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Speech; $s=New-Object System.Speech.Synthesis.SpeechSynthesizer; $s.SelectVoiceByHints(...); $s.Speak([Console]::In.ReadToEnd())` and **the text is fed on stdin** (`runner.run(..., stdin=text)`), so no quoting/injection.

`Available()` = `look(primaryBinary)` succeeds for the OS.

### Google backend (google.go) + player (player.go)

`newGoogle` holds its own `*http.Client{Timeout}` (mirror `engine/google.go:43`). `Speak`:
1. `tl := googleTL(lang)` (voices.go). Split text into ≤200-char chunks on sentence/space boundaries (Google 400s on long `q`).
2. For each chunk build `GoogleURL + "?" + url.Values{ie:UTF-8, client:tw-ob, tl:tl, q:chunk}.Encode()`, GET with `User-Agent` header (`http.NewRequestWithContext`), append MP3 bytes.
3. Write the concatenated MP3 to the cache path (see §7) if not already present, then hand the file to `player.play(ctx, file)`.
`Available()` = a player is discoverable (network is probed lazily at Speak time, not in Available, so it can sit as a fallback without a network round-trip).

**Player discovery (player.go)**, ordered by OS, exec via runner:
- darwin: `afplay FILE` → `ffplay -nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet FILE`
- linux: `mpv --no-video --really-quiet FILE` → `ffplay -nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet FILE` → `mpg123 -q FILE`
- windows: PowerShell WMPlayer COM (MP3) or fall through to native SAPI as the Windows default.

---

## 2. Language → voice mapping (voices.go)

Lookup order for every backend: exact code (e.g. `zh-TW`) → `lang.Base(code)` → backend default (omit the voice flag). Config `Voices` map merges on top of the built-ins.

**macOS `say`** (voices verified present on this host):
```
en→"Samantha"  zh/zh-CN→"Tingting"  zh-TW→"Meijia"  zh-HK→"Sinji"
ja→"Kyoko"  ko→"Yuna"  fr→"Thomas"  es→"Monica"  de→"Anna"  it→"Alice"  ru→"Milena"
```
If `say` errors on a missing voice, retry once without `-v` (graceful degrade). 

**Google `tl` normalization**: identity for base codes; `zh`/`zh-CN`→`zh-CN`, `zh-TW`/`zh-HK`→`zh-TW`, everything else → `lang.Base`.

**espeak-ng**: `zh`→`cmn`, `zh-HK`→`yue`, `ja`→`ja`, `ko`→`ko`, else `lang.Base`.

Add **`lang.Base(code string) string`** in `internal/lang/detect.go` (export the existing `baseCode` logic at `detect.go:106`) so tts and voices reuse it rather than re-implementing region stripping.

---

## 3. Text/side selection logic (select.go) — "副語言" + focus (pure function)

```go
type Side int
const ( SideAuto Side = iota; SideSource; SideResult )

type SelectInput struct {
    SourceText, SourceLang string // user input + resolved source code ("auto"/"" ok)
    ResultText, ResultLang string // translation OR dict head word + effective target code
    Foreign  string               // the 副/away language to prefer ("" => derive)
    Forced   Side                 // TUI focus override; SideAuto elsewhere
}
type Choice struct{ Text, Lang string }

func Select(in SelectInput) (Choice, bool) // bool=false when nothing speakable
```

Algorithm (concrete):
1. `Forced == SideSource` → `Choice{SourceText, langOf(SourceText, SourceLang)}`; if empty, fall through to auto.
2. `Forced == SideResult` → `Choice{ResultText, langOf(ResultText, ResultLang)}`; if empty, fall through.
3. `SideAuto`: compute `sl := langOf(SourceText, SourceLang)`, `rl := langOf(ResultText, ResultLang)`. Determine `foreign`:
   - `Foreign` if set; else "prefer the non-Chinese side" (user is zh-native): if exactly one of `{sl,rl}` is non-`zh`, that's `foreign`; if ambiguous, default `foreign = rl` (the translation).
   - Speak `ResultText` if `Base(rl)==Base(foreign)`, else `SourceText` if `Base(sl)==Base(foreign)`, else default to `ResultText`. Guard: if the chosen side is empty, use the other; if both empty return `false`.

`langOf(text, hint)`: `lang.IsChinese(text)` → refine to `hint` when `Base(hint)=="zh"` (keeps zh-TW/zh-HK region for the voice), else `"zh"`; else `lang.Detect(text)` if non-empty; else `hint`. Region (zh-TW vs zh-CN) can only come from the hint code, never from detection — that's why the caller passes `SourceLang`/`ResultLang`.

**Runtime values available to callers:** CLI — `src`, `effTgt`, `r.Translation` / `r.Dictionary`, `pairWith` (root.go:141-146,169-174). TUI — `m.source`, `m.target`, `m.result`, `m.ta.Value()`, `m.pairWith` (model.go:90-94,110).

---

## 4. Config schema (config.go)

Add a top-level `TTS TTS` field to `Config` (after `SmartDict`, `config.go:25`) and the struct (model it on `Google`, `config.go:84-90`):

```go
type TTS struct {
    Enabled       bool              `toml:"enabled"`
    AutoSpeak     bool              `toml:"auto_speak"`        // CLI: speak every result w/o --speak (default false)
    Order         []string          `toml:"order,omitempty"`   // ["native","google"]
    Foreign       string            `toml:"foreign,omitempty"` // 副 language; "" => derive
    PreferForeign bool              `toml:"prefer_foreign"`    // auto-side selection (default true)
    Rate          int               `toml:"rate,omitempty"`    // words/min; 0 => backend default
    Voices        map[string]string `toml:"voices,omitempty"`  // lang -> native voice
    GoogleTTSURL  string            `toml:"google_tts_url,omitempty"`
    UserAgent     string            `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
    TimeoutMs     int               `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
    CacheDir      string            `toml:"cache_dir,omitempty"`
    Player        string            `toml:"player,omitempty"`
}
```

`Default()` seed (add after the `SmartDict` block, `config.go:196-200`):
```go
TTS: TTS{
    Enabled: true, AutoSpeak: false, Order: []string{"native", "google"},
    Foreign: "", PreferForeign: true,
    GoogleTTSURL: "https://translate.google.com/translate_tts",
    UserAgent: "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli", TimeoutMs: 5000,
},
```

**Overlay / Resolved / env plumbing — deliberately NOT needed for v1.** `--speak` is a per-invocation trigger (like `--json`, `flagJSON` at `root.go:41`), not a resolved setting, so it does not go through `Resolve`. Everything else is read directly from `cfg.TTS`, exactly as `define.go:37` reads `cfg.Dict`. This keeps `Resolved` (`resolve.go:33`) and `applyOverlay` (`resolve.go:74`) untouched. (If per-front-end `auto_speak` is wanted later, add an `*bool` to `Overlay` and one line in `applyOverlay` — noted as a follow-up, not v1.)

Config sub-tests go in a new `internal/config/tts_test.go` verifying `Default().TTS` seeds and that unknown keys don't clobber (Load starts from `Default()`, `config.go:231`).

---

## 5. CLI wiring

**Flags (root.go):** add `flagSpeak bool`, `flagSpeakLang string` to the var block (`root.go:30-44`); register as **persistent** flags (so `define` and `speak` inherit) after `root.go:77`:
```go
f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud")
f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")
```
`overrides()` (`root.go:90`) is **not** touched — speak is not a `Resolved` field.

**`runRoot` (root.go):** after each `oneShot` result is printed and `recordAndRemember` runs — both the args branch (`root.go:170-174`) and the stdin branch (`root.go:186-192`) — add, guarded by `(flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak) && cfg.TTS.Enabled`:
```go
speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, pairWith) // helper in a new cmd/speak.go
```
`speakResult` builds `tts.New(optsFromCfg(cfg))`, computes `tts.Select(SelectInput{SourceText:text, SourceLang:src, ResultText:r.Translation, ResultLang:effTgt, Foreign:foreignFor(cfg,res,pairWith), Forced:SideAuto})` (or `--speak-lang` overrides `Choice.Lang`), then `sp.Speak(ctx, choice.Text, choice.Lang)`; on error prints `translate: speech unavailable (%v)` to stderr (never fatal). Speaking happens **after** stdout render so audio never interleaves with streamed tokens.

**`define.go`:** after `renderDict` (`define.go:62`), if `flagSpeak`, speak the looked-up word itself: `text = res2.Dictionary.Word` (fallback `res2.Translation`), `lang = detect(word)` — define pronounces the entry regardless of foreign preference.

**Standalone subcommand — new `cmd/speak.go`:**
```
translate speak <text...> [--lang code] [--backend native|google] [--voice name]
```
`newSpeakCmd`: `config.Load()`, build `tts.New`, resolve lang from `--lang`/`--speak-lang` or `lang.Detect(text)`, `Speak(cmd.Context(), text, lang)`. Register in `root.AddCommand(...)` at `root.go:80`. This is the fastest end-to-end smoke path.

---

## 6. TUI wiring

**Key (keys.go):** add `Speak key.Binding` to `keyMap` (`keys.go:7-21`) and to `defaultKeys` (`keys.go:24-38`):
```go
Speak: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+s"), key.WithHelp("^s", "speak")),
```
Note in a comment: `ctrl+s` is XOFF flow-control only under cooked-mode terminals; Bubble Tea puts the TTY in **raw mode (IXON disabled)**, so `ctrl+s` is delivered as a normal key here — safe. (Fallback keys if desired: `ctrl+b` or `ctrl+j`.)

**Handler (update.go):** add a case in `handleKey` mirroring the Copy case (`update.go:217-226`), before the `focusOutput` scroll block:
```go
case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Speak):
    if m.p.Speaker == nil { m.flash = "tts disabled"; return m, flashCmd() }
    forced := SideAuto
    if m.focus == focusOutput { forced = tts.SideResult } // explicit output focus → speak result
    in := tts.SelectInput{
        SourceText: m.ta.Value(), SourceLang: m.source,
        ResultText: resultSpeakText(m.result), ResultLang: m.target,
        Foreign: m.p.Foreign, Forced: forced,
    }
    ch, ok := tts.Select(in)
    if !ok { m.flash = "nothing to speak"; return m, flashCmd() }
    m.stopSpeak()                       // debounce: cancel any in-flight playback
    ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
    m.speakCancel = cancel
    m.flash = "speaking…"
    return m, tea.Batch(flashCmd(), speakCmd(m.p.Speaker, ctx, ch))
```

Design note on focus (locked-decision #2): `focusInput` is the *default*, not an explicit choice, so only `focusOutput` (user pressed Tab, `update.go:259-268`) forces `SideResult`; `focusInput` uses auto/副 selection so a foreign input can still be heard. Cite `m.focus` at `model.go:87`.

**Async playback (msgs.go):** add
```go
type speakDoneMsg struct{ err error }
func speakCmd(sp tts.Speaker, ctx context.Context, ch tts.Choice) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg { return speakDoneMsg{sp.Speak(ctx, ch.Text, ch.Lang)} }
}
```
Handle `speakDoneMsg` in `Update` (next to `flashClearMsg`, `update.go:64`): set `m.flash = ""` on success or `"speak failed"` on error, clear `m.speakCancel`. Because `Speak` runs inside the `tea.Cmd` goroutine, **the UI never blocks**.

**Model state (model.go):** add `speakCancel context.CancelFunc` (near `cancel`, `model.go:120`) and a `stopSpeak()` helper (mirror `cancelInflight`, `update.go:420`). Call `stopSpeak()` in the Quit (`update.go:174-176`) and Clear (`update.go:246`) cases so exit/clear kills playback.

**Params plumbing (root.go `runTUI` + tui.Params):** build the speaker once in `runTUI` (`root.go:319-353`) from `cfg.TTS` and add two `tui.Params` fields (`model.go:28-42`): `Speaker tts.Speaker` and `Foreign string`. Set `m.p.Speaker`/`m.p.Foreign` in `New`. `tui` importing `tts` is fine (tts is UI-agnostic, like `engine`).

**Footer hint (view.go):** append `^s speak` to both hardcoded help strings — dict mode at `view.go:132` and translate mode at `view.go:160`. Height auto-reserves via `footerContent(true)` (`view.go:97`), so wrapping is handled.

---

## 7. Caching & cancellation

**Add `xdgpath.CacheDir()`** (mirror `DataDir`, `paths.go:34`):
```go
func CacheDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CACHE_HOME", ".cache"), app) }
```
tts joins its own subdir `<CacheDir>/tts` and `os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o700)` lazily on first write (do **not** force it into `EnsureDirs` at `paths.go:49` — cache is optional). `cfg.TTS.CacheDir` overrides.

**Cache key (cache.go):** `sha256.Sum256([]byte(tl + "\x00" + text))` → hex, file `<dir>/<hash>.mp3`. On Speak: if the file exists and is non-empty, skip download and play it; else download → write atomically (temp + rename, like `config.Save` at `config.go:248-261`) → play. Cache is unbounded for v1 (document, like the session cache note at `internal/tui/cache.go:24`; an LRU/size cap is a follow-up).

**Cancellation:** every exec goes through `exec.CommandContext(ctx, …)` and HTTP through `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, …)`. The ctx is `cmd.Context()` (CLI, already SIGINT-cancellable) or `m.base`-derived (TUI, cancelled by `stopSpeak`/Quit). Cancelling the ctx kills `say`/`afplay`/`ffplay` mid-playback.

---

## 8. Error handling & UX

- **No backend/player found** → `fallback.Speak` returns `ErrNoBackend`; CLI: `translate: speech unavailable (no TTS backend found; install espeak-ng or a player like ffplay)`; TUI flash `no TTS backend`.
- **Network failure (Google)** → wrapped error; if native ran first (default `Order`) it never reaches Google. If Google is first/only and fails, the aggregate error surfaces; TUI flash `speak failed`.
- **Unsupported language** → voice-map miss falls back to the backend default voice (omit `-v`); still speaks. Google `tl` miss → base code; a 4xx surfaces as an error.
- **Empty text** → `Select` returns `false`; no-op (`nothing to speak`). `Speak` also guards empty text (`ErrEmptyText`), mirroring `engine.ErrEmptyInput` (`engine/errors.go`).
- Define sentinels in `internal/tts/errors.go` following `internal/engine/errors.go`.

---

## 9. Testing

- **`select_test.go`** (table-driven, style of `smartauto_test.go`): `foreign=en` + Chinese source + English result → speaks result/en; English source + Chinese result → speaks source/en; `Forced=SideResult`/`SideSource` overrides; region preserved (zh-TW hint kept for a Chinese pick); empty-side fallback; both-empty → `ok=false`.
- **`voices_test.go`**: `say`/espeak/google mappings for `en, zh, zh-CN, zh-TW, zh-HK, ja`; `lang.Base` fallback; config `Voices` override wins.
- **`tts_test.go`** with a `fakeRunner`: assert fallback **ordering** (native before google, skip unavailable via programmed `look` hits); assert `run` argv is `["say","-v","Meijia","測試"]`-shaped (**no shell string**, text is a discrete arg); assert Windows path feeds text on **stdin**; assert ctx cancellation propagates (cancelled ctx → `run` sees `ctx.Err()`). Backends are constructed directly with the fake `runner` + injected `goos`, so all three OSes are exercised from one `go test` run — no build tags, no real processes.
- **`config` test**: `Default().TTS` seeds; TOML round-trips.
- **Smoke (manual)**: `just build` then `./translate speak "hello world"`; `./translate speak "測試一下" --lang zh-TW`; `./translate "hola mundo" --to en --speak`; `./translate define ephemeral --speak`; TUI via `just tui` — type + Enter, press `^s` (input focus → foreign/副 side), then Tab (→ `focusOutput`) + `^s` (speaks the result pane). Use `--debug` / the TUI debug log (`root.go:225`) to confirm the chosen side+lang.

---

## 10. Risks / edge cases (each addressed above)

- **`ffplay` must be `-nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet`** or it opens a window and hangs; `afplay FILE` is plain (player.go).
- **`say` takes text directly** (no temp file); long text (>~1000 chars) switches to `say -f -` on stdin to dodge argv limits.
- **Google `translate_tts`** needs `client=tw-ob` + a browser-ish `User-Agent`, rate-limits (429), and 400s on long `q` → chunk to ≤200 chars and concat MP3 parts.
- **Long text** capped (~1000 chars) to bound both Google requests and runaway audio.
- **Overlapping playback**: TUI debounces via `stopSpeak`/`speakCancel`; CLI is synchronous.
- **Shell-injection safety**: never a shell string — always `exec.CommandContext(ctx, bin, args...)` with text as a discrete arg or on stdin, and `url.Values.Encode()` for the Google query (mirror `engine/google.go:65`).
- **Region voices** (zh-HK Sinji) may be absent → retry `say` without `-v`, then default.
- **Windows PowerShell startup latency** (~300-700ms) — acceptable; noted.

---

- /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/config/config.go  (add `TTS` struct + `Default()` seed; anchors 17-27, 84-90, 196-206)

- /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/update.go  (Speak key case mirroring Copy 217-226; `speakDoneMsg`/`stopSpeak`; focus read at 259-268)
- /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/model.go  (focus field 87, add `Speaker`/`Foreign` Params 28-42 + `speakCancel` state)

```md
# Plan: Free cross-platform text-to-speech (讀音 / pronunciation)

## Context

The user asked (paraphrased): *"Is there a free text-to-speech option? When looking up
words I sometimes want to hear the pronunciation (讀音)."* Today the tool shows phonetic
**text** (`DictEntry.Phonetic` — IPA for English, numbered pinyin like `ce4 shi4` for
Chinese) but can never **speak** anything: there is zero audio, zero `os/exec`, and no TTS
code in the repo.

Goal: add a **free, cross-platform TTS** capability so a lookup/translation can be heard,
triggered on demand from both the CLI and the TUI.

Decisions locked with the user:
- **Backend:** cross-platform, each OS with its own fallback chain — native offline first
  (macOS `say`, Linux `espeak-ng`/`spd-say`, Windows PowerShell SAPI), then a shared online
  fallback (**Google Translate TTS** MP3 → discovered player). Free; no API key.
- **What to speak:** the **secondary/foreign ("副") language** — for this user usually `en`
  — with the **TUI tab-focus** as an explicit override (focus the result pane → speak the
  result). Native-language input is not the target unless focused.
- **No phonetic-text changes** (pinyin stays `ce4 shi4`). Audio only.
- **Verified on this machine:** `say`, `afplay`, `ffplay` present; `say` has zh_CN
  (Tingting), zh_TW (Meijia), zh_HK (Sinji) + English voices.

This introduces the repo's **first `os/exec` usage** — it must stay small, injectable, and
never build a shell string (pass text as a discrete arg or on stdin).

## New package: `internal/tts/` (UI-agnostic, like `internal/engine`)

\```
tts.go     Speaker interface, Options, New() fallback orchestrator, runtime.GOOS detection
runner.go  runner interface (exec injection) + execRunner{} — keeps exec testable
native.go  native offline backend; per-OS argv from a GOOS param (NO build tags)
google.go  Google translate_tts: download MP3 (net/http) → play
player.go  audio-player discovery (afplay/ffplay/mpv/mpg123) + play
voices.go  lang→say/espeak voice map; lang→google `tl` normalization
select.go  PURE side/lang selection ("副語言" + focus override)
cache.go   MP3 cache path + sha256 key
errors.go  ErrNoBackend, ErrNoPlayer, ErrEmptyText (mirror internal/engine/errors.go)
\```

Core shapes:

\```go
type Speaker interface {
    Name() string
    Available() bool                                     // binary/player present; no network
    Speak(ctx context.Context, text, lang string) error  // blocks until playback ends / ctx done
}
type Options struct {
    Order []string; Rate int; Voices map[string]string
    GoogleURL, UserAgent, CacheDir, Player string; Timeout time.Duration
    goos string; runner runner  // injected in tests; default runtime.GOOS + execRunner
}
func New(opt Options) Speaker    // *fallback wrapping ordered backends
\```

- **runner injection** (`runner.go`): `look(name)`→`exec.LookPath`; `run(ctx,name,stdin,args...)`
  →`exec.CommandContext` (ctx cancellation kills the child; **stdin** carries long/untrusted
  text). Tests inject a `fakeRunner` — **no process is ever spawned in tests**.
- **native.go** references `"say"`/`"espeak-ng"`/`"powershell"` as strings so it compiles on
  every OS and all three argv shapes are unit-testable from one `go test`. macOS:
  `say [-v VOICE] [-r RATE] TEXT` (long text → `say -f -` on stdin). Windows: text on stdin
  into a fixed `System.Speech` script (no quoting/injection).
- **google.go**: own `*http.Client{Timeout}`, `http.NewRequestWithContext`, `url.Values.Encode()`,
  browser-ish `User-Agent` — copy `internal/engine/google.go:29-78` exactly. Chunk `q` to
  ≤200 chars, concat MP3 parts, cache, then `player.play`.

## Reuse (do not reinvent)

- HTTP pattern: `internal/engine/google.go:29-78` (client, `NewRequestWithContext`, User-Agent, `url.Values`).
- TUI "act on current result" precedent: Copy case `internal/tui/update.go:217-226`, `copyText()` at `update.go:441`.
- TUI async/flash plumbing: `internal/tui/msgs.go` (`flashCmd`), `m.flash` (`model.go:112`), flash handling `update.go:64-66`.
- Atomic file write: `config.Save` temp+rename, `internal/config/config.go:248-261`.
- Language helpers: `lang.IsChinese`/`lang.Detect` (`internal/lang/detect.go:23/13`). **Add** exported
  `lang.Base(code)` by exporting the existing `baseCode` region-strip at `detect.go:106` (reused by voices + select).

## Side/language selection — `select.go` (pure, table-tested)

\```go
type Side int; const ( SideAuto Side = iota; SideSource; SideResult )
type SelectInput struct { SourceText, SourceLang, ResultText, ResultLang, Foreign string; Forced Side }
type Choice struct{ Text, Lang string }
func Select(in SelectInput) (Choice, bool)
\```

Logic: `Forced==SideSource/SideResult` → that pane (fall through if empty). `SideAuto` →
speak the side whose language matches `Foreign`; if `Foreign==""` derive it as the
**non-`zh` side** (user is zh-native), defaulting to the result when ambiguous. Region
(zh-TW vs zh-CN) comes only from the passed `*Lang` hint, never from detection.
Runtime inputs already exist: CLI `src`/`effTgt`/`r.Translation`/`pairWith`
(`cmd/root.go:141-146,169-174`); TUI `m.source`/`m.target`/`m.result`/`m.ta.Value()`/`m.pairWith`
(`internal/tui/model.go:90-94,110`).

## Config — `internal/config/config.go`

Add `TTS TTS` to `Config` (after `SmartDict`, ~`config.go:25`), struct modeled on `Google`
(`config.go:84-90`), and seed it in `Default()` (~`config.go:196`):

\```go
type TTS struct {
    Enabled, AutoSpeak, PreferForeign bool
    Order   []string          // ["native","google"]
    Foreign string            // 副 language; "" => derive
    Rate    int; Voices map[string]string
    GoogleTTSURL, UserAgent, CacheDir, Player string; TimeoutMs int
}
// Default: Enabled:true, AutoSpeak:false, Order:["native","google"], PreferForeign:true,
//          GoogleTTSURL:"https://translate.google.com/translate_tts", UserAgent:"Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli", TimeoutMs:5000
\```

`--speak` is a per-invocation trigger (like `--json`), **not** a `Resolved` field, so
`Resolved`/`applyOverlay`/env plumbing stay untouched; read `cfg.TTS` directly the way
`define.go:37` reads `cfg.Dict`. (`auto_speak` per-front-end overlay = documented follow-up.)

## CLI wiring — `cmd/root.go`, `cmd/define.go`, new `cmd/speak.go`

- Persistent flags after `root.go:77`: `--speak/-s` (bool) and `--speak-lang` (string). Leave `overrides()` untouched.
- In `runRoot`, after each `oneShot` result renders + `recordAndRemember` (args branch
  `root.go:170-174`, stdin branch `root.go:186-192`), guard `(flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak) && cfg.TTS.Enabled`
  → call a `speakResult(...)` helper (new `cmd/speak.go`) that builds `tts.New`, runs
  `tts.Select`, and `Speak`s. Speak **after** stdout so audio never interleaves streamed tokens; errors go to stderr, never fatal.
- `define.go`: after `renderDict` (`define.go:62`), `--speak` pronounces the entry itself
  (`res.Dictionary.Word`, lang from detection) regardless of foreign preference.
- New subcommand `translate speak <text...> [--lang] [--backend] [--voice]` registered at
  `root.go:80` — fastest end-to-end smoke path.

## TUI wiring — `keys.go`, `update.go`, `model.go`, `msgs.go`, `view.go`, `cmd/root.go`

- `keys.go`: add `Speak = ctrl+s` (`^s speak`). Comment: Bubble Tea raw mode disables IXON,
  so `ctrl+s` is delivered normally (fallback `ctrl+b`/`ctrl+j` if ever needed).
- `update.go`: new case mirroring Copy (`update.go:217-226`): if `m.focus==focusOutput`
  force `SideResult`, else `SideAuto`; run `tts.Select`, then `m.stopSpeak()` (debounce) and
  return `speakCmd(...)` as a `tea.Cmd` (non-blocking). Flash `speaking…` / `nothing to speak` / `no TTS backend`.
- `msgs.go`: `speakDoneMsg{err}` + `speakCmd` running `Speaker.Speak` in the cmd goroutine; handle it near `flashClearMsg` (`update.go:64`).
- `model.go`: add `speakCancel context.CancelFunc` + `stopSpeak()` (mirror `cancelInflight`,
  `update.go:420`); call it in Quit (`update.go:174-176`) and Clear (`update.go:246`).
- `tui.Params` (`model.go:28-42`): add `Speaker tts.Speaker` and `Foreign string`, built once
  in `runTUI` (`root.go:319-353`) from `cfg.TTS`.
- `view.go`: append `^s speak` to the two hardcoded footer strings (dict `view.go:132`, translate `view.go:160`); height auto-reserves via `footerContent(true)`.

## Caching & cancellation

- Add `xdgpath.CacheDir()` mirroring `DataDir` (`internal/xdgpath/paths.go:34`); tts uses
  `<CacheDir>/tts`, `os.MkdirAll(...,0o700)` lazily (NOT in `EnsureDirs`). `cfg.TTS.CacheDir` overrides.
- Cache key `sha256(tl + "\x00" + text)` → `<hash>.mp3`; hit ⇒ replay, miss ⇒ download →
  atomic temp+rename → play. Unbounded for v1 (LRU cap = follow-up).
- All exec via `exec.CommandContext`, all HTTP via `http.NewRequestWithContext`; CLI ctx is
  SIGINT-cancellable, TUI ctx cancelled by `stopSpeak`/Quit — cancel kills playback mid-word.

## Error / UX handling

No backend/player → `ErrNoBackend`, CLI stderr "speech unavailable (install espeak-ng or a
player like ffplay)", TUI flash `no TTS backend`. Missing voice → retry `say` without `-v`,
then default. Empty text → `Select` returns false / `ErrEmptyText`. `ffplay` must use
`-nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet`. Google needs `client=tw-ob` + User-Agent; 429/400 surface as errors.

## Verification

1. `just check` (fmt+vet+build) and `just test` — unit tests:
   - `select_test.go`: foreign=en with zh↔en source/result picks the en side; `Forced` overrides; zh-TW region preserved; empty-side fallback; both-empty ⇒ false.
   - `voices_test.go`: say/espeak/google maps for en, zh, zh-CN, zh-TW, zh-HK, ja; `lang.Base` fallback; config `Voices` override wins.
   - `tts_test.go` (fakeRunner): fallback ordering (native before google, skip unavailable); argv is `["say","-v","Meijia","測試"]`-shaped (no shell string); Windows uses stdin; cancelled ctx propagates. No real process spawned.
   - `config` test: `Default().TTS` seeds + TOML round-trip.
2. Manual smoke (audible):
   - `./translate speak "hello world"` → hears English (`say`/`afplay`).
   - `./translate speak "測試一下" --lang zh-TW` → Meijia voice.
   - `./translate "hola mundo" --to en --speak` → speaks the English (副) side.
   - `./translate define ephemeral --speak` → pronounces the headword.
   - `just tui`: type + Enter, `^s` (input focus → 副/foreign side); `Tab` → result pane, `^s` (speaks result). `--debug` confirms chosen side+lang.
   - Force Google path: `[tts].order = ["google"]` (or offline) and repeat `speak` to exercise MP3 fetch+cache+player.

## Critical files

- `internal/tts/*` — new package (all files above).
- `internal/config/config.go` — `TTS` struct + `Default()` seed.
- `internal/xdgpath/paths.go` — add `CacheDir()`.
- `internal/lang/detect.go` — export `lang.Base`.
- `cmd/root.go`, `cmd/define.go`, new `cmd/speak.go` — flags, speak-after-render, subcommand, TUI Params.
- `internal/tui/keys.go`, `update.go`, `model.go`, `msgs.go`, `view.go` — `^s` key, handler, state, async msg, footer.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-13 15:10:28Z)**_

計劃已寫好。簡短回答你的問題:

**有,而且完全免費、跨平台。** 這是 Go 專案,所以做法是包一層 `Speaker` 抽象,每個 OS 有自己的 fallback 鏈:
- **離線內建**(免網路、免金鑰):macOS `say`(這台已確認有婷婷/美佳/善怡+英文)、Linux `espeak-ng`/`spd-say`、Windows PowerShell SAPI。
- **線上備援**(跨平台、音質較好):Google 翻譯 TTS 抓 MP3 → 用 `afplay`/`ffplay` 播放,並在 XDG cache 快取。

依你的選擇:**唸「副語言」**(對你通常是 en),並讓 TUI 的 **Tab focus** 當明確覆寫(focus 到結果框 → 唸結果);拼音文字顯示維持現狀,只做語音。CLI 用 `--speak/-s` 旗標 + 新的 `translate speak` 子指令,TUI 用 `^s`。

## Approved Plan (edited by user):
# Plan: Free cross-platform text-to-speech (讀音 / pronunciation)

## Context

The user asked (paraphrased): *"Is there a free text-to-speech option? When looking up
words I sometimes want to hear the pronunciation (讀音)."* Today the tool shows phonetic
**text** (`DictEntry.Phonetic` — IPA for English, numbered pinyin like `ce4 shi4` for
Chinese) but can never **speak** anything: there is zero audio, zero `os/exec`, and no TTS
code in the repo.

Goal: add a **free, cross-platform TTS** capability so a lookup/translation can be heard,
triggered on demand from both the CLI and the TUI.

Decisions locked with the user:
- **Backend:** cross-platform, each OS with its own fallback chain — native offline first
  (macOS `say`, Linux `espeak-ng`/`spd-say`, Windows PowerShell SAPI), then a shared online
  fallback (**Google Translate TTS** MP3 → discovered player). Free; no API key.
- **What to speak:** the **secondary/foreign ("副") language** — for this user usually `en`
  — with the **TUI tab-focus** as an explicit override (focus the result pane → speak the
  result). Native-language input is not the target unless focused.
- **No phonetic-text changes** (pinyin stays `ce4 shi4`). Audio only.
- **Verified on this machine:** `say`, `afplay`, `ffplay` present; `say` has zh_CN
  (Tingting), zh_TW (Meijia), zh_HK (Sinji) + English voices.

This introduces the repo's **first `os/exec` usage** — it must stay small, injectable, and
never build a shell string (pass text as a discrete arg or on stdin).

## New package: `internal/tts/` (UI-agnostic, like `internal/engine`)

```
tts.go     Speaker interface, Options, New() fallback orchestrator, runtime.GOOS detection
runner.go  runner interface (exec injection) + execRunner{} — keeps exec testable
native.go  native offline backend; per-OS argv from a GOOS param (NO build tags)
google.go  Google translate_tts: download MP3 (net/http) → play
player.go  audio-player discovery (afplay/ffplay/mpv/mpg123) + play
voices.go  lang→say/espeak voice map; lang→google `tl` normalization
select.go  PURE side/lang selection ("副語言" + focus override)
cache.go   MP3 cache path + sha256 key
errors.go  ErrNoBackend, ErrNoPlayer, ErrEmptyText (mirror internal/engine/errors.go)
```

Core shapes:

```go
type Speaker interface {
    Name() string
    Available() bool                                     // binary/player present; no network
    Speak(ctx context.Context, text, lang string) error  // blocks until playback ends / ctx done
}
type Options struct {
    Order []string; Rate int; Voices map[string]string
    GoogleURL, UserAgent, CacheDir, Player string; Timeout time.Duration
    goos string; runner runner  // injected in tests; default runtime.GOOS + execRunner
}
func New(opt Options) Speaker    // *fallback wrapping ordered backends
```

- **runner injection** (`runner.go`): `look(name)`→`exec.LookPath`; `run(ctx,name,stdin,args...)`
  →`exec.CommandContext` (ctx cancellation kills the child; **stdin** carries long/untrusted
  text). Tests inject a `fakeRunner` — **no process is ever spawned in tests**.
- **native.go** references `"say"`/`"espeak-ng"`/`"powershell"` as strings so it compiles on
  every OS and all three argv shapes are unit-testable from one `go test`. macOS:
  `say [-v VOICE] [-r RATE] TEXT` (long text → `say -f -` on stdin). Windows: text on stdin
  into a fixed `System.Speech` script (no quoting/injection).
- **google.go**: own `*http.Client{Timeout}`, `http.NewRequestWithContext`, `url.Values.Encode()`,
  browser-ish `User-Agent` — copy `internal/engine/google.go:29-78` exactly. Chunk `q` to
  ≤200 chars, concat MP3 parts, cache, then `player.play`.

## Reuse (do not reinvent)

- HTTP pattern: `internal/engine/google.go:29-78` (client, `NewRequestWithContext`, User-Agent, `url.Values`).
- TUI "act on current result" precedent: Copy case `internal/tui/update.go:217-226`, `copyText()` at `update.go:441`.
- TUI async/flash plumbing: `internal/tui/msgs.go` (`flashCmd`), `m.flash` (`model.go:112`), flash handling `update.go:64-66`.
- Atomic file write: `config.Save` temp+rename, `internal/config/config.go:248-261`.
- Language helpers: `lang.IsChinese`/`lang.Detect` (`internal/lang/detect.go:23/13`). **Add** exported
  `lang.Base(code)` by exporting the existing `baseCode` region-strip at `detect.go:106` (reused by voices + select).

## Side/language selection — `select.go` (pure, table-tested)

```go
type Side int; const ( SideAuto Side = iota; SideSource; SideResult )
type SelectInput struct { SourceText, SourceLang, ResultText, ResultLang, Foreign string; Forced Side }
type Choice struct{ Text, Lang string }
func Select(in SelectInput) (Choice, bool)
```

Logic: `Forced==SideSource/SideResult` → that pane (fall through if empty). `SideAuto` →
speak the side whose language matches `Foreign`; if `Foreign==""` derive it as the
**non-`zh` side** (user is zh-native), defaulting to the result when ambiguous. Region
(zh-TW vs zh-CN) comes only from the passed `*Lang` hint, never from detection.
Runtime inputs already exist: CLI `src`/`effTgt`/`r.Translation`/`pairWith`
(`cmd/root.go:141-146,169-174`); TUI `m.source`/`m.target`/`m.result`/`m.ta.Value()`/`m.pairWith`
(`internal/tui/model.go:90-94,110`).

## Config — `internal/config/config.go`

Add `TTS TTS` to `Config` (after `SmartDict`, ~`config.go:25`), struct modeled on `Google`
(`config.go:84-90`), and seed it in `Default()` (~`config.go:196`):

```go
type TTS struct {
    Enabled, AutoSpeak, PreferForeign bool
    Order   []string          // ["native","google"]
    Foreign string            // 副 language; "" => derive
    Rate    int; Voices map[string]string
    GoogleTTSURL, UserAgent, CacheDir, Player string; TimeoutMs int
}
// Default: Enabled:true, AutoSpeak:false, Order:["native","google"], PreferForeign:true,
//          GoogleTTSURL:"https://translate.google.com/translate_tts", UserAgent:"Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli", TimeoutMs:5000
```

`--speak` is a per-invocation trigger (like `--json`), **not** a `Resolved` field, so
`Resolved`/`applyOverlay`/env plumbing stay untouched; read `cfg.TTS` directly the way
`define.go:37` reads `cfg.Dict`. (`auto_speak` per-front-end overlay = documented follow-up.)

## CLI wiring — `cmd/root.go`, `cmd/define.go`, new `cmd/speak.go`

- Persistent flags after `root.go:77`: `--speak/-s` (bool) and `--speak-lang` (string). Leave `overrides()` untouched.
- In `runRoot`, after each `oneShot` result renders + `recordAndRemember` (args branch
  `root.go:170-174`, stdin branch `root.go:186-192`), guard `(flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak) && cfg.TTS.Enabled`
  → call a `speakResult(...)` helper (new `cmd/speak.go`) that builds `tts.New`, runs
  `tts.Select`, and `Speak`s. Speak **after** stdout so audio never interleaves streamed tokens; errors go to stderr, never fatal.
- `define.go`: after `renderDict` (`define.go:62`), `--speak` pronounces the entry itself
  (`res.Dictionary.Word`, lang from detection) regardless of foreign preference.
- New subcommand `translate speak <text...> [--lang] [--backend] [--voice]` registered at
  `root.go:80` — fastest end-to-end smoke path.

## TUI wiring — `keys.go`, `update.go`, `model.go`, `msgs.go`, `view.go`, `cmd/root.go`

- `keys.go`: add `Speak = ctrl+s` (`^s speak`). Comment: Bubble Tea raw mode disables IXON,
  so `ctrl+s` is delivered normally (fallback `ctrl+b`/`ctrl+j` if ever needed).
- `update.go`: new case mirroring Copy (`update.go:217-226`): if `m.focus==focusOutput`
  force `SideResult`, else `SideAuto`; run `tts.Select`, then `m.stopSpeak()` (debounce) and
  return `speakCmd(...)` as a `tea.Cmd` (non-blocking). Flash `speaking…` / `nothing to speak` / `no TTS backend`.
- `msgs.go`: `speakDoneMsg{err}` + `speakCmd` running `Speaker.Speak` in the cmd goroutine; handle it near `flashClearMsg` (`update.go:64`).
- `model.go`: add `speakCancel context.CancelFunc` + `stopSpeak()` (mirror `cancelInflight`,
  `update.go:420`); call it in Quit (`update.go:174-176`) and Clear (`update.go:246`).
- `tui.Params` (`model.go:28-42`): add `Speaker tts.Speaker` and `Foreign string`, built once
  in `runTUI` (`root.go:319-353`) from `cfg.TTS`.
- `view.go`: append `^s speak` to the two hardcoded footer strings (dict `view.go:132`, translate `view.go:160`); height auto-reserves via `footerContent(true)`.

## Caching & cancellation

- Add `xdgpath.CacheDir()` mirroring `DataDir` (`internal/xdgpath/paths.go:34`); tts uses
  `<CacheDir>/tts`, `os.MkdirAll(...,0o700)` lazily (NOT in `EnsureDirs`). `cfg.TTS.CacheDir` overrides.
- Cache key `sha256(tl + "\x00" + text)` → `<hash>.mp3`; hit ⇒ replay, miss ⇒ download →
  atomic temp+rename → play. Unbounded for v1 (LRU cap = follow-up).
- All exec via `exec.CommandContext`, all HTTP via `http.NewRequestWithContext`; CLI ctx is
  SIGINT-cancellable, TUI ctx cancelled by `stopSpeak`/Quit — cancel kills playback mid-word.

## Error / UX handling

No backend/player → `ErrNoBackend`, CLI stderr "speech unavailable (install espeak-ng or a
player like ffplay)", TUI flash `no TTS backend`. Missing voice → retry `say` without `-v`,
then default. Empty text → `Select` returns false / `ErrEmptyText`. `ffplay` must use
`-nodisp -autoexit -loglevel quiet`. Google needs `client=tw-ob` + User-Agent; 429/400 surface as errors.

## Verification

1. `just check` (fmt+vet+build) and `just test` — unit tests:
   - `select_test.go`: foreign=en with zh↔en source/result picks the en side; `Forced` overrides; zh-TW region preserved; empty-side fallback; both-empty ⇒ false.
   - `voices_test.go`: say/espeak/google maps for en, zh, zh-CN, zh-TW, zh-HK, ja; `lang.Base` fallback; config `Voices` override wins.
   - `tts_test.go` (fakeRunner): fallback ordering (native before google, skip unavailable); argv is `["say","-v","Meijia","測試"]`-shaped (no shell string); Windows uses stdin; cancelled ctx propagates. No real process spawned.
   - `config` test: `Default().TTS` seeds + TOML round-trip.
2. Manual smoke (audible):
   - `./translate speak "hello world"` → hears English (`say`/`afplay`).
   - `./translate speak "測試一下" --lang zh-TW` → Meijia voice.
   - `./translate "hola mundo" --to en --speak` → speaks the English (副) side.
   - `./translate define ephemeral --speak` → pronounces the headword.
   - `just tui`: type + Enter, `^s` (input focus → 副/foreign side); `Tab` → result pane, `^s` (speaks result). `--debug` confirms chosen side+lang.
   - Force Google path: `[tts].order = ["google"]` (or offline) and repeat `speak` to exercise MP3 fetch+cache+player.

## Critical files

- `internal/tts/*` — new package (all files above).
- `internal/config/config.go` — `TTS` struct + `Default()` seed.
- `internal/xdgpath/paths.go` — add `CacheDir()`.
- `internal/lang/detect.go` — export `lang.Base`.
- `cmd/root.go`, `cmd/define.go`, new `cmd/speak.go` — flags, speak-after-render, subcommand, TUI Params.
- `internal/tui/keys.go`, `update.go`, `model.go`, `msgs.go`, `view.go` — `^s` key, handler, state, async msg, footer.
```
