```text
1	package lang
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5		"unicode"
6	
7		"github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo"
8	)
9	
10	// Detect returns a best-effort ISO-639-1 source-language code for text, or "" if
11	// the guess is not confident. Used to fill DetectedSource on the LLM path (where
12	// the model returns only the translation) and as a fallback when APIs are down.
13	func Detect(text string) string {
14		info := whatlanggo.Detect(text)
15		if info.Confidence < 0.5 {
16			return ""
17		}
18		return info.Lang.Iso6391()
19	}
20	
21	// IsChinese reports whether text contains any Han characters — used to route
22	// dictionary lookups (Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT).
23	func IsChinese(text string) bool {
24		for _, r := range text {
25			if unicode.Is(unicode.Han, r) {
26				return true
27			}
28		}
29		return false
30	}
31	
32	// containsCJK reports whether text contains any CJK-script rune (Han, Hiragana,
33	// Katakana, or Hangul). This is a reliable, offline signal for routing a
34	// CJK/non-CJK language pair — unlike trigram detection, it is confident even on
35	// very short input.
36	func containsCJK(text string) bool {
37		for _, r := range text {
38			if unicode.Is(unicode.Han, r) || unicode.Is(unicode.Hiragana, r) ||
39				unicode.Is(unicode.Katakana, r) || unicode.Is(unicode.Hangul, r) {
40				return true
41			}
42		}
43		return false
44	}
45	
46	// isCJKLang reports whether a language code is Chinese, Japanese, or Korean.
47	func isCJKLang(code string) bool {
48		switch baseCode(code) {
49		case "zh", "ja", "ko":
50			return true
51		}
52		return false
53	}
54	
55	// PairTarget implements bidirectional "pair" mode: detect which of the two pair
56	// languages the text is written in and return the OTHER one (so "foreign → my
57	// language, my language → foreign" both work from one input box).
58	//
59	// When exactly one side of the pair is a CJK language, routing is decided purely
60	// by script presence — reliable and offline, and symmetric regardless of which
61	// side is "home". This is what makes short Latin input like "test" route to the
62	// CJK side instead of falling through to the wrong language. Same-script pairs
63	// (e.g. en⇄es) fall back to best-effort trigram detection.
64	func PairTarget(home, away, text string) string {
65		if away == "" || away == home {
66			return home
67		}
68		homeCJK, awayCJK := isCJKLang(home), isCJKLang(away)
69		if homeCJK != awayCJK {
70			cjk, latin := home, away
71			if awayCJK {
72				cjk, latin = away, home
73			}
74			if containsCJK(text) {
75				return latin // CJK text → the non-CJK language
76			}
77			return cjk // non-CJK text → the CJK language
78		}
79		// Same-script pair (both or neither CJK): best-effort, symmetric detection.
80		inHome, inAway := inLang(text, home), inLang(text, away)
81		if inHome && !inAway {
82			return away
83		}
84		if inAway && !inHome {
85			return home
86		}
87		return home // inconclusive → default to home
88	}
89	
90	// inLang reports whether text is (best-effort) in the language of code. Chinese
91	// uses a reliable Han-script check; others fall back to offline detection.
92	func inLang(text, code string) bool {
93		base := baseCode(code)
94		switch base {
95		case "", "auto":
96			return false
97		case "zh":
98			return IsChinese(text)
99		default:
100			d := Detect(text)
101			return d != "" && d == base
102		}
103	}
104	
105	// baseCode strips a region suffix: "zh-TW" → "zh".
106	func baseCode(code string) string {
107		code = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(code))
108		if i := strings.IndexByte(code, '-'); i > 0 {
109			return code[:i]
110		}
111		return code
112	}
113
```

 `./internal/xdgpath/paths.go`

```text
1	// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
2	//
3	// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
4	// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
5	// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
6	// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
7	// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
8	package xdgpath
9	
10	import (
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13	)
14	
15	// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
16	const app = "translate"
17	
18	// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
19	func base(env, def string) string {
20		if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
21			return v
22		}
23		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
24		if err != nil {
25			home = "."
26		}
27		return filepath.Join(home, def)
28	}
29	
30	// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
31	func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }
32	
33	// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
34	func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }
35	
36	// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
37	func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }
38	
39	// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
40	func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }
41	
42	// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
43	func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }
44	
45	// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
46	func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }
47	
48	// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
49	func EnsureDirs() error {
50		for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
51			if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
52				return err
53			}
54		}
55		return nil
56	}
57
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
16	// GoogleConfig configures the free, unofficial Google translate_a/single endpoint.
17	type GoogleConfig struct {
18		Endpoint  string
19		ExtraDT   []string // extra dt slots, e.g. "bd" (bilingual dict), "at" (alternates)
20		UserAgent string
21		Timeout   time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// GoogleEngine is a keyless translation engine using Google's web endpoint.
25	// It is unofficial and best-effort: it can rate-limit (HTTP 429) or change shape
26	// without notice, so it lives at the end of the fallback chain.
27	type GoogleEngine struct {
28		cfg  GoogleConfig
29		http *http.Client
30	}
31	
32	// NewGoogle builds a Google engine with sensible defaults.
33	func NewGoogle(cfg GoogleConfig) *GoogleEngine {
34		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
35			cfg.Endpoint = "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single"
36		}
37		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
38			cfg.Timeout = 4 * time.Second
39		}
40		if cfg.UserAgent == "" {
41			cfg.UserAgent = "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli"
42		}
43		return &GoogleEngine{cfg: cfg, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
44	}
45	
46	// Name returns "google".
47	func (e *GoogleEngine) Name() string { return "google" }
48	
49	// Supports reports that Google handles translation only (not dictionary).
50	func (e *GoogleEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
51	
52	func (e *GoogleEngine) buildURL(req Request) string {
53		sl := req.Source
54		if sl == "" {
55			sl = "auto"
56		}
57		q := url.Values{}
58		q.Set("client", "gtx")
59		q.Set("sl", sl)
60		q.Set("tl", req.Target)
61		q.Set("dt", "t")
62		for _, dt := range e.cfg.ExtraDT {
63			q.Add("dt", dt)
64		}
65		q.Set("q", req.Text)
66		return e.cfg.Endpoint + "?" + q.Encode()
67	}
68	
69	// Translate performs one keyless translation (non-streaming).
70	func (e *GoogleEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
71		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
72			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
73		}
74		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.buildURL(req), nil)
75		if err != nil {
76			return nil, err
77		}
78		httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
79	
80		resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
81		if err != nil {
82			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: %w", err)), nil
83		}
84		defer resp.Body.Close()
85		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
86			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
87			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))), nil
88		}
89	
90		var data []json.RawMessage
91		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&data); err != nil {
92			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: decode: %w", err)), nil
93		}
94		res, err := parseGoogle(data, req)
95		if err != nil {
96			return single(nil, err), nil
97		}
98		res.Engine = e.Name()
99		return single(res, nil), nil
100	}
101	
102	// parseGoogle defensively walks the nested-array response. Positions: data[0] is
103	// an array of [translated, original, ...] segments (long text splits into many);
104	// data[2] is the detected source language.
105	func parseGoogle(data []json.RawMessage, req Request) (*TranslateResult, error) {
106		if len(data) < 1 {
107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: empty payload")
108		}
109		var segs []json.RawMessage
110		if err := json.Unmarshal(data[0], &segs); err != nil {
111			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: bad segments: %w", err)
112		}
113		var b strings.Builder
114		for _, s := range segs {
115			var parts []json.RawMessage
116			if json.Unmarshal(s, &parts) != nil || len(parts) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			var txt string
120			if json.Unmarshal(parts[0], &txt) == nil {
121				b.WriteString(txt)
122			}
123		}
124		res := &TranslateResult{Translation: strings.TrimSpace(b.String()), Target: req.Target}
125		if res.Translation == "" {
126			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: no translation in response")
127		}
128		if len(data) > 2 {
129			var det string
130			if json.Unmarshal(data[2], &det) == nil {
131				res.DetectedSource = det
132			}
133		}
134		return res, nil
135	}
136	
137	// Detect uses the offline detector (cheap, no network round-trip).
138	func (e *GoogleEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
139		return lang.Detect(text), nil
140	}
141	
142	// Available probes the endpoint with a tiny translation request.
143	func (e *GoogleEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
144		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
145		defer cancel()
146		u := e.buildURL(Request{Text: "a", Source: "auto", Target: "en"})
147		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
148		if err != nil {
149			return false
150		}
151		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
152		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
153		if err != nil {
154			return false
155		}
156		defer resp.Body.Close()
157		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
158		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
159	}
160
```

```text
5	// Sentinel errors returned across the engine layer.
6	var (
7		// ErrEmptyInput is returned when a request has no text to translate.
8		ErrEmptyInput = errors.New("engine: empty input")
9		// ErrNoResult is returned when a channel closed without a terminal result.
10		ErrNoResult = errors.New("engine: stream closed without a result")
11		// ErrNoEngineForMode is returned by the chain when no engine supports the mode.
12		ErrNoEngineForMode = errors.New("engine: no engine supports this mode")
13		// ErrAllEnginesFailed is returned by the chain when every candidate failed.
```

```text
1	// Package config defines translate's on-disk configuration (config.toml) and the
2	// resolution of effective settings (flags > config > env > defaults).
3	package config
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10	
11		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
14	)
15	
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
22		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
23		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
24		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
25		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
26		History   History    `toml:"history"`
27	}
28	
29	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
30	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
31	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
32	type Overlay struct {
33		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
34		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
35		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
36		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
37		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
38		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
39		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
40		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
41		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
42		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
43		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
44		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
45		Debug         *bool   `toml:"debug,omitempty"`
46	}
47	
48	// General holds behavior settings.
49	type General struct {
50		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
51		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
52		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
53		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
54		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`
55		LiveTranslate     bool   `toml:"live_translate"`
56		DebounceMs        int    `toml:"debounce_ms"`
57		Engine            string `toml:"engine"`                 // auto | smartauto | llm | google | <provider>
58		Tier              string `toml:"tier"`                   // default | fast | max
59		Preset            string `toml:"preset"`                 // concise | contextual | dictionary
60		Instructions      string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"` // extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)
61		AlternativesCount int    `toml:"alternatives_count"`
62		Stream            bool   `toml:"stream"`
63		Color             string `toml:"color"` // auto | always | never
64		Debug             bool   `toml:"debug"` // verbose decision logging (also --debug / TRANSLATE_DEBUG)
65	}
66	
67	// Chain is the ordered fallback list of provider/engine names.
68	type Chain struct {
69		Order []string `toml:"order"`
70	}
71	
72	// Provider is one OpenAI-compatible LLM backend.
73	type Provider struct {
74		Name      string `toml:"name"`
75		Type      string `toml:"type"` // openai | ollama | openrouter | litellm | generic
76		BaseURL   string `toml:"base_url"`
77		Model     string `toml:"model"`
78		ModelFast string `toml:"model_fast,omitempty"`
79		ModelMax  string `toml:"model_max,omitempty"`
80		APIKeyEnv string `toml:"api_key_env,omitempty"` // "" => no Authorization header
81	}
82	
83	// Google configures the free translate_a/single endpoint.
84	type Google struct {
85		Enabled   bool     `toml:"enabled"`
86		Endpoint  string   `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
87		ExtraDT   []string `toml:"extra_dt,omitempty"`
88		UserAgent string   `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
89		TimeoutMs int      `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
90	}
91	
92	// Dict configures the dictionary lookup engine.
93	type Dict struct {
94		Enabled  bool   `toml:"enabled"`
95		Source   string `toml:"source"` // local (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT) | api (dictionaryapi.dev)
96		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
97		Lang     string `toml:"lang,omitempty"`
98		Fuzzy    bool   `toml:"fuzzy"`
99		Wordlist string `toml:"wordlist,omitempty"` // "" => /usr/share/dict/words if present
100	
101		// Local bilingual dictionary (source=local).
102		Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => <XDG data>/dict
103		CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
104		EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`
105		AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"` // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first zh lookup
106		APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`  // fall back to dictionaryapi.dev on an English miss
107	}
108	
109	// History configures translation history storage.
110	type History struct {
111		Enabled bool   `toml:"enabled"`
112		Backend string `toml:"backend"` // jsonl | sqlite
113		Path    string `toml:"path,omitempty"`
114	}
115	
116	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back
117	// to the LLM when it misses or the fuzzy match is too far off (see engine.SmartDict).
118	type SmartDict struct {
119		// CloseDistance is the English edit-distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
120		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
121		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.
122		CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`
123		Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"` // LLM prompt style for the fallback
124		DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`   // `translate define` uses smart-dict when a provider is available
125	}
126	
127	// Recommended copilot-proxy model ids per tier. Claude models are served via the
128	// Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages); the LLM engine routes them there
129	// automatically (they 400 on /chat/completions).
130	const (
131		ModelDefault = "claude-sonnet-5"  // balanced
132		ModelFast    = "claude-haiku-4-5" // snappy — the out-of-box default tier
133		ModelMax     = "claude-opus-4-8"  // highest quality
134	)

181				ExtraDT:   []string{"bd", "at"},

193				AutoDownload: false, // explicit `dict update` is the blessed path
194				APIFallback:  true,  // English lookups still work before ECDICT is installed
195			},
196			SmartDict: SmartDict{

198				Preset:        "dictionary", // gloss + example sentences
199				DefineDefault: true,         // `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up

207	
208	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
209	func Path() string {
210		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
211			return p
212		}
213		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
214	}
215	
216	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
217	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
218	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
219		p := Path()
220		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
221		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
222			cfg = Default()
223			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
224				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
225			}
226			return cfg, true, nil
227		}
228		if err != nil {
229			return nil, false, err
230		}
231		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
232		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
233			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
234		}
235		return cfg, false, nil
236	}
237	
238	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
239	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
240		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
241			return err
242		}
243		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
244		if err != nil {
245			return err
246		}
247		p := Path()
248		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
249		if err != nil {
250			return err
251		}
252		tmpName := tmp.Name()
253		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
254		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
255			tmp.Close()
256			return err
257		}
258		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
259			return err
260		}
261		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
262	}
263	
264	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
265	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
266		for i := range c.Providers {
267			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
268				return &c.Providers[i]
269			}
270		}
271		return nil
272	}
273	
274	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
275	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
276	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
277		switch tier {
278		case "fast":
279			if p.ModelFast != "" {
280				return p.ModelFast
281			}
282		case "max":
283			if p.ModelMax != "" {
284				return p.ModelMax
285			}
286		}
287		return p.Model
288	}
```

```text
1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strings"
14	
15		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
16		"golang.org/x/term"
17	
18		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
19	
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
26		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
27		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
28	)
29	
30	var (
31		flagTo           string
32		flagFrom         string
33		flagModel        string
34		flagProvider     string
35		flagEngine       string
36		flagTier         string
37		flagPreset       string
38		flagInstructions string
39		flagPair         bool
40		flagPairWith     string
41		flagJSON         bool
42		flagNoHistory    bool
43		flagDebug        bool
44	)
45	
46	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
47	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
48		root := &cobra.Command{
49			Use:   "translate [text...]",
50			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
51			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
52				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
53				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
54				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
55			Version:       buildVersion(),
56			SilenceUsage:  true,
57			SilenceErrors: true,
58			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
59			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
60			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
61			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
62			RunE: runRoot,
63		}
64		f := root.PersistentFlags()
65		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
66		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
67		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
68		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
69		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
70		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
71		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
72		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
73		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
74		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
75		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
76		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
77		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
78	
79		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
80		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd())
81		return root
82	}
83	
84	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
85	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
86		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
87	}
88	
89	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
90	func overrides() config.Overrides {
91		return config.Overrides{
92			Source:       flagFrom,
93			Target:       flagTo,
94			Engine:       flagEngine,
95			Provider:     flagProvider,
96			Model:        flagModel,
97			Tier:         flagTier,
98			Preset:       flagPreset,
99			Instructions: flagInstructions,
100			Pair:         flagPair,
101			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
102			Debug:        flagDebug,
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
107	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
108	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
109	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
110		if len(args) > 0 {
111			return config.ModeCLI
112		}
113		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
114			return config.ModeCLI
115		}
116		return config.ModeTUI
117	}
118	
119	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
120		ctx := cmd.Context()
121	
122		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
123		if err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126		if created {
127			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
128		}
129	
130		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
131		if res.Debug {
132			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
133			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
134			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
135				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
136			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
137				debug.Enable(w)
138			}
139		}
140		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
141		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
142		pairWith := ""
143		if res.Pair {
144			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
145			pairWith = pwm.Code
146		}
147		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s",
148			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith)
149		if res.Pair && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
150			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
151		}
152	
153		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
154			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
155		}
156		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
157		if err != nil {
158			return err
159		}
160	
161		st := openStore(cfg)
162		if st != nil {
163			defer st.Close()
164		}
165	
166		switch {
167		case len(args) > 0:
168			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
169			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
170			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
171			if err != nil {
172				return err
173			}
174			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
175			return nil
176	
177		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
178			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
179			if err != nil {
180				return err
181			}
182			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
183			if text == "" {
184				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
185			}
186			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
187			r, err := oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith)
188			if err != nil {
189				return err
190			}
191			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
192			return nil
193	
194		default:
195			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
196		}
197	}
198	
199	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
200	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
201		if pair && away != "" {
202			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
203			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
204			return t
205		}
206		return home
207	}
208	
209	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
210	func clip(s string, n int) string {
211		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
212		r := []rune(s)
213		if len(r) > n {
214			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
215		}
216		return s
217	}
218	
219	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
220	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
221	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
222		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
223			return nil
224		}
225		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
226			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
227		if err != nil {
228			return nil
229		}
230		return f
231	}
232	
233	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
234	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
235	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
236		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
237			return
238		}
239		stt, err := state.Load()
240		if err != nil || stt == nil {
241			return
242		}
243		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
244			res.Source = stt.Source
245			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
246		}
247		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
248			res.Target = stt.Target
249			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
250		}
251	}
252	
253	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
254	// suppressed with --no-history.
255	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
256		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
257			return nil
258		}
259		path := cfg.History.Path
260		if path == "" {
261			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
262		}
263		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
264		if err != nil {
265			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
266			return nil
267		}
268		return st
269	}
270	
271	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
272	//
273	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
274	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
275	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
276	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
277		if st != nil {
278			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
279		}
280		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
281			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
282		}
283	}
284	
285	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
286	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
287		src := source
288		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
289			src = res.DetectedSource
290		}
291		return store.Record{
292			SourceLang:   src,
293			TargetLang:   target,
294			Engine:       res.Engine,
295			Model:        res.Model,
296			Input:        input,
297			Output:       res.Translation,
298			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
299			Notes:        res.Notes,
300		}
301	}
302	
303	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
304	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
305		mode := "fixed"
306		if source == "auto" {
307			mode = "auto"
308		}
309		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
310			Source:     source,
311			Target:     target,
312			SourceMode: mode,
313			Engine:     engineName,
314		})
315	}
316	
317	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
318	// pair on exit.
319	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
320		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
321		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
322		modelProvider := ""
323		if res.Provider != nil {
324			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
325			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
326		}
327		p := tui.Params{
328			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
329			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
330			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
331			Store:         st,
332			Source:        source,
333			Target:        target,
334			Pair:          res.Pair,
335			PairWith:      pairWith,
336			Model:         res.Model,
337			Preset:        res.Preset,
338			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
339			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
340			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
341		}
342		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
343		if err != nil {
344			return err
345		}
346		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
347			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
348				s, t := fm.Pair()
349				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
350			}
351		}
352		return nil
353	}
354	
355	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
356	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
357	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
358		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
359		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
360		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
361			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
362		}
363		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
364			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
365		}
366		return sm.Code, tm.Code
367	}
368	
369	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
370	//
371	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
372	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
373	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
374	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
375		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
376		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON
377	
378		req := engine.Request{
379			Text:     text,
380			Source:   source,
381			Target:   target,
382			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
383			Stream:   stream,
384			Preset:   preset,
385			Extra:    instructions,
386			Pair:     pair,
387			PairHome: pairHome,
388			PairAway: pairAway,
389		}
390		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
391		if err != nil {
392			return nil, err
393		}
394	
395		var onTok func(string)
396		if stream {
397			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
398		}
399		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
400		if err != nil {
401			if stream {
402				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
403			}
404			return nil, err
405		}
406	
407		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
408		// failed and why, and how to switch.
409		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
410			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
411		}
412		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
413			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
414		}
415	
416		switch {
417		case flagJSON:
418			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
419			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
420			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
421				return nil, err
422			}
423		case stream:
424			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
425		default:
426			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
427		}
428		return res, nil
429	}
430
```

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		return nil
64	}
65	
66	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
67	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
68	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
69	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
70		cfg := res.Cfg
71		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
72			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
73		}
74		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
75			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
76		}
77		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
78	}
79	
80	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
81	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
82		d := res.Dictionary
83		if d == nil {
84			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
85				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
86			}
87			return res.Translation + "\n"
88		}
89		var b strings.Builder
90		head := d.Word
91		if d.Phonetic != "" {
92			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
93		}
94		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
95		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
96			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
97			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
98				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
99					break
100				}
101				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
102				if def.Example != "" {
103					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
104				}
105			}
106		}
107		return b.String()
108	}
109
```

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
21	}
22	
23	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
24	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
25	type Mode int
26	
27	const (
28		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
29		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
30	)
31	
32	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
33	type Resolved struct {
34		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
35		Target       string
36		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
37		Provider     *Provider
38		Model        string
39		Tier         string
40		Preset       string
41		Instructions string
42		Pair         bool
43		PairWith     string
44		Stream       bool
45		Color        string
46		Debug        bool
47	
48		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
49		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
50		LiveTranslate bool
51		DebounceMs    int
52	
53		Cfg *Config
54	}
55	
56	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
57	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
58	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
59	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
60	
61	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
62	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
63		switch mode {
64		case ModeCLI:
65			return c.CLI
66		case ModeTUI:
67			return c.TUI
68		}
69		return nil
70	}
71	
72	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
73	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
74	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
75		if ov == nil {
76			return
77		}
78		if ov.Engine != nil {
79			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
80		}
81		if ov.Tier != nil {
82			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
83		}
84		if ov.Preset != nil {
85			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
86		}
87		if ov.Instructions != nil {
88			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
89		}
90		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
91			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
92		}
93		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
94			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
95		}
96		if ov.Pair != nil {
97			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
98		}
99		if ov.PairWith != nil {
100			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
101		}
102		if ov.Stream != nil {
103			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
104		}
105		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
106			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
107		}
108		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
109			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
110		}
111		if ov.Debug != nil {
112			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
113		}
114	}
115	
116	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
117	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
118	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
119		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
120		// the on-disk config is untouched).
121		g := c.General
122		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
123		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
124	
125		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
126			if flag != "" {
127				return flag
128			}
129			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
130				return v
131			}
132			return cfgVal
133		}
134	
135		r := Resolved{
136			Cfg:           c,
137			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
138			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
139			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
140			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
141			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
142			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
143			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
144			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
145			Color:         g.Color,
146			Stream:        g.Stream,
147			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
148			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
149			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
150		}
151	
152		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
153		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
154		if provName == "" {
155			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
156				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
157			} else {
158				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
159			}
160		}
161		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
162	
163		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
164		// provider's tier model.
165		cfgModel := ""
166		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
167			cfgModel = *ov.Model
168		}
169		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
170		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
171			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
172		}
173		return r
174	}
175	
176	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
177	// else the first configured provider, else "".
178	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
179		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
180			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
181				return name
182			}
183		}
184		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
185			return c.Providers[0].Name
186		}
187		return ""
188	}
189
```

 `./internal/tui/msgs.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9	)
10	
11	// debounceMsg fires debounce after the last keystroke. It carries the seq it was
12	// armed with; a newer keystroke bumps m.seq so the stale tick is ignored.
13	type debounceMsg struct{ seq int }
14	
15	// streamMsg carries one engine Chunk (token, done, or error), stamped with the
16	// request's seq so cancelled/superseded events are dropped in O(1).
17	type streamMsg struct {
18		seq   int
19		chunk engine.Chunk
20	}
21	
22	// flashClearMsg clears a transient footer notice.
23	type flashClearMsg struct{}
24	
25	// flashCmd shows a transient footer notice for ~1.5s.
26	func flashCmd() tea.Cmd {
27		return tea.Tick(1500*time.Millisecond, func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return flashClearMsg{} })
28	}
29	
30	// armDebounce schedules a debounceMsg after d. tea.Tick fires exactly once, so N
31	// keystrokes arm N ticks; the seq guard collapses them to the last.
32	func armDebounce(seq int, d time.Duration) tea.Cmd {
33		return tea.Tick(d, func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return debounceMsg{seq: seq} })
34	}
35	
36	// waitStream reads the next Chunk from ch and wraps it as a seq-stamped msg. The
37	// engine contract guarantees a terminal ChunkDone/ChunkError before close, so a
38	// closed channel without one is surfaced as an error.
39	func waitStream(ch <-chan engine.Chunk, seq int) tea.Cmd {
40		return func() tea.Msg {
41			c, ok := <-ch
42			if !ok {
43				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: engine.ErrNoResult}}
44			}
45			return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: c}
46		}
47	}
48
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
8		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"

16	)
17	
18	// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
19	// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
20	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
21		switch msg := msg.(type) {
22		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
23			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
24			m.relayout()
25			m.ready = true
26			return m, nil
27	
28		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
29			return m.handleKey(msg)
30	
31		case debounceMsg:
32			if msg.seq != m.seq {
33				return m, nil // a newer keystroke arrived; this tick is stale
34			}
35			return m.launch(false)
36	
37		case historyLoadedMsg:
38			m.hist.SetItems(toListItems(msg.items))
39			return m, nil
40	
41		case modelsLoadedMsg:
42			m.modelsLoading = false
43			m.modelsErr = msg.err
44			m.cachedModels = msg.models
45			m.modelList.SetItems(modelItems(msg.models, m.currentModelID()))
46			return m, nil
47	
48		case tea.MouseWheelMsg:
49			return m.handleMouseWheel(msg)
50	
51		case tea.MouseClickMsg:
52			return m.handleMouseClick(msg)
53	
54		case spinner.TickMsg:
55			var cmd tea.Cmd
56			m.sp, cmd = m.sp.Update(msg)
57			// Animate the placeholder inside the (fixed-height) viewport so the
58			// layout never jumps while waiting for the first token.
59			if m.status == statusTranslating && m.streamBuf == "" {
60				m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder())
61			}
62			return m, cmd
63	
64		case flashClearMsg:
65			m.flash = ""
66			return m, nil
67	
68		case streamMsg:
69			return m.handleStream(msg)
70		}
71	
72		var cmd tea.Cmd
73		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
74		return m, cmd
75	}
76	
77	// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
78	// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
79	// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
80	func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
81		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
82			return m, nil
83		}
84		switch msg.chunk.Kind {
85		case engine.ChunkToken:
86			m.retrying = false // real tokens are flowing; drop the "retrying" label
87			m.streamBuf += msg.chunk.Text
88			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf))
89			m.vp.GotoBottom()
90			return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for the next token
91	
92		case engine.ChunkDone:
93			m.inflight = 0
94			m.status = statusDone
95			m.err = nil
96			m.lastDoneKey = m.pendingKey // remember what was translated (skip re-runs)
97			if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
98				r := *msg.chunk.Result
99				// A truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is re-fired once
100				// automatically — a fresh request almost always completes. Only after a
101				// second truncation do we settle on the partial text + ⚠.
102				if r.Truncated && m.autoRetryKey != m.pendingKey {
103					m.autoRetryKey = m.pendingKey
104					nm, cmd := m.launch(true) // force a fresh fetch of the same input
105					mm := nm.(Model)
106					mm.retrying = true
107					mm.vp.SetContent(mm.translatingPlaceholder())
108					return mm, cmd
109				}
110				m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{} // settled (complete, or gave up after the retry)
111				m.retrying = false
112				m.result = msg.chunk.Result
113				// A truncated result keeps its partial text on screen (with a ⚠), but
114				// must not be cached or persisted as if it were the real answer — so a
115				// manual retry (Enter) actually re-fetches instead of replaying it.
116				if !r.Truncated {
117					m.cache[m.pendingKey] = msg.chunk.Result // write-through (force overwrites)
118				}
119				if r.Engine != "" {
120					m.curEngine = r.Engine
121				}
122				if r.Model != "" {
123					m.curModel = r.Model
124				}
125				m.relayout() // engine/model segment width may have changed
126				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(r))
127				m.vp.GotoTop() // show a completed result from its start, not scrolled
128				// Don't persist a truncated result, or a suggestions-only result
129				// (empty translation, no entry).
130				if !r.Truncated && (r.Dictionary != nil || r.Translation != "") {
131					return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.p.Store, m.recordFor(r))
132				}
133				return m, nil
134			}
135			return m, nil
136	
137		case engine.ChunkError:
138			m.inflight = 0
139			m.retrying = false
140			m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{}
141			m.status = statusError
142			m.err = msg.chunk.Err
143			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))
144			return m, nil
145		}
146		return m, nil
147	}
148	
149	// isTextInput reports whether a key press produced a printable, non-whitespace
150	// character (Key.Text is set only for printable characters). Space/tab are
151	// treated as navigation so they can page the viewport instead of typing.
152	func isTextInput(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) bool {
153		return msg.Text != "" && strings.TrimSpace(msg.Text) != ""
154	}
155	
156	func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
157		// An open overlay (history / language / model picker) captures keys.
158		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
159			switch msg.String() {
160			case "ctrl+c":
161				m.cancelInflight()
162				return m, tea.Quit
163			case "esc":
164				m.overlay = overlayNone
165				return m, nil
166			case "enter":
167				return m.overlaySelect()
168			}
169			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
170			return m, cmd
171		}
172	
173		switch {
174		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
175			m.cancelInflight()
176			return m, tea.Quit
177	
178		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.History):
179			m.overlay = overlayHistory
180			return m, loadHistoryCmd(m.p.Store)
181	
182		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickLang):
183			m.overlay = overlayLang
184			return m, nil
185	
186		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickModel):
187			if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
188				return m, nil // no model concept in dictionary mode
189			}
190			return m.openModelPicker()
191	
192		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickPreset):
193			if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
194				return m, nil // presets are LLM translate styles
195			}
196			m.presetList.SetItems(presetItems(m.preset))
197			m.overlay = overlayPreset
198			return m, nil
199	
200		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.TogglePair):
201			m.pair = !m.pair
202			// Pair mode is a no-op when "away" equals the target; pick a distinct away.
203			if m.pair && (m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target)) {
204				if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
205					m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
206				} else {
207					m.pairWith = "en"
208				}
209			}
210			m.relayout() // footer pair segment changed
211			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
212				return m.launch(false)
213			}
214			m.clearResult()
215			return m, nil
216	
217		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Copy):
218			if txt := m.copyText(); txt != "" {
219				if err := clipboard.WriteAll(txt); err != nil {
220					m.flash = "copy failed"
221				} else {
222					m.flash = "copied ✓"
223				}
224				return m, flashCmd()
225			}
226			return m, nil
227	
228		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
229			m.live = !m.live
230			return m, nil
231	
232		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.CycleEngine):
233			if n := len(m.p.Engines); n > 1 {
234				m.engIdx = (m.engIdx + 1) % n
235				m.curEngine, m.curModel = "", ""
236				m.relayout() // footer content (engine/mode) changed
237				// Only re-translate automatically in live mode; otherwise switch the
238				// engine, clear the now-stale result, and wait for Enter.
239				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
240					return m.launch(true)
241				}
242				m.clearResult()
243			}
244			return m, nil
245	
246		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
247			m.cancelInflight()
248			m.seq++
249			m.ta.Reset()
250			m.focus = focusInput
251			m.ta.Focus()
252			m.result = nil
253			m.err = nil
254			m.streamBuf = ""
255			m.status = statusIdle
256			m.vp.SetContent("")
257			return m, nil
258	
259		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.SwitchFocus):
260			// Toggle which pane the keyboard drives. Blurring the textarea also dims its
261			// border (View keys off ta.Focused()); the result box highlights via m.focus.
262			if m.focus == focusInput {
263				m.focus = focusOutput
264				m.ta.Blur()
265				return m, nil
266			}
267			m.focus = focusInput
268			return m, m.ta.Focus()
269	
270		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
271			// After a dictionary miss the input still equals the missed word, so a
272			// second Enter means "help me choose" — open the ranked suggestions.
273			if m.result != nil && len(m.result.Suggestions) > 0 &&
274				strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == m.lastInput {
275				m.suggestList.SetItems(suggestItems(m.result.Suggestions))
276				m.overlay = overlaySuggest
277				return m, nil
278			}
279			return m.launch(true)
280		}
281	
282		// Output pane focused: the keyboard scrolls the result viewport. A printable
283		// character (not whitespace) snaps focus back to the input and is typed there,
284		// so the user can just start typing without pressing Tab first.
285		if m.focus == focusOutput {
286			if isTextInput(msg) {
287				m.focus = focusInput
288				cmd := m.ta.Focus()
289				var tcmd tea.Cmd
290				m.ta, tcmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
291				return m, tea.Batch(cmd, tcmd)
292			}
293			var cmd tea.Cmd
294			m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
295			return m, cmd
296		}
297	
298		// Ordinary keystroke: update the textarea, then (re)arm the debounce.
299		var cmd tea.Cmd
300		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
301	
302		if strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == "" {
303			// Input cleared: cancel in-flight work and clear the result pane.
304			m.cancelInflight()
305			m.seq++
306			m.result = nil
307			m.streamBuf = ""
308			m.status = statusIdle
309			m.vp.SetContent("")
310			return m, cmd
311		}
312	
313		if m.live {
314			m.seq++            // invalidate any pending debounce tick
315			m.cancelInflight() // stop any in-flight request; its results become stale
316			m.status = statusTyping
317			return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq, m.debounce))
318		}
319		return m, cmd
320	}
321	
322	// translatingPlaceholder renders the fixed-height "translating…" placeholder,
323	// upgraded to a "retrying" label while an auto-retry after a truncation is
324	// in flight so the user sees why a second request fired.
325	func (m Model) translatingPlaceholder() string {
326		label := "translating…"
327		if m.retrying {
328			label = "translating… · retrying (stream truncated)"
329		}
330		return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render(label)
331	}
332	
333	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
334	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
335	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
336	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
337	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
338		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
339		if text == "" {
340			m.status = statusIdle
341			return m, nil
342		}
343		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
344		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
345			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
346		}
347		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
348		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
349		if !force {
350			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
351				m.cancelInflight()
352				m.status = statusDone
353				m.err = nil
354				m.result = cached
355				if cached.Engine != "" {
356					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
357				}
358				if cached.Model != "" {
359					m.curModel = cached.Model
360				}
361				m.streamBuf = ""
362				m.lastInput = text
363				m.lastDoneKey = key
364				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
365				return m, nil
366			}
367		}
368		m.seq++
369		if m.cancel != nil {
370			m.cancel()
371		}
372		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
373		m.cancel = cancel
374		m.inflight = m.seq
375		m.status = statusTranslating
376		m.streamBuf = ""
377		m.retrying = false // a fresh launch clears any stale "retrying" label
378		m.err = nil
379		m.result = nil
380		m.lastInput = text
381		m.pendingKey = key
382		m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder()) // fixed-height placeholder
383	
384		seq := m.seq
385		ne := m.active()
386		target := m.target
387		pairOn := m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate
388		if pairOn {
389			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
390			debug.Logf("tui pair route: home=%s away=%s → target=%s", m.target, m.pairWith, target)
391		}
392		debug.Logf("tui launch: engine=%s mode=%d target=%s preset=%s", ne.Name, ne.Mode, target, m.preset)
393		req := engine.Request{
394			Text:          text,
395			Source:        m.source,
396			Target:        target,
397			Mode:          ne.Mode,
398			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
399			Model:         m.modelOverride,
400			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
401			Preset:        m.preset,
402			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
403			Pair:          pairOn,
404			PairHome:      m.target,
405			PairAway:      m.pairWith,
406		}
407		ch, err := ne.Engine.Translate(ctx, req)
408		if err != nil {
409			e := err
410			return m, func() tea.Msg {
411				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
412			}
413		}
414		m.stream = ch
415		return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
416	}
417	
418	// cancelInflight cancels the current request (if any) and marks it as no longer
419	// in flight so late chunks are dropped.
420	func (m *Model) cancelInflight() {
421		if m.cancel != nil {
422			m.cancel()
423			m.cancel = nil
424		}
425		m.inflight = 0
426	}
427	
428	// clearResult drops the shown result. Used when a setting (engine/lang/model/
429	// style/pair) changes without an immediate re-translate, so the pane never shows
430	// output that's stale relative to the (now different) footer.
431	func (m *Model) clearResult() {
432		m.cancelInflight()
433		m.result = nil
434		m.streamBuf = ""
435		m.err = nil
436		m.status = statusIdle
437		m.vp.SetContent("")
438	}
439	
440	// copyText returns the text to place on the clipboard for the current result.
441	func (m Model) copyText() string {
442		if m.result == nil {
443			return ""
444		}
445		return strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
446	}
447	
448	// currentModelID is the model id currently in effect (for the ✓ marker).
449	func (m Model) currentModelID() string {
450		if m.modelOverride != "" {
451			return m.modelOverride
452		}
453		return m.curModel
454	}
455	
456	// openModelPicker opens the model overlay, fetching the model list once per session.
457	func (m Model) openModelPicker() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
458		m.overlay = overlayModel
459		if m.cachedModels == nil && !m.modelsLoading && m.modelsErr == nil {
460			m.modelsLoading = true
461			return m, fetchModelsCmd(m.p.ModelSource)
462		}
463		return m, nil
464	}
465	
466	// overlayListUpdate forwards a message to the active overlay's list.
467	func (m *Model) overlayListUpdate(msg tea.Msg) tea.Cmd {
468		var cmd tea.Cmd
469		switch m.overlay {
470		case overlayHistory:
471			m.hist, cmd = m.hist.Update(msg)
472		case overlayLang:
473			m.langList, cmd = m.langList.Update(msg)
474		case overlayModel:
475			m.modelList, cmd = m.modelList.Update(msg)
476		case overlaySuggest:
477			m.suggestList, cmd = m.suggestList.Update(msg)
478		case overlayPreset:
479			m.presetList, cmd = m.presetList.Update(msg)
480		}
481		return cmd
482	}
483	
484	// overlaySelect applies the highlighted overlay item and closes the overlay.
485	func (m Model) overlaySelect() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
486		switch m.overlay {
487		case overlayHistory:
488			// Recall is an explicit "show me this" action: always translate.
489			if it, ok := m.hist.SelectedItem().(histItem); ok {
490				m.ta.SetValue(it.rec.Input)
491				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
492				m.overlay = overlayNone
493				return m.launch(true)
494			}
495		case overlayLang:
496			if it, ok := m.langList.SelectedItem().(langItem); ok {
497				m.target = it.code
498				m.overlay = overlayNone
499				m.relayout() // pair segment width changed
500				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
501					return m.launch(true)
502				}
503				m.clearResult()
504				return m, nil
505			}
506		case overlayModel:
507			if it, ok := m.modelList.SelectedItem().(modelItem); ok {
508				m.modelOverride = it.id
509				m.overlay = overlayNone
510				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
511					return m.launch(true)
512				}
513				m.clearResult()
514				return m, nil
515			}
516		case overlaySuggest:
517			// Picking a suggestion updates the input and looks it up.
518			if it, ok := m.suggestList.SelectedItem().(suggestItem); ok {
519				m.ta.SetValue(it.word)
520				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
521				m.overlay = overlayNone
522				return m.launch(true)
523			}
524		case overlayPreset:
525			if it, ok := m.presetList.SelectedItem().(presetItem); ok {
526				m.preset = it.id
527				m.overlay = overlayNone
528				m.relayout() // style segment width changed
529				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
530					return m.launch(false)
531				}
532				m.clearResult()
533				return m, nil
534			}
535		}
536		m.overlay = overlayNone
537		return m, nil
538	}
539	
540	// handleMouseWheel scrolls the active overlay list, or the result viewport.
541	func (m Model) handleMouseWheel(msg tea.MouseWheelMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
542		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
543			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
544			return m, cmd
545		}
546		var cmd tea.Cmd
547		m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
548		return m, cmd
549	}
550	
551	// handleMouseClick routes clicks. In an overlay it forwards to the list; in
552	// normal mode a click focuses the pane it lands in (so the keyboard scrolls it),
553	// except a click on the footer row opens the language picker.
554	func (m Model) handleMouseClick(msg tea.MouseClickMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
555		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
556			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
557			return m, cmd
558		}
559		if m.height > 0 && msg.Y == m.height-1 {
560			m.overlay = overlayLang
561			return m, nil
562		}
563		// The input box occupies the first inputH+2 rows (border + inputH + border);
564		// everything below it (down to the footer) is the result box.
565		if msg.Y < inputH+2 {
566			if m.focus != focusInput {
567				m.focus = focusInput
568				return m, m.ta.Focus()
569			}
570			return m, nil
571		}
572		if m.focus != focusOutput {
573			m.focus = focusOutput
574			m.ta.Blur()
575		}
576		return m, nil
577	}
578	
579	// recordFor builds a history record for a completed result.
580	func (m Model) recordFor(res engine.TranslateResult) store.Record {
581		src := m.source
582		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
583			src = res.DetectedSource
584		}
585		target := m.target
586		if res.Target != "" {
587			target = res.Target // effective target (may differ in pair mode)
588		}
589		return store.Record{
590			SourceLang:   src,
591			TargetLang:   target,
592			Engine:       res.Engine,
593			Model:        res.Model,
594			Input:        m.lastInput,
595			Output:       res.Translation,
596			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
597			Notes:        res.Notes,
598		}
599	}
600	
601	// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. The input/result
602	// boxes are rendered at .Width(m.width-2); lipgloss treats that as the TOTAL
603	// width, so the inner content area is that minus the box frame (border 2 +
604	// padding 2 = 4) = m.width-6. Wrapping content to anything wider makes each line
605	// overflow the box and get re-wrapped, orphaning a character per line.
606	func (m *Model) relayout() {
607		if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
608			return
609		}
610		footerH := m.footerHeight() // wraps to width; may be >1 on narrow terminals
611		compW := m.width - 6
612		if compW < 1 {
613			compW = 1
614		}
615		resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
616		if resultH < 3 {
617			resultH = 3
618		}
619		m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
620		m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
621		m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
622		m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
623		m.resultH = resultH
624		overlayH := m.height - 2 // overlays use a single-line footer
625		if overlayH < 1 {
626			overlayH = 1
627		}
628		m.hist.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
629		m.langList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
630		m.modelList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
631		m.suggestList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
632		m.presetList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
633	}
634
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
8		lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
12	)
13	
14	// View renders the two-box layout (input, result) plus a status/help footer.
15	// AltScreen + mouse are set declaratively (the v2 way).
16	func (m Model) View() tea.View {
17		if !m.ready {
18			return altView("initializing…")
19		}
20		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
21			return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, m.overlayBody(), m.overlayFooter()))
22		}
23	
24		inStyle := m.st.input
25		if m.ta.Focused() {
26			inStyle = m.st.inputHi
27		}
28		input := inStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Render(m.ta.View())
29	
30		// The result box is kept at a fixed height (viewport height) so the layout
31		// never jumps between the "translating…" placeholder and streamed output.
32		resStyle := m.st.result
33		if m.focus == focusOutput {
34			resStyle = m.st.resultHi
35		}
36		result := resStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Height(m.resultH).Render(m.vp.View())
37		footer := m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.statusLine())
38	
39		return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, input, result, footer))
40	}
41	
42	// overlayBody renders the active picker's list (or its loading/empty state).
43	func (m Model) overlayBody() string {
44		switch m.overlay {
45		case overlayHistory:
46			return m.hist.View()
47		case overlayLang:
48			return m.langList.View()
49		case overlayModel:
50			switch {
51			case m.modelsLoading:
52				return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("loading models…")
53			case m.modelsErr != nil:
54				return m.st.warn.Render("⚠ " + m.modelsErr.Error())
55			case len(m.cachedModels) == 0:
56				return m.st.warn.Render("no models available (is the provider up?)")
57			default:
58				return m.modelList.View()
59			}
60		case overlaySuggest:
61			return m.suggestList.View()
62		case overlayPreset:
63			return m.presetList.View()
64		}
65		return ""
66	}
67	
68	func (m Model) overlayFooter() string {
69		hint := "↵ select  esc close  ^c quit"
70		switch m.overlay {
71		case overlayHistory:
72			hint = "↵ recall  esc close  ^c quit"
73		case overlaySuggest:
74			hint = "↵ look up  esc close  ^c quit"
75		}
76		return m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.st.dim.Render(hint))
77	}
78	
79	// altView wraps content in a full-screen (alt-screen) tea.View with mouse on.
80	func altView(content string) tea.View {
81		v := tea.NewView(content)
82		v.AltScreen = true
83		v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
84		return v
85	}
86	
87	// statusLine renders the footer with the current transient state.
88	func (m Model) statusLine() string { return m.footerContent(false) }
89	
90	// footerHeight measures how many rows the footer occupies at the current width.
91	// It reserves the widest transient state ("translating") so the layout never
92	// jumps when that segment appears mid-flight.
93	func (m Model) footerHeight() int {
94		if m.width <= 0 {
95			return 1
96		}
97		return lg.Height(m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.footerContent(true)))
98	}
99	
100	// footerContent builds the footer string. forceState reserves the transient
101	// "translating" segment (for height measurement); otherwise the actual state is
102	// shown. lipgloss word-wraps it to the width, so narrow terminals get 2+ rows.
103	func (m Model) footerContent(forceState bool) string {
104		live := m.st.liveOff.Render("live○")
105		if m.live {
106			live = m.st.liveOn.Render("live●")
107		}
108	
109		var state string
110		if forceState {
111			state = m.st.dim.Render("⠿ translating")
112		} else if m.flash != "" {
113			state = m.st.liveOn.Render(m.flash)
114		} else {
115			switch m.status {
116			case statusTranslating:
117				state = m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("translating")
118			case statusError:
119				state = m.st.errText.Render("error")
120			}
121		}
122	
123		// Dictionary mode: no source→target pair (English-only lookups per script).
124		if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
125			left := strings.Join([]string{
126				m.st.label.Render("dictionary (zh↔en)"),
127				live,
128			}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
129			if state != "" {
130				left += "  " + state
131			}
132			return left + "  " + m.st.dim.Render("↵ define  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
133		}
134	
135		pair := fmt.Sprintf("%s→%s", lang.Name(m.source), lang.Name(m.target))
136		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
137			pair = fmt.Sprintf("pair %s⇄%s", m.target, m.pairWith) // compact codes
138		}
139	
140		// Engine segment: the selected engine name, plus the model that actually
141		// served the last result (once known).
142		engineSeg := m.active().Name
143		if m.curEngine != "" && m.curEngine != m.active().Name {
144			engineSeg = m.curEngine // chain fell back to a specific engine
145		}
146		if m.curModel != "" {
147			engineSeg = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", engineSeg, m.curModel)
148		}
149	
150		left := strings.Join([]string{
151			m.st.label.Render(pair),
152			m.st.dim.Render(engineSeg),
153			m.st.dim.Render("style:" + m.preset),
154			live,
155		}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
156		if state != "" {
157			left += "  " + state
158		}
159	
160		help := m.st.dim.Render("↵ translate  ^y copy  ^l live  ^e engine  ^t lang  ^p model  ^o style  ^g pair  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
161		return left + "  " + help
162	}
163	
164	// renderResult formats a completed translation, a dictionary entry, or a ranked
165	// "did you mean" list when a dictionary lookup missed.
166	func (m Model) renderResult(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
167		if res.Dictionary != nil {
168			return m.renderDictionary(res)
169		}
170		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
171			return m.renderSuggestions(res)
172		}
173		var b strings.Builder
174		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(res.Translation))
175	
176		if res.DetectedSource != "" && (m.source == "" || m.source == "auto") {
177			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("detected: "+lang.Name(res.DetectedSource)))
178		}
179		for _, a := range res.Alternatives {
180			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("~ "+a))
181		}
182		if res.Notes != "" {
183			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+res.Notes))
184		}
185		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
186			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("⚠ "+w))
187		}
188		// A truncation warning already tells the user to press Enter to retry; the
189		// engine-switch hint only fits chain-fallback warnings, so skip it there.
190		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && !res.Truncated {
191			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("(^e switch engine · check the model/provider)"))
192		}
193		return b.String()
194	}
195	
196	// renderDictionary formats a dictionary lookup for the result pane.
197	func (m Model) renderDictionary(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
198		d := res.Dictionary
199		var b strings.Builder
200		head := d.Word
201		if d.Phonetic != "" {
202			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
203		}
204		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(head))
205		for _, mn := range d.Meanings {
206			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render(mn.PartOfSpeech))
207			for i, def := range mn.Definitions {
208				if i >= 3 {
209					break
210				}
211				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("• "+def.Text))
212			}
213		}
214		return b.String()
215	}
216	
217	// renderSuggestions renders the ranked "did you mean" list for a dictionary miss.
218	func (m Model) renderSuggestions(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
219		var b strings.Builder
220		b.WriteString(m.st.dim.Render("no exact match — did you mean:"))
221		for i, w := range res.Suggestions {
222			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d.", i+1)) + " " + m.st.alt.Render(w))
223		}
224		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("↵ choose"))
225		return b.String()
226	}
227
```

```go
package tts

import "errors"

// Sentinel errors returned by the TTS layer.
var (
	// ErrNoBackend is returned when no configured speech backend is available
	// (no native TTS binary and no audio player for the online fallback).
	ErrNoBackend = errors.New("tts: no speech backend available")
	// ErrNoPlayer is returned when the online backend produced audio but no
	// audio player could be found to play it.
	ErrNoPlayer = errors.New("tts: no audio player found")
	// ErrEmptyText is returned when there is nothing to speak.
	ErrEmptyText = errors.New("tts: empty text")
)
```

```go
package tts

import (
	"context"
	"io"
	"os/exec"
	"strings"
)

// runner abstracts binary discovery and process execution so tests can inject a
// fake and never spawn a real process. execRunner is the production implementation.
//
// This is the only place the package shells out; keeping it behind an interface
// is what makes every per-OS argv path unit-testable from a single `go test`.
type runner interface {
	// look resolves name in PATH, returning its path or an error when absent.
	look(name string) (string, error)
	// run executes name with args, feeding stdin on the child's standard input
	// when non-empty, and blocks until it exits. It uses exec.CommandContext, so
	// cancelling ctx kills the child process (mid-playback for players).
	run(ctx context.Context, name, stdin string, args ...string) error
}

type execRunner struct{}

func (execRunner) look(name string) (string, error) { return exec.LookPath(name) }

func (execRunner) run(ctx context.Context, name, stdin string, args ...string) error {
	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, name, args...)
	if stdin != "" {
		cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(stdin)
	}
	cmd.Stdout = io.Discard
	cmd.Stderr = io.Discard
	return cmd.Run()
}

```

```go
package tts

import (
	"strings"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
)

// sayVoices maps a language code to a macOS `say` voice. Region-specific codes
// (zh-TW) are consulted before the base code (zh). "" lets `say` pick its default.
var sayVoices = map[string]string{
	"en":    "Samantha",
	"zh":    "Tingting",
	"zh-cn": "Tingting",
	"zh-tw": "Meijia",
	"zh-hk": "Sinji",
	"ja":    "Kyoko",
	"ko":    "Yuna",
	"fr":    "Thomas",
	"es":    "Monica",
	"de":    "Anna",
	"it":    "Alice",
	"ru":    "Milena",
	"pt":    "Luciana",
	"nl":    "Xander",
}

// espeakVoices maps a language code to an espeak-ng voice/language name.
var espeakVoices = map[string]string{
	"zh":    "cmn",
	"zh-cn": "cmn",
	"zh-tw": "cmn",
	"zh-hk": "yue",
	"ja":    "ja",
	"ko":    "ko",
}

// normKey lower-cases and trims a language code for map lookups.
func normKey(code string) string { return strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(code)) }

// lookupVoice resolves code against an override map first, then a built-in map,
// trying the exact code before the base code. Returns "" when nothing matches.
func lookupVoice(builtin, override map[string]string, code string) string {
	k := normKey(code)
	base := lang.Base(k)
	for _, m := range []map[string]string{override, builtin} {
		if m == nil {
			continue
		}
		if v, ok := m[k]; ok {
			return v
		}
		if base != k {
			if v, ok := m[base]; ok {
				return v
			}
		}
	}
	return ""
}

// sayVoice returns the macOS `say` voice for code (override wins), or "" for the
// system default.
func sayVoice(override map[string]string, code string) string {
	return lookupVoice(sayVoices, override, code)
}

// espeakVoice returns the espeak-ng voice/language for code, falling back to the
// base code so espeak still attempts a matching language.
func espeakVoice(override map[string]string, code string) string {
	if v := lookupVoice(espeakVoices, override, code); v != "" {
		return v
	}
	return lang.Base(normKey(code))
}

// googleTL normalizes a language code to a Google translate_tts `tl` value.
func googleTL(code string) string {
	switch normKey(code) {
	case "zh", "zh-cn":
		return "zh-CN"
	case "zh-tw", "zh-hk":
		return "zh-TW"
	}
	if b := lang.Base(normKey(code)); b != "" {
		return b
	}
	return "en"
}
```
